records.title: Continuous positive airway pressure for early pulmonary atelectasis in infants with cystic fibrosis
records.summary: Background: Pulmonary atelectasis may represent an early radiological manifestation of lung disease in infants with cystic fibrosis (CF). In early life, therapeutic options are limited, and supportive respiratory strategies may play a role in preventing progression of lung abnormalities. Methods: Since 2024 in our CF Centre, infants underwent to low-dose chest CT at 3-6 months of age. Infants with CT evidence of pulmonary atelectasis were admitted to our hospital for bronchoalveolar lavage followed by empirical intravenous antibiotic therapy adjusted by microbiological data. Prophylactic continuous positive airway pressure therapy (CPAP) was initiated in hospital setting in infants with body weight ≥5 kg. CPAP...
secondary_themes: Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
records.why_it_matters: Early CPAP is presented as a feasible supportive option for infant atelectasis before modulator eligibility.
records.confidence_reason: Structured sections describe infant imaging, intervention, follow-up, and clinical response.

records.title: Evolving physiotherapy challenges through a lung cancer journey in a woman with cystic fibrosis
records.summary: 66 year old woman with Cystic Fibrosis (CF) diagnosed 2002 aged 44 via cascade screening. She had a 40 pack year smoking history, stopped March 2023. History of emphysema, osteopenia, depression, anxiety, agoraphobia, breathing pattern disorder (BPD) and chronic pain. Started Elexacaftor/Tezacaftor/Ivacaftor October 2021; FEV1 improved from 1.43L (67%) to 1.59L (74%), FVC 2.24 L (83%) to 2.53 (97%). October 2024 she presented with haemoptysis, shortness of breath, increased cough and sputum. Chest x-ray showed slight left lower lobe collapse, resolved with intravenous antibiotics, physiotherapy and tranexamic acid, but then recurred 6 weeks later. High resolution CT scan showed left lower lobe collapse suspici...
secondary_themes: Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
Care burden, adherence, and psychosocial impact
records.why_it_matters: A complex adult cancer pathway illustrates how CF physiotherapy must adapt around comorbidity, symptoms, and changing respiratory risk.
records.confidence_reason: Summary gives a detailed case context, but structured sections are absent.

records.title: ExACT comparison in cystic fibrosis: endurance training VS HIIT Circuit. Single case
records.summary: Background: Exercise as an Airway Clearance Technique (ExACT) is an exercise-based airway clearance program that includes at least 20 minutes of aerobic exercise at an intensity sufficient to induce deep breathing, combined with breath-control techniques, huffing, and coughing. Objective: To compare two aerobic exercise modalities used as substitutes for traditional airway clearance techniques (ACT), assessing their ability to maintain clinical stability and to determine whether one provides superior clinical benefit. Methods: A 23-year-old man with CF, undergoing elexacaftor–tezacaftor–ivacaftor (ETI) treatment for 3 years, stable clinical condition for at least 3 months prior to starting the study, poor expe...
secondary_themes: Care burden, adherence, and psychosocial impact
records.why_it_matters: Exercise-based airway clearance may substitute for some traditional ACT sessions while maintaining stability and adherence.
records.confidence_reason: Structured abstract reports intervention design, repeated outcomes, and adherence data.

records.title: Functional and respiratory improvement through physiotherapy: optimizing exercise response, airway clearance, and quality of life
records.summary: Background: People with Cystic Fibrosis (PwCF) struggle with exercise intolerance and dyspnea, which can gradually limit participation in daily activities. Beyond the physiological burden, repeated episodes of breathlessness may also generate fear or avoidance of physiotherapy, particularly airway clearance techniques. In this context, supervised and individualized physiotherapy may play a role not only in improving physical performance but also in rebuilding confidence and treatment adherence. Case Description: We report the case of a 9-year-old boy with CF who presented with exercise-related dyspnea, increased oxygen requirements during physical activity, and marked discomfort during airway clearance. These...
secondary_themes: Care burden, adherence, and psychosocial impact
Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
records.why_it_matters: The case shows functional gains from individualized physiotherapy even when distance walked changes little.
records.confidence_reason: Detailed case narrative includes intervention components and pre/post functional outcomes.

records.title: Physiotherapy as a key driver of adherence in an adolescent with cystic fibrosis: a case presentation
records.summary: Adolescence is a well-known risk period for reduced adherence to cystic fibrosis (CF) therapies. Despite CFTR modulator therapy, effective airway clearance and inhaled antibiotics remain essential to prevent pulmonary exacerbations and preserve lung function. Physiotherapists support adolescents and their families in maintaining adherence during this complex period. We present a 14-year-old female with CF (ΔF508 heterozygous), diagnosed at the age of 2 years, with intermittent disease severity (Leeds criteria). Triple combination CFTR modulator therapy was initiated in January 2024. She lives with foster parents and a foster sibling, with limited socioeconomic and educational resources, living in a home with s...
records.primary_theme: Care burden, adherence, and psychosocial impact
secondary_themes: Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
records.why_it_matters: Adolescent adherence remains fragile despite modulators, especially when social context complicates airway clearance and inhaled therapy.
records.confidence_reason: The summary identifies the adherence problem and patient context, but full structured evidence is unavailable.

records.title: Physiotherapy management of prolonged admission required for Mycobacterium abscessus eradication
records.summary: We present two cases of prolonged admission in patients with Cystic Fibrosis colonised with Mycobacterium Abscessus. Patient A presented with multiple episodes of haemoptysis, one large volume requiring embolisation. He previously failed NTM eradication, required multiple courses of intravenous (IV) antibiotics, more recently commencing IV bacteriophage therapy. Admission lasted 18 months. Patient B presented unwell with a background history of NTM, on a maintenance oral regime. NTM pneumonias were discovered on CT, requiring eradication therapy. Admission lasted 17 weeks. Physiotherapy management involved increased treatment time, airway clearance modification to maximise secretion clearance, minimising airwa...
secondary_themes: Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
records.why_it_matters: Prolonged M. abscessus eradication admissions create major physiotherapy and airway-clearance demands.
records.confidence_reason: Summary gives two cases and management themes, but lacks full structured methods and outcomes.

records.title: A one-year follow-up of a patient with cystic fibrosis receiving home non-invasive ventilation in Kazakhstan
records.summary: Background: Progressive decline in pulmonary function in CF is accompanied by the development of chronic respiratory failure. Long-term non-invasive ventilation (NIV) helps correct hypercapnia, improve oxygenation, and can be used as a component of comprehensive respiratory support. Objective: To present the first one-year follow-up of a CF patient and hypercapnic respiratory failure receiving long-term home NIV. Materials and Methods: A 19-year-old male CF patient was observed. He received low-flow oxygen therapy for 6 months without clinical benefit. Due to the progression of respiratory failure, long-term home NIV was initiated. Assessments were performed at baseline and after 6 and 12 months and included g...
secondary_themes: Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
records.why_it_matters: Home NIV may stabilize gas exchange, weight, and quality of life in advanced CF respiratory failure where local experience is limited.
records.confidence_reason: Structured abstract provides baseline, six-month, and twelve-month clinical outcomes.

records.title: Initiation of CFTR modulator therapy in the first 2 years of life: real-world outcomes with elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor
records.summary: Background: Advances in newborn screening and supportive care have significantly improved early cystic fibrosis (CF) outcomes. Early use of highly effective CFTR modulators is an emerging strategy, but data on elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor (ETI) in children under 2 years remain scarce. We report early outcomes of ETI in infants included in the MODUL-CF study (NCT04301856). Methods: The initiation of ETI was decided after a multidisciplinary discussion in patients with severe cystic fibrosis. Follow-up included clinical assessment, sweat testing, microbiology, laboratory tests, CFTR modulator plasma levels, and chest CT. Age- and weight-based ETI dosing were extrapolated from ivacaftor monotherapy recommenda...
records.why_it_matters: ETI use before age two may change early lung and pancreatic trajectories in severe infant CF.

records.title: Organoid testing to implement theratyping for CFTR modulator therapy
records.summary: Objectives: The successful history of CFTR modulator therapy in people with cystic fibrosis (pwCF) began in 2012 with the corrector ivacaftor. Thirteen years later, two highly effective CFTR modulator therapies are already available in Europe for the treatment of non-class I mutations. The extent to which the CFTR modulator therapy response using patient-derived intestinal organoids (PDIOs) correlates with the clinical response is of great importance, as theratyping could provide a basis for deciding between one CFTR modulator or another. Methods: Human intestinal organoids were established from biopsy specimens by standard methods. Human colonic and duodenal biopsy specimens were obtained from CF-patients and...
records.primary_theme: Precision CFTR biology and rare variants
records.why_it_matters: Patient-derived organoids may support theratyping and choice between available triple modulators.
records.confidence_reason: Structured abstract describes organoid methods, donor testing, and comparative modulator response.

records.title: Real-world experience of switching from elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor to vanzacaftor/tezacaftor/deutivacaftor in a UK adult cystic fibrosis centre
records.summary: Objectives: To evaluate decision-making, clinical outcomes and tolerability of switching from elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor (ETI) to vanzacaftor/tezacaftor/deutivacaftor (VTD) in a UK adult cystic fibrosis centre. Methods: Eligible patients were offered to switch to VTD following discussion of benefits and risks. Demographics, decisions, clinical parameters (weight, FEV₁, FVC, liver function) and side effects were recorded. Baseline and three-month data were compared using paired t-tests or Wilcoxon signed-rank tests. Results: Of 159 patients on ETI, 78 chose to switch, 69 declined and 12 were undecided. No significant difference in age (p=0.27) or baseline FEV₁ %predicted (p=0.69) existed between those who...
secondary_themes: Care burden, adherence, and psychosocial impact

records.title: Dose reduction of elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor in children with cystic fibrosis: clinical outcomes and adverse event resolution
records.summary: Objective: Elexacaftor-tezacaftor-ivacaftor (ETI) has transformed outcomes for people with cystic fibrosis (pwCF), but adverse events (AEs) may require dose modifications. Limited evidence exists to guide dose reduction strategies in paediatric patients. To evaluate the clinical outcomes and AE resolution following ETI dose reduction in children and adolescents with CF. Methods: This multicentre, retrospective observational study included pwCF aged 6-18 years from seven Italian CF centres who underwent ETI dose reduction due to drug-related AEs between August 1, 2021 and December 31, 2024. records.title: Pancreatic function in preschool children with cystic fibrosis on elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor
records.summary: Background: CFTR-modulator therapy improved the clinical outcome of people with cystic fibrosis (pwCF) remarkably. So far, little is known on pancreatic function recovery especially in children. Individual case descriptions indicate a recovery of the pancreatic function in young children with early initiation of elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor (ETI) therapy. We therefore aimed to systematically evaluate the pancreatic function in preschool children with ETI treatment. Methods: In a multicenter manner four large CF centers in Germany collected stool samples from newly ETI eligible children aged 2-5 years prior and after 3 and 12 months of ETI therapy. Fecal-elastase (< 200µ/g defines pancreatic insuffenciency)...
records.why_it_matters: Preschool ETI may partially restore pancreatic function, making fecal elastase monitoring clinically relevant.

records.summary: Objectives: Cystic fibrosis (CF) is a multisystem genetic disease leading to defective chloride transport. Dose reduction is a potential strategy to improve tolerability while maintaining efficacy; however, there is limited data for paediatric populations. records.title: Real-life experience with elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor in children with cystic fibrosis: dose reduction for adverse events
records.summary: Objective: Cystic fibrosis (CF) is a multisystem, genetic disease caused by mutations in the CFTR gene. records.summary: Objectives: Clinical response to ETI among eligible people with cystic fibrosis (pwCF) is heterogeneous. records.title: Model-informed dose reduction of elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor therapy in adult people with cystic fibrosis: a simulation study
records.summary: Background: Elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor (ETI) therapy is used by most people with cystic fibrosis (pwCF). While generally efficacious, some patients experience adverse events. This is typically managed by empirical dose reduction, but the optimal regimen remains unknown. We evaluated the pharmacokinetic (PK) and pharmacodynamic (PD) impact of ETI dose reduction using model-based simulations. Methods: Available ETI population PK models were used to simulate ETI PK under five dose reduction strategies. First, standard dose ETI PK was simulated, after which individuals were selected for dose reduction based on elexacaftor trough levels (Cmin). Subsequently, ETI exposure was translated to predicted efficacy u...
records.why_it_matters: PK/PD simulation may guide safer ETI dose reduction strategies for adults with high drug exposure.
records.confidence_reason: Structured abstract details registry-based simulation, dose strategies, exposure-response modeling, and conclusions.

records.title: Rationale and design of a Phase 2b study in people with cystic fibrosis carrying the 3849 +10 Kb C->T mutation to evaluate the combined effect of SPL84 and CFTR modulators
records.summary: Background: SPL84 is an inhaled antisense oligonucleotide (ASO) for the treatment of pwCF carrying the 3849 +10kb C->T (3849) mutation. SPL84 was shown to fully restore CFTR function in HNEs and HBEs from pwCF. In addition, SPL84 and ETI had a comparable and additive effect on HBEs from 3849/F508del pwCF. SPL84-002 is a global Phase 2a study in 3849 pwCF to evaluate the safety and preliminary efficacy of SPL84 as mono therapy. Twelve participants received 9 weekly doses of 25 or 50 mg SPL84 (n=6 each). SPL84 lead to a clinically meaningful improvement in ppFEV1 in up to ~70% of participants per cohort, with an average absolute change from baseline in ppFEV1 of 5 for both cohorts. Real-world data shows that ETI...
records.primary_theme: Emerging genetic, RNA, and cell-model therapies
secondary_themes: Precision CFTR biology and rare variants
records.why_it_matters: An inhaled antisense therapy may add benefit for 3849+10kb C-to-T patients already on modulators.
records.confidence_reason: Partial structured abstract describes mechanism, prior clinical signal, and planned phase 2b combination design.

records.title: Standard Aggressive Chemotharapy versus capecitabine in pancreatic adenocarcinoma with lung metastasis in cystic fibrosis patients
records.summary: Objectives: This study investigated the impact of standard combination chemotherapy versus single-agent therapy in metastatic pancreatic adenocarcinoma in cystic fibrosis patients. The aim was to compare objective response rate, toxicity, progression-free survival, and overall survival. Methods: Involved 35 CF patients with newly diagnosed metastatic pancreatic adenocarcinoma, maintained on CFTR modulators, bronchodilators, dornase alfa, and chest physiotherapy. 18 patients received standard chemotherapy (combination of Folinic acid, Fluorouracil, Irinotecan, Oxaliplatin), and 17 received single-agent chemotherapy (Capecitabine). TNF-α levels were measured before and after six cycles to monitor CF activity. Re...
secondary_themes: Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
records.why_it_matters: Pancreatic cancer treatment in CF needs evidence on efficacy and toxicity within complex baseline lung disease.
records.confidence_reason: Structured abstract includes comparative outcomes, but oncology does not map cleanly to the controlled domains.

records.title: Tolerability and efficacy of rheumatoid arthritis medications in cystic fibrosis patients with concurrent rheumatoid arthritis
records.summary: Objectives: Tolerability and toxicity of RA medications on pulmonary CF patients. Monitor the levels of CRP and serum Calprotectin for RA treatment response. Evaluate serum IL-17 levels (as a biomarker for both CF and RA). Methods: 28 CF patients with recent-onset RA. All CF patients were managed either with CFTR modulators (for those with at least one F508del mutation) or standard treatments such as Dornase alfa, bronchodilators, and chest physiotherapy. Patients received treatment with Hydroxychloroquine, either alone or in combination with Sulfasalazine. Results: 24 patients completed the six-month study; four discontinued due to non-pulmonary adverse events (gastrointestinal upset, skin rash). Importantly,...
records.primary_theme: Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
records.why_it_matters: Managing rheumatoid arthritis in CF requires safety evidence for anti-rheumatic drugs alongside pulmonary care.
records.confidence_reason: Structured abstract provides a cohort and biomarker outcomes, but the topic is outside most controlled CF domains.

records.title: Beyond CFTR modulators: a randomized controlled trial of intensified vitamin D supplementation to improve bone health in paediatric cystic fibrosis
records.summary: Objectives: To evaluate the efficacy of an intensified vitamin D supplementation strategy to achieve and sustain serum vitamin D (vitD) levels >30 ng/mL in pediatric patients with cystic fibrosis (CF). Secondary objectives included assessment of bone health and evaluation of safety and tolerability in the context of optimized standard CF care beyond CFTR modulator therapies. Methods: Double-blind, randomized controlled trial including children and adolescents aged 5–18 years with CF followed at a tertiary multidisciplinary CF center. Participants with baseline serum vitD levels between 10 and 30 ng/mL were randomly assigned (1:1) to receive either an intensified supplementation regimen with a fixed initial dos...
secondary_themes: Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
records.why_it_matters: Intensified vitamin D dosing may improve pediatric CF bone health beyond standard supplementation.
records.confidence_reason: Structured abstract reports randomized blinded design, supplementation protocol, vitamin D and bone outcomes.

records.title: Plasma lipidome after double-blind randomized lipid supplementation for 1 year in patients with cystic fibrosis
records.summary: Background: Cystic fibrosis (CF) patients with pancreatic insufficiency, pathological sweat tests, and pulmonary symptoms have long been known to develop linoleic acid (LA) deficiency, documented for over 60 years. Abnormal profiles of other fatty acids, such as palmitoleic acid, Mead acid, and often docosahexaenoic acid (DHA), are also common. Objectives: This randomized, double-blind study investigated the effects of LA supplementation in children with CF. Patients aged 5–15 years, carrying class I–II mutations and classical CF symptoms, were assigned to receive either LA or oleic acid (10 g/day) for one year. All participants also received DHA (400 mg/day). Forty patients completed the study. Standard CF tr...
records.why_it_matters: Lipidomic responses to fatty-acid supplementation may reveal metabolic effects not captured by standard nutrition measures.
records.confidence_reason: Structured abstract reports randomized double-blind supplementation, pediatric cohort, and lipidomic analyses.

records.title: The CARMUCI study design: a double-blind, cross-over sham-controlled trial of indoor air purification in people with cystic fibrosis and primary ciliary dyskinesia
records.summary: Background: People with cystic fibrosis (pwCF) and primary ciliary dyskinesia (pwPCD) are particularly vulnerable to the harmful effects of air pollution due to the impairment of mucociliary clearance (MCC). Despite growing evidence supporting the use of indoor air purification in common lung conditions, its role in CF and PCD remains unexplored. Methods: The Clean Air for Rare MUcociliary Clearance dIsorders (CARMUCI) study is a randomized, double-blind, cross-over, sham-controlled trial aiming to evaluate the impact of indoor air purification on respiratory health of pwCF and pwPCD (children and adults) in Cyprus. Sixty-two participants will receive two 3-month intervention periods using identical-looking ai...
secondary_themes: Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
records.why_it_matters: Indoor air purification is being tested as an environmental intervention for impaired mucociliary clearance disorders.
records.confidence_reason: Structured abstract gives trial design and enrollment status, but results are not yet available.

records.title: CF-Pain study: pain in patients with cystic fibrosis is a relevant and often underestimated issue regardless of age
records.summary: Objectives: Pain is a common and often underestimated problem. Self-medication has a risk of adverse drug reaction and interactions. Most studies to date have been conducted without validated questionnaires. There is few information about pain in children and adolescents. The aim of the ongoing CF Pain study is to develop and review a standardized pain questionnaire for people with CF (pwCF) and started in 2022. Methods: We use a pain questionnaire and interview children and adolescents aged 6 years and older and caregiver at the same time or adults with CF by phone. PwCF were treated in three locations of the Christiane-Herzog-Centre Ruhr. Additionally the first 10 patients of each age group were asked for fe...
records.primary_theme: Care burden, adherence, and psychosocial impact
secondary_themes: Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
records.why_it_matters: Pain is common across ages in CF and may limit social participation and activity while remaining under-recognized.
records.confidence_reason: Structured abstract reports questionnaire/interview approach, age groups, pain prevalence, and limitations.

records.title: Body composition as a determinant of exercise capacity in people with cystic fibrosis
records.summary: Objectives: To investigate the relationship between body composition parameters, VO₂peak, and peak workload (Wpeak) in people with cystic fibrosis (pwCF). Methods: Forty-seven pwCF (28 female/19 male; mean age 17.7 ± 10.7 years, range 10–49 years; mean ppFEV₁ 85.0 ± 17.9, range 40–114%) participated. Cycle ergometry was performed to determine VO₂peak and Wpeak. Body composition was assessed using bioelectrical impedance analysis (BIA), including fat mass (FM), fat mass index (FMI), fat-free mass (FFM), and fat-free mass index (FFMI). Data were collected during a four-week inpatient rehabilitation program. Results: Exercise capacity was VO₂peak at 78.6 ± 17.1% predicted and Wpeak at 82.7 ± 16.6% predicted. Afte...
records.why_it_matters: Fat-free mass may help interpret exercise capacity beyond lung function alone in CF rehabilitation settings.
records.confidence_reason: Structured abstract reports cohort, body-composition measurement, exercise testing, and adjusted correlations.

records.title: Supporting world class innovative research – how the UK Clinical Trials Accelerator Platform (CTAP) business development strategy is advancing new treatments for cystic fibrosis
records.summary: The aim of business development activity at Cystic Fibrosis Trust is to attract global sponsors and increase the number of commercial clinical trials aimed at improving the health of people with CF. It is guided by the Trust’s four research goals and forms part of a global collaboration of accelerating CF research alongside the European Cystic Fibrosis Society and the Cystic Fibrosis Foundation. The Trust’s business development activity includes horizon scanning and monitoring the global CF pipeline. The objective is to work with global sponsors to maintain a broad and dynamic portfolio of CF research which covers a range of therapeutic areas and different trial phases. The aim is to initiate conversations wit...
records.primary_theme: Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
secondary_themes: Emerging genetic, RNA, and cell-model therapies
records.why_it_matters: CTAP business development aims to expand the CF trial portfolio and attract sponsors for new treatments.
records.confidence_reason: Summary describes programme aims and strategy, but no structured results are provided.

records.title: Exploring perspectives of patients receiving outpatient palliative care alongside usual CF treatment: A qualitative study within a Randomized Controlled Trial
records.summary: Objectives: Many people with cystic fibrosis have significant unmet physical, emotional, and care planning needs. Prior work has indicated that palliative care may improve a range of outcomes for people with CF. The present RCT compared the addition of specialty palliative care to outpatient CF clinic care vs usual care at five North American sites. Participants received four outpatient telehealth palliative care visits over 12 months, with visits focusing on needs assessment, symptom management, and advanced care planning. Methods: We conducted semi-structured interviews with 48 participants. Interview questions centered on general experiences in the study, perceptions of the role of palliative care, and thei...
records.primary_theme: Care burden, adherence, and psychosocial impact
secondary_themes: Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
records.why_it_matters: Outpatient palliative care may address symptoms, stress, care coordination, and planning needs alongside usual CF treatment.
records.confidence_reason: Structured abstract reports RCT context, qualitative interviews, participant count, and thematic benefits.

records.title: Exploring the experiences of healthcare professionals recruiting infants with cystic fibrosis into research: a survey based study
records.summary: Objectives: Research in infants with CF (iwCF) is imperative to understand pathophysiology in the early months and to ensure safe, effective treatments can be started early. There are several observational and interventional studies active in iwCF, however infant research poses practical and ethical challenges. We aimed to understand the experiences of research active healthcare professionals (HCPs) to support recruitment of iwCF to studies. Methods: An anonymous survey was distributed via Qualtrics to UK-based HCPs with experience recruiting iwCF to research Apr-May ‘25. Closed & open-ended questions were organised around five themes. Results: Respondents (n=23) highlighted the importance of navigating recrui...
records.primary_theme: Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
secondary_themes: Care burden, adherence, and psychosocial impact
records.why_it_matters: Improving staff training and team communication may support ethical, feasible infant CF research recruitment.
records.confidence_reason: Structured abstract reports survey design, respondent count, barriers, facilitators, and recommendations.

records.title: Eliciting clinical trial estimands for cystic fibrosis: a Bayesian framework
records.summary: Objectives: Since its introduction in the ICH E9 (R1) guideline, estimands have been considered an important element in designing clinical trials. While the use of classical and well-known estimands is straightforward, the building of new ones lacks detailed methodological guidance, and this may be attributable to the limited usage of the estimand framework in more complex clinical trials. In our study, we propose a framework for constructing estimands using expert elicitation and Bayesian statistical models. Methods: We designed a two-stage elicitation process to obtain expert opinions on organoids and on estimand attributes under prespecified scenarios. Using the data, we applied a series of hierarchical joi...
records.primary_theme: Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
secondary_themes: Emerging genetic, RNA, and cell-model therapies
records.why_it_matters: A Bayesian elicitation framework could make complex CF trial endpoints and intercurrent-event assumptions more explicit for novel therapies.
records.confidence_reason: Structured abstract describes the framework, expert elicitation sample, selected endpoints, and intended trial-design use.

records.title: Reduce and reform: reducing bureaucracy in clinical trials: European guidance
records.summary: Excessive bureaucracy in clinical trials burdens investigators and limits patient access to innovative treatments. The ECFS is part of the cross-disciplinary Coalition for Reducing Bureaucracy in Clinical Trials which was founded in 2020 by medical societies and patient advocates. The coalition developed new EU-level recommendations to streamline regulatory processes, improve safety reporting and make informed consent more patient-centred. These include: A: Regulatory: 1. harmonising clinical trial processes across the EU: streamlining the EU clinical trial assessment framework (coordinating the Reporting Member State training); aligning informed consent form requirements; establishing a European fast-track me...
records.primary_theme: Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
records.why_it_matters: Reducing research bureaucracy may improve the feasibility and reach of CF clinical trials across Europe.
records.confidence_reason: Only programme metadata and title are available, so the classification is inferred from the guidance topic.

records.title: Access for all – how CTAP is ensuring everyone with CF in the UK has opportunities to take part in research
records.summary: With support from the Cystic Fibrosis Foundation Cystic Fibrosis Trust launched the Clinical Trials Accelerator Platform (CTAP) in 2017. In collaboration with the European Cystic Fibrosis Society’s Clinical Trials Network CTAP has built a nationwide network consisting of 25 Cystic Fibrosis (CF) centres with 26 trial coordinators, providing a robust infrastructure and resourcing to increase capacity for CF clinical research across the UK. CTAP has developed an efficient and collaborative national recruitment model that advocates collaboration between all UK CF centres to broaden access of CF clinical research to the UK CF community. Several projects have been facilitated by CTAP to support this model: Champions...
records.primary_theme: Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
records.why_it_matters: The CTAP access model signals an effort to broaden research participation opportunities for people with CF.
records.confidence_reason: No abstract text is present; the theme rests on title and programme metadata only.

records.title: Discontinuing therapy in children with cystic fibrosis on CFTR modulators
records.summary: Background: The introduction of highly effective cystic fibrosis transmembrane conductance regulator (CFTR) modulators has shifted the treatment paradigm of cystic fibrosis (CF). Reducing treatment burden is now a priority for patients/caregivers and healthcare professionals.However, guidance for CF therapy stepdown is not standardized. We aimed to evaluate the impact of dornase alfa (DA) discontinuation on pulmonary function, quality of life (QoL), and the frequency of respiratory exacerbations in clinically stable paediatric CF patients receiving CFTR modulators. Methods: Longitudinal observational study conducted at a CF reference centre. Fifty-four children with CF were included; 29 clinically stable patie...
secondary_themes: Care burden, adherence, and psychosocial impact
records.why_it_matters: Dornase alfa stepdown in stable children on modulators could reduce treatment burden without short-term loss of lung function or quality of life.
records.confidence_reason: Structured longitudinal abstract reports cohort size, discontinuation approach, spirometry, QoL, and exacerbation outcomes.

records.title: Sustained improvement in lung function up to 36 months after elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor initiation: a real-world longitudinal study
records.summary: Background: Elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor (ETI) leads to rapid improvements in lung function in people with cystic fibrosis (pwCF). However, real-world data on the long-term trajectory and durability of lung function response beyond the first year remain limited. Objectives: To evaluate the longitudinal trajectory of percent predicted forced expiratory volume in one second (ppFEV1) up to 36 months after ETI initiation in routine clinical practice. Methods: We conducted a retrospective observational study including pwCF who initiated ETI at a single CF center and completed at least 12 months of treatment. Lung function was assessed at baseline and at 12, 18, 24, and 36 months. Changes in ppFEV1 were analyzed...
records.why_it_matters: Three-year real-world ETI follow-up supports durability of lung-function gains and reductions in severe disease markers.
records.confidence_reason: Structured abstract provides baseline characteristics, serial ppFEV1 results, and clinical secondary outcomes.

records.title: Changes in Forced Expiratory Volume in 1 Second in patients with cystic fibrosis on elexacaftor /tezacaftor/ivacaftor since 2020. Data from a UK Cystic Fibrosis centre
records.summary: Background: Elexacaftor/Tezacaftor /Ivacaftor (ETI), a Cystic Fibrosis Transmembrane Conductance Regulator modulator therapy for Cystic Fibrosis (CF), gained European authorisation in August 2020 and was available on the National Health Service in England shortly after. At the Leicester Adult CF Unit our first patients commenced ETI in March 2020 on a managed access scheme. Here we present data from our centre on Forced Expiratory Volume in 1 Second (FEV₁) for patients who commenced ETI in 2020. Inclusion: 72 modulator naïve patients commenced ETI in 2020. All values are presented as change in FEV₁ % predicted and peak is defined as the highest FEV₁ % predicted. Results: Mean change from baseline to peak FEV₁...
records.why_it_matters: Longitudinal center data show that many ETI starters retain benefit but lose some peak FEV1 response, raising follow-up and adherence questions.
records.confidence_reason: Partial abstract gives useful cohort and FEV1 trends, but methods and explanatory factors are limited.

records.title: Beyond spirometry: forced oscillation technique for monitoring lung disease in cystic fibrosis on highly effective CFTR modulator therapy
records.summary: Objectives: Lung disease monitoring in cystic fibrosis (CF) relies on spirometry and body plethysmography. In the era of highly effective CFTR modulator therapy (HEMT), lung function is often preserved, increasing the need for sensitive non-invasive methods to detect subtle airway abnormalities. The forced oscillation technique (FOT) enables assessment of respiratory mechanics during tidal breathing. The aim of this study was to evaluate correlations between FOT parameters and conventional spirometric and plethysmographic indices in adults with CF. Methods: Fifty-five adult CF patients receiving HEMT were included. All participants underwent spirometry, body plethysmography and FOT. FOT parameters included res...
records.why_it_matters: Forced oscillation may detect airway mechanics abnormalities when spirometry is preserved in adults receiving highly effective modulators.
records.confidence_reason: Structured abstract includes adult cohort, testing methods, correlations, and clear monitoring conclusion.

records.title: The real-life efficacy of ETI therapy in young children with cystic fibrosis and at least one F508del allele
records.summary: Background: Elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor (ETI) has proven efficacy in clinical trials in individuals with cystic fibrosis (CF), including the paediatric population. However, real-world data in young children is limited, and the consistency of outcomes with older age groups remains to be established. Objective: This post-approval, retrospective, single-centre study aimed to assess the effects of ETI on CFTR biomarkers and lung function over 6 months of clinical use in Polish paediatric patients. Methods: In CF children aged 2–12 years with at least one F508del allele who initiated ETI therapy between May and November 2025, demographic and clinical characteristics were analysed, including changes from baseli...
records.why_it_matters: Post-approval ETI data in young children show early improvements in sweat chloride and LCI outside trial settings.
records.confidence_reason: Structured abstract reports pediatric eligibility, sample size, biomarker and lung-function changes, and follow-up timing.

records.title: Early response variations and influential factors in children with cystic fibrosis treated with elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor: single center experience
records.summary: Objectives: To evaluate the clinical response to elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor(ETI)in pediatric cystic fibrosis(CF)patients and to identify factors associated with limited or no treatment response in a real-world setting. Methods: This retrospective study analyzed 90pediatric CFpatients treated with ETI for ≥3months. Genotype,sweat chloride(SwCl),pulmonary function(FEV₁),and anthropometric parameters were evaluated. Patients were classified as “responsive/responsive”(R/R) or “responsive/unresponsive”(R/U) based on ETI eligibility.Treatment response was evaluated according to the Canadian Clinical Consensus Guideline for CFTR Modulator Therapies. Results: The mean age was 13.30±4.48 years.At baseline,mean Sw...
secondary_themes: Precision CFTR biology and rare variants
records.why_it_matters: Early ETI response varied by sex, infection status, and genotype, underscoring the need to understand partial responders in children.
records.confidence_reason: Structured abstract gives a 90-child cohort, response metrics, genotype details, and factors linked to response.

records.title: Respiratory outcomes after one year of triple combination modulator therapy elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor in people with cystic fibrosis aged 2 to 5 years
records.summary: Objectives: To evaluate the respiratory impact of elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor (ETI) treatment in people with Cystic Fibrosis (pwCF) aged 2 to 5 years. To describe baseline demographic and clinical characteristics. Methods: single-center, prospective observational study including pwCF initiating ETI, with a year follow-up. Demographic, clinical, and microbiological data were collected. Respiratory cultures, pulmonary exacerbations (PEx), and antibiotic courses during the first year of ETI therapy were compared to the previous year. Statistical analysis was performed using SPSS software. Results: Data were obtained from 18 pwCF, with a median age of 3 years (IQR 2–4), 66.7% male. All were diagnosed via newb...
records.why_it_matters: One-year ETI follow-up in preschool children suggests microbiology may shift even when exacerbation frequency is unchanged.
records.confidence_reason: Structured prospective abstract reports age group, cultures, exacerbations, antibiotics, and sweat chloride changes.

records.title: Two-year real-world effectiveness and safety of elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor in a Colombian cystic fibrosis cohort.
records.summary: Objective: To describe long-term clinical outcomes and safety of ETI in a Colombian cohort of patients with CF. Methods: We conducted a descriptive longitudinal study including patients with CF followed at a CF center (Hospital Infantil Universitario de San José in Bogotá) who completed 24 months of ETI treatment. Anthropometric measures and lung function were assessed quarterly, and liver function tests semiannually. Clinical data were collected during each visit of the cystic fibrosis clinic. Longitudinal changes were analyzed using mixed-effects models. Results: Thirty-one patients were included; 70% were female, mean age at ETI initiation was 16.6 (±7.5) years, and 51.6% were <18 years. Pancreatic insuffic...
records.why_it_matters: A Colombian cohort adds long-term ETI evidence from a setting where access and continuity can be challenging.
records.confidence_reason: Structured abstract provides 24-month outcomes for lung function, BMI, strength, safety, and access context.

records.title: Real-world outcomes of CFTR modulation in advanced cystic fibrosis lung disease - in a Portuguese cystic fibrosis reference centre
records.summary: Background: Advanced cystic fibrosis lung disease (ACFLD) remains a major cause of morbidity and mortality among young people with cystic fibrosis (CF), despite highly effective CFTR modulators. Objective: To study the eficacy and safety of elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor (ETI) in ACFLD. Methods: Retrospective observational study including patients with ACFLD followed at the CF Reference Centre of Hospital de Santa Maria between March 2021 and December 2025 on treatment with ETI for at least 36 months. Results: Of 78 adult patients followed at the centre, 11 were enrolled in the study. 54.5% were female; 72.7% were aged 18–39 years. Median age at initiation of ETI was 31 years. All patients carried at least o...
secondary_themes: Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
records.why_it_matters: ETI may stabilize advanced CF lung disease and reduce oxygen, inhaled antibiotic, or transplant-related needs in some patients.
records.confidence_reason: Structured abstract focuses on advanced disease with 36-month ETI follow-up and clinically relevant support outcomes.

records.title: SNOT-22 scores for sino-nasal symptoms improved following elexecaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor (ETI) introduction
records.summary: The SNOT- 22 (sino-nasal outcome test) questionnaire is a validated tool used to measure symptom severity in chronic rhinosinusitis, and the impact on quality of life. Objectives: We tested the hypothesis that SNOT-22 score would improve after the introduction of elexecaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor (ETI). This has previously been documented in a retrospective review in Germany, but not with prospectively collected data. Methods: SNOT-22 scores were recorded prospectively during annual reviews of adult patients with cystic fibrosis. We analysed these retrospectively, comparing the mean scores from 2018-2019 (pre ETI) and 2021-2025 (post ETI), using paired t-test for normally distributed paired data, Wilcoxon signe...
records.why_it_matters: Prospectively collected SNOT-22 data suggest ETI can improve sino-nasal symptom burden in adult CF care.
records.confidence_reason: Structured abstract gives pre/post score comparison, adult cohort details, and atopy subgroup analysis.

records.title: One-year effects of highly effective modulator therapy on lung function, anthropometric parameters, and lipid profile in children with cystic fibrosis
records.summary: Objectives: The aim of this study was to evaluate the effects of the highly effective modulator treatment (HEMT) on lipid profiles in children with cystic fibrosis (CF). Methods: This retrospective cohort study includes 29 children with CF who have received HEMT for at least 1 year. Clinical, anthropometric, spirometric, and lipid parameters were compared at baseline and after one year of treatment. Results: Descriptive characteristics of the patients are given in Table 1. After one year, BMI increased significantly from 15.7 to 16.2 kg/m² (p = 0.043), whereas the BMI Z-score remained stable (p = 0.259). Pulmonary function improved significantly: forced expiratory volume in 1 second (FEV1) lt, FEV1%, and FEV1...
records.why_it_matters: Child modulator follow-up showed improved lung function and BMI without an atherogenic lipid pattern, informing metabolic monitoring.
records.confidence_reason: Structured abstract reports one-year pediatric lipid, anthropometric, and spirometric comparisons.

records.title: Development and validation of a human scalp hair analysis for determining long-term exposure to CFTR modulators
records.summary: Objectives: Cystic Fibrosis Transmembrane Conductance regulator (CFTR) modulators (elexacaftor (ELX), tezacaftor (TEZ), ivacaftor (IVA), lumacaftor (LUM) are the cornerstone of CF’s pharmacologic treatment. While adherence to CFTR modulators is critical for effectiveness, a reliable noninvasive method for determining long-term medication adherence is lacking. Direct measurements such as plasma only reflect a snapshot of last day’s adherence and are invasive to perform. Therefore, we aimed to develop and validate a method for quantification of CFTR modulators in human scalp hair. Methods: LC-MSMS was used for quantification. For method development scalp hair from people with CF (pwCF) using CFTR modulators was...
secondary_themes: Care burden, adherence, and psychosocial impact
records.why_it_matters: Hair measurement of modulator exposure could provide a less invasive long-term adherence and exposure marker than plasma sampling.
records.confidence_reason: Structured abstract describes LC-MS/MS development, validation criteria, and which modulators were reliably quantified.

records.title: The “last families” in cystic fibrosis: equity, non-responders, and the translational imperative
records.summary: Objectives: Highly effective CFTR modulators have transformed outcomes for approximately 90% of patients in the United States. Individuals with Class I mutations, disproportionately Hispanic and Black, derive no benefit from these therapies and continue to experience the full burden of disease. These “last families,” identified from birth as non-responders, represent a growing equity and translational gap. Methods: Through a CFF Research Development Program, our rural CF center partnered with Cedars-Sinai to implement a patient-derived nasal epithelial “nose-on-a-chip” platform for individualized drug testing. We conducted a longitudinal clinical and social evaluation of a 19-year-old Hispanic male with a Clas...
records.primary_theme: Precision CFTR biology and rare variants
secondary_themes: Emerging genetic, RNA, and cell-model therapies
Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
records.why_it_matters: A non-responder case links rare variant biology, patient-derived testing, and social vulnerability as an equity gap in the modulator era.
records.confidence_reason: Structured abstract provides genotype, engagement barriers, nose-on-chip testing, and registry implications.

records.title: Characteristics of adult patients with cystic fibrosis infected with Burkholderia cepacia complex microorganisms: twenty years of observation
records.summary: Aim: To assess the dynamics of infection and survival of adult patients with cystic fibrosis (CF) in groups of patients with chronic respiratory tract infection with Pseudomonas aeruginosa(Pa) and Burkholderia cepacia complex(Bcc); to compare the group of Pa and Bcc by age, age of diagnosis, genotype, anthropometric parameters, spirometry, frequency of major complications of CF, volume of the received therapy. Methods: To analyse survival, we compared groups of adult patients with chronic Рa/Bсс infection (852 patients in total, 188 of them with Bсс) observed from November 1992 to December 2025. Survival was assessed using the Kaplan-Meier curve. A one-time analysis of Рa/Всс groups was conducted in December 2...
records.why_it_matters: Long-term adult data reinforce Burkholderia cepacia complex infection as a major survival risk in CF.
records.confidence_reason: Methods and results compare large Pa and Bcc infected cohorts with survival and clinical characteristics.

records.title: Impact of elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor on neutrophil homeostasis: evidence from 12-month therapy in cystic fibrosis
records.summary: Objectives: Cystic fibrosis (CF) is characterized by persistent neutrophil-driven inflammation, which plays a central role in progressive lung disease and systemic inflammatory burden. While the triple cystic fibrosis transmembrane conductance regulator (CFTR) modulator therapy elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor (ETI) has significantly improved lung function and clinical outcomes, its effects on circulating inflammatory markers, particularly peripheral blood neutrophil counts, remain insufficiently characterized. Methods: We conducted a prospective, observational study including 56 people with CF (pwCF), aged 8–41 years. Peripheral blood neutrophil counts were measured at baseline and after 12 months of ETI ther...
records.why_it_matters: A 12-month ETI neutrophil signal supports systemic anti-inflammatory effects beyond standard lung-function endpoints.
records.confidence_reason: Structured abstract reports prospective sampling, cohort age range, neutrophil reductions, and responder analysis.

records.title: Microbiological characteristics of patients with cystic fibrosis(СF) with chronic Burkholderia cepacia complex infection(Всс) in an adult center in Russia during treatment of CFTR modulator therapy
records.summary: Aim: To evaluate the microbiological characteristics of adult patients with chronic Bсс infection, including those taking CFTR modulators. records.title: The effect of Staphylococcus aureus in the lung epithelial barrier in cystic fibrosis
records.summary: Background: The bronchial epithelium acts as first defence against a wide range of exogenous substances and pathogens by forming a barrier that prevents them from penetrating the tissue. The epithelial barrier function is maintained by apical junctional proteins (AJP) that form between neighbouring cells and consist of apical tight junctions (TJ) such as claudins, occludin and JAM-A and adherent junctions (AJ) such as E-cadherin. People with cystic fibrosis (CF) have a weakened lung epithelial barrier; at the same time, CF patients are also more susceptible to infections such as Staphylococcus aureus(Sa), in part due to this same weakened barrier. It has been proposed that CFTR interacts with some AJPs to help...
secondary_themes: Precision CFTR biology and rare variants
records.why_it_matters: Cell work shows Staphylococcus aureus can disrupt epithelial barrier proteins more severely in CFTR-deficient cells.

records.title: Sputum-based single-cell profiling of airway immune responses in cystic fibrosis patients with Non-tuberculous mycobacteria pulmonary disease
records.summary: People with cystic fibrosis (PwCF) are at high risk of Mycobacterium abscessus complex (MABSC) infection and show heterogeneous clinical outcomes, ranging from asymptomatic colonization to non-tuberculous mycobacterial pulmonary disease (NTM-PD), a chronic condition associated with progressive lung damage. While systemic immune signatures have improved disease characterization, local airway immune responses remain limited, highlighting the need for reliable biomarkers reflecting lung disease severity. records.title: Tenascin-C in children with cystic fibrosis
records.summary: Objective-aim: Tenascin-C is an extracellular matrix protein involved with immunomodulatory properties that plays a role in epithelial-to-mesenchymal transition, tissue regeneration, and lung development. We aimed to investigate the role of tenascin-C in cystic fibrosis (CF). Method: This is a cross-sectional, observational study that included 44 children with CF and 44 healthy subjects. Serum of Tenascin-C, interleukin (IL)-8, and neutrophil elastase (NE) were determined by the enzyme-linked immunosorbent assay (ELISA) method. records.title: High-dimensional spectral flow cytometry profiling reveals systemic immune dysregulation in pediatric cystic fibrosis
records.summary: Cystic fibrosis (CF) is a rare, life-limiting genetic disorder affecting 1 in 5000 newborns in Spain. CF arises from mutations that disrupt or abolish Cystic Fibrosis Transmembrane Conductance Regulator (CFTR) protein function. The lungs are most severely affected due to thick mucus that impairs mucociliary clearance, leading to a self-perpetuating cycle of infection and inflammation. CFTR modulators have improved protein function and quality of life, but airway inflammation often persists, and their effect on immune-mediated inflammation remains unclear. Children with CF also exhibit intrinsic innate immune abnormalities; innate cells may be developmentally immature or functionally impaired, reducing bacteria...

records.title: The IκB-kinase-β (IKKβ) inhibitor PS-1145 modulates inflammatory response and oxidative stress in F508del-CFTR airway epithelial cells
records.summary: Objectives: Prolonged P. aeruginosa infections have been linked to chronic inflammation in Cystic Fibrosis (CF) lung, worsening the damage to lung tissue by neutrophil-derived reactive oxygen species (ROS) and proteases. Methods: We performed a dose-response of an IkB-kinase-b (IKKb) inhibitor, PS-1145, in F508del-CFTR CFBE cells and primary nasal epithelial (HNE) cells from pwCF bearing F508del mutation. records.title: Exploring upper airway proteomics in cystic fibrosis: insights into airway immune biology from the CF-Tracker study
records.summary: Background: Pulmonary exacerbations are a major cause of morbidity in cystic fibrosis and are commonly triggered by viral infection and dysregulated airway immune responses. The nasal mucosa provides an accessible site in which upper airway immune processes may offer insight into mechanisms underlying pulmonary exacerbations. Methods: Nasal mucosal lining fluid was collected from people with cystic fibrosis (PwCF; n=12) and age-matched healthy controls (n=8). records.title: Phase 2 population pharmacokinetics (popPK) of verducatib (BI 1291583) in people with cystic fibrosis and non-cystic fibrosis bronchiectasis (CFBE and NCFBE)
records.summary: Objectives: Bronchiectasis, a chronic lung disease, is driven by dipeptidyl peptidase 1 (DPP1)-activated neutrophil serine proteases. records.title: Asthma biologic therapy in patients with cystic fibrosis in an adult cystic fibrosis centre in the United Kingdom
records.summary: Objectives: Asthma is present in 8.9% of adult patients with cystic fibrosis (pwCF)1. Biological therapies have been used safely in the management of severe asthma in non-CF patients for a number of years. Use of these drugs in the CF population is limited thus far and there have been some anecdotal reports of CF patients deteriorating after starting them in other centres. Methods: Patients in our adult CF centre who had been prescribed asthma biologics were retrospectively identified and data collected before and after biologic started. records.title: Reducing multiple-breath washout test burden: agreement between the first acceptable trial and mean lung clearance index
records.summary: Background: The lung clearance index (LCI) derived from multiple-breath washout (MBW) testing is a sensitive marker of early lung disease in cystic fibrosis (CF). However, widespread clinical implementation remains limited, partly due to long test duration. Current ATS/ERS technical standards recommend reporting LCI as the mean of at least three technically acceptable trials. Previous studies have shown minimal differences in group mean LCI when reducing repetitions from three to two; however, agreement between the first technically acceptable trial and the mean LCI has not been evaluated. records.summary: Objectives: Description of people with CF (pwCF) not treated with CFTR modulators (CFTRm) is needed to improve health of this population. records.title: Correlation between Lung Clearance Index, spirometry and Bhalla Score in paediatric patients with cystic fibrosis
records.summary: Objectives: To correlate LCI and spirometry with structural damage measured by the Bhalla Score. records.summary: Introduction: The Lung Clearance Index (LCI) determined Nitrogen Multiple breath washout(N₂-MBW) allows detection of early peripheral airways disease. records.title: Can CF caregivers reliably predict home spirometry usage in pwCF?
records.summary: Objective: Home spirometry has been widely adopted in the delivery of cystic fibrosis (CF) care. While existing literature largely supports its feasibility and positive outcomes, behaviour around home disease monitoring remains poorly understood. This study aimed to evaluate healthcare professionals’ (HCPs') ability to estimate home spirometry usage pwCF and compare these with actual recorded data. records.title: Breathing at home with CF: year-long indoor air quality insights from the CF-Tracker study in Greater Manchester
records.summary: Background: Indoor and outdoor air pollution are major health risks. Indoor air quality (IAQ) accounts for > 90% of exposure. In people with cystic fibrosis (pwCF) heightened susceptibility could mean that peaks in particulate matter (PM) or poorly ventilated conditions may trigger symptoms. Yet indoor pollutant levels, links to outdoor air, and clinical relevance in CF are poorly characterised. Methods: As part of the CF-Tracker study, real-time IAQ pollution sensors were placed in the homes of a subset of CF participants. records.title: Real-world impact of elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor on functional exercise capacity in paediatric cystic fibrosis
records.summary: Introduction: In people with cystic fibrosis (pwCF), exercise intolerance and reduced functional capacity are relevant outcomes with prognosis implications. Although elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor (ETI) improves lung function and nutritional status, its impact on functional exercise capacity (feC) remains incompletely characterized. Objectives: This study aimed to assess the impact of ETI on feC using the 6MWT and to evaluate associated clinical and laboratory changes in pwCF. records.title: Machine learning-based prediction of longitudinal lung function trajectories in patients not using modulators from national CF registry data in Turkey
records.summary: Objectives: Predicting lung function (LF) trajectories is vital for cystic fibrosis (CF) patients without access to CFTR modulators. records.summary: Background: Body plethysmography complements spirometry in cystic fibrosis by assessing lung volumes and airway resistance, enabling detection of lung hyperinflation and air trapping, as well as supporting longitudinal treatment monitoring. records.title: A breath of fresh air: 10-year changes in the presence of bronchiectasis and lung function amongst people with cystic fibrosis joining adult services in Wales
records.summary: Objectives: Amongst the public and non-specialists, Cystic Fibrosis (CF) remains most known for its respiratory manifestations. records.summary: Objective: To evaluate pulmonary changes associated with Aspergillus fumigatus in children with cystic fibrosis using CT imaging investigations. records.title: Clinical utility of 129Xe ventilation Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) in paediatric cystic fibrosis (CF) patients
records.summary: Objectives: To demonstrate the clinical impact of 129Xe ventilation MRI on CF care at Sheffield Children’s Hospital, UK, we here describe 3 recent typical clinical scenarios where Xe-MRI uniquely impacted decision making. records.title: Longitudinal lung ultrasound assessment in cystic fibrosis patients undergoing CFTR modulator therapy: a 2-year prospective study
records.summary: Objectives: Pulmonary involvement in cystic fibrosis (CF) is the main determinant of disease prognosis. records.summary: Objectives: As an emerging technology in cystic fibrosis (CF), there is a need to support the understanding and communication of lung magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) scan results. records.title: Chest x-ray utilisation and impact on clinical decision making in the modulator era: a retrospective cohort study
records.summary: Introduction: Highly effective modulator therapy (HEMT) has transformed survival in people with cystic fibrosis (pwCF), with life expectancy 71.6 years. records.title: Breath volatile organic compounds as biomarkers of pulmonary exacerbations in cystic fibrosis, initial data from Cystic Fibrosis-Tracker study
records.summary: Background: Analysis of volatile organic compounds (VOCs) in exhaled breath offers a non-invasive method to capture metabolic, inflammatory, and microbial processes within the cystic fibrosis (CF) airway. records.title: CF-Tracker study: measuring the exposome in CF
records.summary: Background: Pulmonary exacerbations (PEx) in cystic fibrosis (CF) are clinically heterogeneous, varying in symptoms, triggers, and treatment response. records.title: Circulating T and B cell signatures in cystic fibrosis and prediction of pulmonary exacerbations
records.summary: Background: Pulmonary exacerbations remain a prominent feature of cystic fibrosis (CF) but causes and pathophysiology are poorly understood. records.title: Determinants of acute pulmonary exacerbations in cystic fibrosis: a nationwide registry-based analysis
records.summary: Objectives: Pulmonary exacerbations (PEx) remain a significant cause of morbidity and lung function decline in cystic fibrosis (CF). This study utilizes data from the National CF Registry to identify independent risk factors for PEx in pediatric patients across different developmental stages. Methods: A retrospective analysis was conducted on 2,228 patients from the Cystic Fibrosis National Registry, stratified by age: <6 years and ≥6 years. records.title: Impact of highly effective modulator therapy on iron status and anaemia in children with cystic fibrosis
records.summary: Objectives: Cystic fibrosis (CF) affects respiratory and gastrointestinal systems and frequently causes exocrine pancreatic insufficiency, leading to impaired absorption of macro- and micronutrients, including iron. records.summary: Objective: To evaluate changes in nutritional status and pulmonary function (FEV1) in adults with CF following the initiation of CFTR modulator treatment. records.title: Predictors of cystic fibrosis-related liver disease: data from cystic fibrosis registry of Turkey
records.summary: Background: Cystic fibrosis (CF) is a multisystem disorder frequently complicated by cystic fibrosis–related liver disease (CFLD). records.summary: Objectives: Cystic fibrosis (CF) is a multisystem disease primarily affecting the respiratory system and is associated with muscle weakness and reduced exercise capacity. records.summary: Objectives: CFTR modulator therapies have substantially improved clinical outcomes in cystic fibrosis (CF); however, unequal access to these treatments remains a major concern. records.summary: Objectives: Advanced lung damage remains the primary cause of morbidity and mortality in cystic fibrosis (CF). records.title: Advancing inclusive enrollment: insights from the LTx READY CF 2 study
records.summary: Objective: LTx READY CF 2 is a fully remote, multicenter randomized controlled trial of a lung transplant education website that aimed to enroll populations underrepresented in CF research (goal >33% minoritized race/ethnicity, Medicaid insurance, or high school education or less). records.summary: Objectives: Test whether a CF-specific lung transplant (LTx) education website, Take on Transplant (ToT), improves preparedness for LTx decisions compared to a general transplant website (unos.org). records.summary: Objectives: Sleep disorders among people with cystic fibrosis (PwCF) appear to be an important comorbidity that is frequently underdiagnosed. records.summary: Objectives: Cystic fibrosis (CF) is a chronic multisystemic disease characterised by malnutrition and progressive lung disease. records.summary: Background: Sleep disturbance is common in adults with cystic fibrosis (CF) and is likely multi-factorial. records.title: Working to improve out-of-hours care for cystic fibrosis patients presenting in a district general hospital
records.summary: Background: Local paediatric DGH hospital in the North west caring for 34 children with cystic fibrosis. Most of the care for these children is done by the specific CF team, however out of hours the on-call general paediatric team are responsible for them. Incidents occurred where out of hours prescribers did not alter the dosing for antibiotics when the patient was on modulator therapy. records.summary: Objective: To present the regional experience of the Republic of Tatarstan in the use of ivacaftor+tezacaftor+elexacaftor in children with cystic fibrosis (CF) with a change in the brand name of the drug. records.title: A-STEP performance test and its correlation with muscle strength and clinical parameters in patients diagnosed with cystic fibrosis

records.summary: Objectives: With modulator therapy, People With Cystic Fibrosis (pwCF) live longer but face increasing age-related comorbidities.
