records.title: Correlation between 1-minute sit-to-stand test performance and FEV1 in adults with cystic fibrosis
records.summary: Objectives: To evaluate the correlation between functional capacity (assessed via the 1-minute Sit-to-Stand Test (STST)) and lung function parameters (absolute FEV1 and FEV1%) in a cohort of patients with Cystic Fibrosis (CF), determining if this field test serves as a reliable predictor. Methods: A cross-sectional descriptive study was conducted analyzing data from 40 adults patients. The following variables were recorded: lung function (absolute FEV1 (L) and FEV1 percentage (FEV1%) predicted; functional capacity (total number of repetitions achieved in the STST. Statical analysis: Pearson correlation coefficient (r) was used to determine the strength of the association between variables. Results: A moderate...
records.why_it_matters: The one-minute sit-to-stand test is a low-cost functional measure that can complement spirometry in adult CF monitoring.
records.confidence_reason: Structured abstract reports adult cohort, STST repetitions, FEV1 correlations, and interpretation of variability.

records.title: Body composition and muscular strength in adults with cystic fibrosis in the post-modulator era
records.summary: Objectives: Highly effective CFTR modulator therapy has improved lung function and body mass in adults with cystic fibrosis (awCF). However, whether these improvements translate into preserved skeletal muscle mass, strength and functional capacity remains unclear. We compared absolute measures of muscle function and body composition in awCF receiving modulator therapy with age-, sex- and BMI-matched healthy controls (HC). Methods: Fifteen male awCF and nine HC were assessed. Maximal hand grip strength and isometric knee extensor strength were measured using dynamometry. Whole-body fat mass (FM) and fat-free mass (FFM) were quantified by dual-energy X-ray absorptiometry. Functional capacity was assessed using 6...
records.why_it_matters: Normal BMI in the post-modulator era may mask deficits in muscle mass, strength, and functional capacity.
records.confidence_reason: Structured abstract compares modulator-treated adult men with matched controls using DXA, dynamometry, and walk distance.

records.title: The relationship between handgrip strength, and pulmonary function, body mass index, and peak power in adults with cystic fibrosis.
records.summary: Objectives: Handgrip Strength (HGS) is an assessment of forearm grip strength and is often used alongside a nutritional assessment, with lower results often linking to negative health outcomes, whilst also acting as a proxy for exercise function and musculoskeletal health. As part of a Cystic Fibrosis (CF) patients annual review, HGS was introduced alongside other regular exercise tests. This study sought to assess whether there is a relationship between HGS, Pulmonary Function Tests (PFT), Body Mass Index (BMI) and Peak Power Output (PPO). Methods: Forty-nine adult patients with CF (PwCF) (19 females, 30 males) with a mean (SD) age of 36.4 ± 13.1 years, and FEV1 of 3.09 ± 1.15 L (82 ± 22%) performed a HGS tes...
records.why_it_matters: Handgrip strength may reflect disease status and exercise function better than BMI-based nutritional assessment alone.
records.confidence_reason: Structured abstract reports adult annual-review testing and correlations with lung function, BMI, weight, and peak power.

records.title: Substrate oxidation during submaximal exercise in children with cystic fibrosis on elexacaftor-tezacaftor-ivacaftor therapy
records.summary: Objective: Exercise is a well-established therapy for children with cystic fibrosis (CF). As CF health continues to evolve with elexacaftor-tezacaftor-ivacaftor (ETI) therapy, understanding fuel metabolism during exercise is increasingly important to support a healthy, active lifestyle. This study aims to compare substrate oxidation during exercise in children with CF who were short-term and long-term users of ETI. Methods: Children aged 8 to 17 y, were divided into short-term (G1) or long-term (G2) ETI therapy groups using a median split of ETI duration. Basic anthropometric measurements were collected. Aerobic fitness, defined as the highest 20-sec oxygen uptake (VO2peak), was measured on a treadmill using a...
records.why_it_matters: Exercise fuel-use data may help tailor nutrition and activity guidance for children on longer-term ETI.
records.confidence_reason: Structured abstract reports pediatric ETI-duration groups, treadmill testing, indirect calorimetry, and substrate oxidation results.

records.title: Skeletal muscle mass and physical activity after CFTR modulator therapy in adults with cystic fibrosis in Croatia
records.summary: Objectives: The objective of this study was to evaluate longitudinal changes in skeletal muscle mass and physical activity levels in adults with cystic fibrosis before and after initiation of CFTR modulator therapy, and to explore the association between physical activity and changes in body composition. Methods: A retrospective longitudinal study was conducted in adult patients with cystic fibrosis treated at the Cystic Fibrosis Center at University Hospital Centre Zagreb, Croatia. Skeletal muscle mass and body fat percentage were assessed using bioelectrical impedance analysis at the last visit before CFTR modulator initiation and at annual follow-up visits thereafter. Physical activity was recorded from cli...
records.why_it_matters: Physical activity appears important for favorable muscle and body-composition changes after adult modulator initiation.
records.confidence_reason: Structured abstract describes longitudinal adult body-composition follow-up and associations with activity level.

records.title: Playphysio: gamification of airway clearance physiotherapy for children with cystic fibrosis - A feasibility randomised clinical trial.
records.summary: Objectives: Airway clearance techniques (ACT) contribute to a high burden of care for young people with Cystic Fibrosis (CF). Treatment compliance is important to help maintain health and combat respiratory infections. Technology now makes gamification and monitoring of ACT possible. Playphysio® was developed as a social venture with the goal of supporting ACT and making a positive impact on the lives of children and their families. This pilot study aimed to assess feasibility of the technology within clinical practice. Secondary outcomes around treatment quality, compliance and quality of life (CFQ-R) were also captured. Method: Single site, investigator single blinded randomised controlled feasibility trial....
secondary_themes: Care burden, adherence, and psychosocial impact
records.why_it_matters: Gamified airway clearance improved compliance and treatment quality in children, addressing a high-burden daily therapy.
records.confidence_reason: Structured feasibility RCT abstract reports randomization, intervention, sample size, compliance, and treatment-quality outcomes.

records.title: Benchmarking sputum induction (SI) practice across UK cystic fibrosis (CF) centres
records.summary: Background: Sputum induction (SI) is used in CF when patients cannot provide a spontaneous sample; however, SI practice varies widely across UK CF centres in terms of protocols and devices used. Although the European Cystic Fibrosis Society Clinical Trials Network (ECFS-CTN) has published a standard operating procedure (SOP) for SI, recommending jet or ultrasonic (US) nebulisers for use in clinical trials, the extent to which this informs routine clinical practice remains unclear. Methods: A national benchmarking survey was distributed September-December 2025 to all UK CF centres. The questionnaire was circulated to physiotherapists via the Association of Chartered Physiotherapists in CF (ACPCF), and direct em...
records.primary_theme: Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
records.why_it_matters: UK sputum induction practice varies widely, indicating a need for updated competencies and SOPs aligned with current devices.
records.confidence_reason: Structured benchmarking survey reports national center responses, indications, devices, protocols, and SOP gaps.

records.title: Sustained feasibility and success of induced sputum sampling in the era of elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor: a real-world single-centre study
records.summary: Objectives: The introduction of elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor (ETI) therapy has been associated with a substantial reduction in sputum production. Nevertheless, routine surveillance of bronchopulmonary pathogens via sputum samples remains central to the clinical management of cystic fibrosis (CF). This study aimed to compare the success rates of induced sputum (IS) collection before and after the initiation of ETI in a real-world cohort. Methods: Clinical data and IS success rate were retrospectively analysed over a 3-year period. Sputum frequency and estimated volume were recorded on the day of IS collection using a standardized scale. All IS procedures were performed by trained physiotherapists in accorda...
records.why_it_matters: Induced sputum remains highly feasible after ETI and can reveal pathogens that change antibiotic management.
records.confidence_reason: Structured abstract reports pre/post ETI sample volume, success rates, age groups, and pathogen-driven treatment decisions.

records.title: The role of induced sputum, oropharyngeal swabs and cough swabs in non-expectorating children with cystic fibrosis
records.summary: Objectives: Children with cystic fibrosis (CF) often have difficulty spontaneously expectorating sputum. Induced sputum (IS), oropharyngeal swabs (OPS) and cough swabs (CS) are commonly used. Evidence for diagnostic accuracy of samples in the post modulator setting is limited and no study has compared OPS to CS. It is also unknown if clinical features (cough quality and sample appearance) are useful in determining best sampling approach. Methods: In this prospective study, children undergoing clinically indicated IS provided paired IS, OPS and CS. Diagnostic accuracy of OPS and CS was calculated with IS treated as the reference standard. Feasibility was assessed using collection time and distress ratings. Asso...
records.why_it_matters: Oropharyngeal swabs outperformed cough swabs against induced sputum in non-expectorating children, guiding sampling choices.
records.confidence_reason: Structured prospective abstract reports paired samples, diagnostic accuracy, feasibility, and pathogen detection results.

records.title: Feasibility of sputum collection in adults with cystic fibrosis in the CFTR modulator era
records.summary: Background: Sputum is a key biofluid in cystic fibrosis (CF) clinical studies. However, sputum volume is often markedly reduced following initiation of CFTR modulator therapies. Many people with CF (pwCF) experience decreased cough and sputum production, and some report being unable to expectorate at all following treatment. These changes have raised concerns about the feasibility of sputum-based endpoints in clinical studies. Methods: Sputum was collected from pwCF as part of the CF-Tracker study (www.pulse-cf.com/tracker-study). Eligible participants must have experienced a pulmonary exacerbation within the past 12 months; those a priori known to be unable to produce sputum, even with saline induction, are e...
records.why_it_matters: Adult CF-Tracker data show sputum endpoints remain feasible in selected modulator-treated populations with physiotherapy support.
records.confidence_reason: Partial abstract provides methods and early feasibility results, but full structured conclusions are limited.

records.title: Detection of dysfunctional breathing patterns in children and young people with cystic fibrosis during cardiopulmonary exercise testing
records.summary: Objectives: Breathing patterns may reflect physiological responses to respiratory disease; however, in the absence of organic pathology they may represent dysfunctional breathing. Dysfunctional breathing can occur in children and young people (CYP) with cystic fibrosis (CF), where symptoms may overlap with disease-related limitation and be difficult to distinguish clinically. This study aimed to evaluate the incidence and characteristics of dysfunctional breathing patterns identified on cardiopulmonary exercise testing (CPET) in this population. Methods: We retrospectively analysed CPETs performed between January 2022 and December 2025 in children and young people with CF attending a tertiary centre. Inclusion...
records.why_it_matters: CPET can identify dysfunctional breathing in children with CF and direct targeted physiotherapy intervention.
records.confidence_reason: Structured abstract reports CPET review criteria, prevalence, pattern types, and referral implications.

records.title: International practice in the assessment and management of dysfunctional breathing in cystic fibrosis: a cross-sectional survey
records.summary: Objectives: Dysfunctional breathing (DB) is increasingly recognised in cystic fibrosis (CF), yet no internationally standardised definition, diagnostic pathway, or treatment framework exists. The objective was to describe international clinical practice in DB assessment, diagnosis, and management in CF, and to identify priorities for standardisation. Methods: An international, cross-sectional, anonymous online survey of healthcare professionals working in CF centres was conducted. Developed by the European Cystic Fibrosis Society (ECFS) Exercise Working Group, the survey explored terminology, diagnostic and management approaches, and perceived challenges. Data were analysed descriptively. Results: 64 responses...
secondary_themes: Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
records.why_it_matters: International variation in dysfunctional-breathing care highlights the need for consensus definitions and treatment pathways.
records.confidence_reason: Structured survey abstract reports respondents, diagnostic methods, barriers, and consensus priorities.

records.title: Prevalence of urinary incontinence in adults with cystic fibrosis in the era of CFTR modulator therapy: a cross-sectional study
records.summary: Objectives: Urinary incontinence (UI) is an underreported yet clinically relevant concern in adults with cystic fibrosis (CF). Previous studies have shown high prevalence rates in CF. However, most were conducted prior to the widespread implementation of CFTR modulator therapy. The aim of this study was to determine the prevalence, severity and associated factors of UI in adults with CF, and to evaluate its relationship with CFTR modulator use. Methods: This retrospective cross-sectional study included adults with CF from the CF Centre of Ghent University Hospital. Using the validated International Consultation on Incontinence Questionnaire (ICIQ), UI was assessed during annual reviews from January to December...
records.primary_theme: Care burden, adherence, and psychosocial impact
records.why_it_matters: Urinary incontinence remains common in adult CF, particularly women, and should be screened regardless of modulator use.
records.confidence_reason: Structured abstract reports validated ICIQ assessment, adult sample size, prevalence, regression, and sex-specific findings.

records.title: Treatment strategies for managing pelvic floor dysfunction in individuals with chronic respiratory conditions including cystic fibrosis: a scoping review
records.summary: Objective: Chronic respiratory conditions, such as cystic fibrosis, increase the risk of PFD due to persistent coughing and altered breathing patterns that strain pelvic floor muscles. Despite robust evidence supporting pelvic floor muscle training in the general population its effectiveness and other treatment strategies in individuals with chronic respiratory conditions remain underexplored. This scoping review aimed to map and critically appraise interventions for PFD in this population. Methods: A comprehensive search of CINAHL, PubMed, Cochrane Library, MedLine, and grey literature was conducted up to April 2025, including studies of any design addressing PFD management in adults with chronic respiratory...
secondary_themes: Care burden, adherence, and psychosocial impact
records.why_it_matters: Pelvic floor muscle training appears promising, but evidence for chronic respiratory populations remains small and uneven.
records.confidence_reason: Structured scoping-review abstract reports search methods, included designs, intervention categories, and evidence gaps.

records.title: Expert consensus and validation of Good Practice Points for the UK Standards of Care and Good Clinical Practice for the Physiotherapy Management of Cystic Fibrosis document
records.summary: Background: The dynamic evolution of Cystic Fibrosis (CF) care necessitated an update to the CF Trust and Association of Chartered Physiotherapists in CF (ACPCF) Standards of Care and Good Clinical Practice for the Physiotherapy Management of Cystic Fibrosis document. Where robust evidence was unavailable, Good Practice Points (GPPs) were developed from the collective clinical experience of chapter authors and document editors. Objective: To systematically validate the updated GPPs among ACPCF members, ensuring high clinical consensus and maximising their applicability in CF physiotherapy care. Method: A sequential, four-part survey was deployed among ACPCF members to mitigate survey fatigue (maximum member pa...
secondary_themes: Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
records.why_it_matters: Validated physiotherapy good-practice points provide consensus guidance where modulator-era evidence is still emerging.
records.confidence_reason: Structured abstract describes consensus survey process, acceptance thresholds, statement counts, and guideline incorporation.

records.title: Global collaboration to develop a physiotherapy outcome measure toolkit
records.summary: Background: An endeavour to improve harmony of data collection in physiotherapy resulted in global collaboration and development of an outcome measure (OM) toolkit for clinicians. Developing recommendations for OMs in physiotherapy, with global consensus, will enable quality research, inform treatment decisions, and improve service delivery in cystic fibrosis (CF). Methods: Through a Delphi process key statements which underpin data used by physiotherapists in clinical practice and research were identified. A broader survey was circulated across a global physiotherapy network to determine current clinical use of OMs, self-rated importance of these, and identification of additional and emerging OMs. An internat...
secondary_themes: Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
records.why_it_matters: A global outcome-measure toolkit could standardize physiotherapy assessment for clinical care and research.
records.confidence_reason: Structured abstract reports Delphi work, broad survey responses, core measure areas, and toolkit development.

records.title: “It’s good to keep talking”: ongoing delivery of the REACH for CF virtual international physiotherapy discussion forum.
records.summary: Background: Ongoing professional development (PD) is vital for clinicians in the ever-changing landscape of cystic fibrosis (CF) care. Collaboration across the globe on research initiatives and clinical service development priorities is essential. In 2021 a team of five clinicians from the UK, Australia and US combined to develop REACH for CF (a Research, Education and Collaboration Hub for Cystic Fibrosis), a global consortium to promote education, collaboration, and virtual discussion forums. Method: In 2022, a survey was sent out to physiotherapists, physical and respiratory therapists to identify PD priorities in CF practice. REACH for CF developed an education program to meet these priorities, delivering...
records.primary_theme: Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
records.why_it_matters: The REACH forum demonstrates a scalable model for international physiotherapy education and collaboration in evolving CF care.
records.confidence_reason: Structured abstract reports professional-development needs survey, virtual sessions, feedback, and collaboration aims.

records.title: Finding the measurement of frailty in Adult Cystic Fibrosis
records.summary: Objectives: Historically, shorter life expectancy in people with cystic fibrosis (PWCF) limited the emergence of age-related comorbidities such as frailty. Frailty represents a geriatric syndrome generally recognized as a state of vulnerability due to adverse health outcomes including an increased risk of falls and fractures, hospitalization, decreased quality of life, physical disability, and early mortality. Advances in Cystic Fibrosis (CF) care, particularly Cystic Fibrosis Transmembrane Regulator (CFTR) modulators, have significantly improved survival, with many PWCF now expected to live into older age. Despite this, there is limited evidence to guide assessment and management of frailty in an ageing CF po...
records.why_it_matters: Frailty assessment is becoming relevant in adult CF as survival improves and age-related vulnerability emerges earlier than expected.
records.confidence_reason: Structured abstract applies two frailty tools and reports comorbidities, frailty categories, and measurement considerations.

records.title: What’s the use? Provision of consumable nebuliser equipment – what we can learn.
records.summary: Introduction: Nebulised therapy is a cornerstone of routine Cystic Fibrosis (CF) care, and the consumables this requires generate a considerable cost burden. We audited this in our large adult CF centre over a 12-month period to explore potential cost saving. Methods: We retrospectively reviewed the use of PARI compressor and eflow consumables between March 2024-February 2025, comparing this with expected use based on individual patients’ prescriptions and manufacturer recommendations for replacement of equipment. Usage was then categorised as either: as expected, under expected use, over expected use or significantly over expected use. Findings were used to develop a standard operating procedure (SOP) to stan...
records.primary_theme: Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
secondary_themes: Care burden, adherence, and psychosocial impact
records.why_it_matters: Nebuliser consumable tracking can standardize supply, reduce waste, and reveal possible adherence or education issues.
records.confidence_reason: Methods/results/conclusion provide equipment-use audit, cost, overuse/underuse categories, and SOP response.

records.title: How should airway clearance for people with cystic fibrosis be advised following the widespread availability of cystic fibrosis transmembrane regulator modulators?
records.summary: Objectives: To map the available evidence on how airway clearance techniques are being used, reduced, or replaced in people with cystic fibrosis receiving modulators. Method: A comprehensive search of MEDLINE, PubMed, CINAHL, PEDro, and the Cochrane Library was conducted for studies published between October 2019 and March 2025. Results: Eight studies met the inclusion criteria, spanning qualitative, observational, Delphi, and survey methodologies. Conducted in the UK, Italy, the US, and Canada, they captured perspectives from people with cystic fibrosis, caregivers, and healthcare professionals. Three themes emerged: (1) a marked reduction in the perceived necessity and burden of airway clearance techniques f...
secondary_themes: Care burden, adherence, and psychosocial impact
records.why_it_matters: Evidence mapping shows airway clearance is being reduced after modulators, but long-term safety remains unresolved.
records.confidence_reason: Structured review abstract reports search strategy, included studies, themes, and need for larger trials.

records.title: Promixin to generic colistimethate switching: worth the challenge?
records.summary: Background: Guidelines recommended inhalation challenges for new inhaled antibiotics, but their role is unclear in switches of preparations of the same drug. We therefore explored outcomes of inhalation challenges in all switches from Promixin to generic Colistimethate at our large adult CF centre. Methods: A retrospective analysis of inhalation challenges was undertaken for consecutive inhalation challenges between January-December 2025. Lung function changes and subsequent treatment initiation decisions were captured. Results: Inhalation challenges completed in 41 pwCF (30 male (71%), median age was 32yrs [range 18-60yrs], median ppFEV1 of 76% [range 30-124]) were performed and included. Median FEV1 drop aft...
secondary_themes: Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
records.why_it_matters: Colistimethate preparation switches rarely caused clinically meaningful bronchoconstriction, questioning routine test-dose challenges.
records.confidence_reason: Partial abstract reports challenge outcomes and management decisions, but the full structured record is incomplete.

records.title: A qualitative study of children with cystic fibrosis and their parents’ understanding of terminology used by the multidisciplinary team in a UK shared care paediatric cystic fibrosis centre
records.summary: Background: Mucus, phlegm, secretions, and sputum are terms used interchangeably within the Cystic Fibrosis (CF) multidisciplinary team (MDT) alongside descriptions of wet and productive cough. These are understood to have the same meaning. It is unclear if parents and their child with CF (CwCF) share this understanding and whether using one or two standardised terms would improve communication. Objectives: To understand parent and CwCF’s interpretation of terms and identify preferred terms. Method: Two questionnaires were devised, one sent to 30 parents and one sent to 16 CwCF (aged 6-16 years). Results were analysed to determine if the MDT language was understood and if there was a preferred term. Results: 4...
records.primary_theme: Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
secondary_themes: Care burden, adherence, and psychosocial impact
records.why_it_matters: Standardizing child-friendly respiratory terminology may improve communication among children, parents, and CF teams.
records.confidence_reason: Structured qualitative abstract describes parent and child questionnaires, response rates, term preferences, and MDT implications.

records.title: Elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor treatment reduces hypertonic saline inhalation-related treatment burden
records.summary: Objectives: Treatment with elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor (ETI) has proven effective in many ways for patients with CF but there is a lack of real-world data regarding effects on treatment burden in Swedish people with CF (pwCF). The aim of this study was to investigate if hypertonic saline inhalation-related treatment burden decreases during the first 12 months of ETI treatment in pwCF followed at Stockholm CF Center. Methods: Data was collected from the Stockholm ETI Task Force study database, from the patient charts and from the National Swedish CF registry before ETI initiation and three and twelve months after initiation of ETI treatment. Statistical analysis was performed with GraphPad. Descriptive sta...
secondary_themes: Care burden, adherence, and psychosocial impact
records.why_it_matters: ETI reduced hypertonic-saline inhalation frequency, documenting real-world treatment-burden change that needs safety follow-up.
records.confidence_reason: Structured abstract reports Swedish cohort, inhalation frequency at baseline, three months, and twelve months.

records.title: Recognition of poor quality Pulmonary Function Tests (PFTs) and re-education of upper airway flow to achieve optimal results
records.summary: Objectives: Pulmonary Function Tests (PFTs) are the gold standard of monitoring Cystic Fibrosis, used to assess lung disease severity, track progression, evaluate treatment efficiency and predict prognosis. However, technical errors occur with many patients, such as inconsistent effort, glottis closure, cough and tongue obstruction, adversely affecting their results. Technical errors were observed in 3 patients attending the National Referral Centre for Adult Cystic Fibrosis here at St. Vincents University Hospital. Method: We identified 3 patients in our unit who were clinically stable but had either chronically poor PFT technique with substantial variability or unusually low PFT results. Standardised PFTs we...
records.why_it_matters: Physiotherapy assessment of upper-airway flow can distinguish poor PFT technique from true clinical decline.
records.confidence_reason: Structured case abstract reports three adults, re-education intervention, repeated PFTs, and improved test quality.

records.title: Observational pilot study: tele-yoga in adults with cystic fibrosis as a complementary integration to conventional therapeutic practice
records.summary: Background This study was designed as an observational pilot project to assess the potential benefits of a tele-yoga program, minimizing the risk of cross-infection that prevents in-person meetings among patients with this condition. Objective To explore the potential benefits of tele-yoga for adults with cystic fibrosis as a complementary approach to conventional therapeutic care. Methods A tele-yoga program was offered to 7 patients followed at the Marche Cystic Fibrosis Center and consisted of 25 weekly live sessions, each lasting 60 minutes, delivered through a shared online platform. Patients were assessed at baseline (T0), three months (T1), and six months (T2) using spirometry, the Cystic Fibrosis Quest...
secondary_themes: Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
records.why_it_matters: Tele-yoga may offer a lower-exposure adjunct to conventional care, but the available abstract evidence is sparse.
records.confidence_reason: Pilot design is clear, but the accessible record gives limited outcome detail.

records.title: Blowing things out of proportion? Spirometry outcomes in clinic compared to formal lung function testing in children and young adults with Cystic Fibrosis
records.summary: Background: Spirometry is critical to monitoring lung health in people with cystic fibrosis (pwCF) . It is commonly performed during outpatient clinic visits, where children are often fatigued, distracted, or preoccupied. Formal laboratory-based spirometry offers a quieter, more focussed setting and may provide more accurate results. Methods: Formal laboratory-based spirometry (Jaeger Masterscreen PFT) was obtained in 25 pwCF aged 6-18 years, and compared to matched routine clinic-based spirometry (Spirolab, MIR, Italy) from preceding and subsequent scheduled clinics. All testing took place in clinically well patients. Pairwise non-parametric tests (Wilcoxon Signed Rank Test) were used to compare percent predi...
secondary_themes: Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
records.why_it_matters: Clinic spirometry may underestimate lung function versus formal lab testing, affecting monitoring and treatment decisions.
records.confidence_reason: Structured abstract reports methods, sample size, comparative results, and conclusion.

records.title: Breath expectations: lung function response to GLP1 use in adults with cystic fibrosis
records.summary: Objectives: In people with cystic fibrosis (pwCF) diabetes is common and prevalence of obesity is increasing. Both are known to affect lung function. Glucagon-like peptide 1 agonist receptor (GLP1) medications are proven therapies for both conditions in the general population and not contraindicated in pwCF. Our aim was to explore the impact of GLP1 on pwCF’s lung function, BMI and glycaemic control in our adult population. Method: Adult pwCF commencing a GLP1 were identified. Pre and post GLP1 data included BMI, HbA1c, and ppFEV1. Data was gathered retrospectively and statistical analysis using excel included change pre and post GLP1 in ppFEV1, BMI and HbA1c respectively. Correlation of change in ppFEV1 was e...
records.why_it_matters: Early real-world GLP1 experience suggests metabolic treatment choices could also affect respiratory outcomes in adults with CF.
records.confidence_reason: Structured results are available, but the cohort is very small.

records.title: Clinical and demographic factors associated with rate of pulmonary exacerbations in adults with cystic fibrosis: A UK Cystic Fibrosis Registry Study
records.summary: Objectives: Pulmonary exacerbations remain a major driver of morbidity and mortality in cystic fibrosis (CF), yet the mechanisms underpinning them are poorly understood. We used national registry data to identify patient factors associated with higher exacerbation risk. Methods: UK CF Registry data from 4,654 adults were analysed. Demographic, socioeconomic, and clinical factors associated with exacerbation were examined using negative binomial regression, reported as incidence rate ratios (IRR) with 95% confidence intervals. Results: Analysis was conducted on 2,507 individuals with complete data (2021–2023 pooled and 2023 cross-sectional). Higher exacerbation rates were strongly associated with female sex, sm...
secondary_themes: Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
records.why_it_matters: Registry risk stratification links exacerbations to clinical comorbidity and socioeconomic disadvantage.
records.confidence_reason: Large registry analysis with adjusted associations and clear findings.

records.title: Demographic and clinical characteristics of children with CF aged 2-5 years initiating ELX/TEZ/IVA in LONGITUDE – a UK CF Registry observational study
records.summary: Objectives: LONGITUDE is a retrospective cohort study using UK Cystic Fibrosis (CF) Registry data to evaluate real-world outcomes in people with CF initiating elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor (ETI) treatment. We report demographic and clinical characteristics for children with CF (cwCF) aged 2-5 years at ETI initiation. Methods: Analyses included children aged 2-5 years initiating ETI (index) between August 2019 (early access program) and December 2024 (latest data available) with ≥1 F508del allele and ≥1 annual review within 14 months pre-index. Baseline characteristics were obtained from the latest annual review pre-index. Genotype, prior CF transmembrane conductance regulator modulator (CFTRm) treatment, fe...
records.why_it_matters: Baseline data show substantial multisystem disease burden before ETI in very young children.
records.confidence_reason: Structured registry abstract provides cohort definition and detailed baseline characteristics.

records.title: Social inequalities in eligibility and use of elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor (ETI): evidence from the UK Cystic Fibrosis Registry, 2020-2024
records.summary: Objectives: Elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor (ETI), a triple CFTR modulator therapy licensed in the UK in 2020, has been shown to be a highly effective treatment for people with cystic fibrosis (pwCF). Some groups may be ineligible for CFTR modulators, impacting health inequalities in treatment access and outcomes. This study aimed to assess social inequalities in eligibility and use of ETI amongst pwCF in the UK. Methods: We conducted an observational cohort study using data from the UK Cystic Fibrosis Registry (2020–2024). ETI eligibility was defined as meeting National Health Service (NHS) commissioning criteria for licensed or off-label use during each year, and ETI use as being prescribed ETI in that year...
records.primary_theme: Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
records.why_it_matters: ETI use appears equitable once eligible, but deprivation affects eligibility itself.
records.confidence_reason: Large longitudinal registry analysis reports eligibility, uptake, and adjusted deprivation effects.

records.title: Impact of CFTR modulator therapy on pulmonary structure and lung function in children and adolescents with cystic fibrosis
records.summary: Objectives: Real-world data on CFTR modulator effects on ventilation inhomogeneity and structural lung disease in children remain scarce. This study evaluated short- and long-term effects of lumacaftor/ivacaftor (LUM/IVA) and elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor (ETI) in children and adolescents with Cystic Fibrosis (CF) using chest computed tomography (CT) analyses and lung function tests. Methods: This retrospective cohort study included CFTR modulator–naïve individuals with cystic fibrosis followed at the Gothenburg CF centre between 2018 and 2025 who had undergone at least one chest CT scan. Chest CT scans were analysed using AI-based methods, and lung function data were retrieved from the Swedish National CF...
records.why_it_matters: CT and lung-function measures give complementary evidence of pediatric structural response to different modulators.
records.confidence_reason: Structured retrospective cohort includes imaging, lung-function endpoints, and longitudinal modelling.

records.title: Lost in transcription: Bringing structure to free-text genotype data in the UK Cystic Fibrosis Registry
records.summary: Objectives: The UKCFR is a secure, centralised database. Containing demographic and routinely collected health data for consenting people with CF. Genotype data are entered by CF centres via a predefined drop-down list, which was expanded in 2024 to include 1,167 CFTR variants using CFTR2. As this list is not exhaustive and users are not necessarily genetics specialists, a free-text option allows variants to be entered when required. Routine cleaning of free-text entries is undertaken to improve data quality and standardisation. Methods: Following extension of the UKCFR list of CFTR variants, free-text entries were analysed to identify variants that could be matched to the updated variant database. An automate...
secondary_themes: Precision CFTR biology and rare variants
records.why_it_matters: Standardizing free-text genotype entries improves registry quality and rare-variant interpretability.
records.confidence_reason: Objective and approach are clear, but the extracted summary gives limited visible results.

records.title: Survival of people with cystic fibrosis in South Africa
records.summary: Objectives: Survival data from Cystic Fibrosis (CF) registries demonstrate improved prognosis for individuals with CF. This study estimates the projected survival age of people living with CF in South Africa. Methods: A retrospective cohort study was conducted using prospectively collected data from CF centres participating in the South African Cystic Fibrosis Registry (SACFR) from 2018 to 2024. A period survival analysis, utilising five-year rolling windows and the Kaplan-Meier method, was used to calculate the median age of survival for 2019–2023 and 2020–2024. A Cox proportional hazards model was used to assess impact on survival. Results: Among 611 individuals followed by the SACFR, 62 (10.1%) deaths and 1...

records.title: Registry-based survival trends in cystic fibrosis across Central Europe from 2010 to 2024
records.summary: Objectives: Life expectancy of people with cystic fibrosis (pwCF) has improved over recent decades, but comprehensive population-level data comparing survival gains before and after introduction of CFTR modulators (CFTRm) in Europe are limited. Methods: Data from 4,048 pwCF of three Central European countries (the Netherlands, Switzerland and Austria) were analysed using national CF registry data. records.title: Impact of elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor on utilization of inhaled therapies in cystic fibrosis and association with clinical outcomes
records.summary: Objectives: To evaluate changes in adherence to inhaled antibiotics and mucolytics following elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor (ETI) initiation and associations with clinical outcomes in cystic fibrosis (CF). records.title: Longitudinal outcomes in people with cystic fibrosis treated with elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor: a two-year comparative analysis from the Italian cystic fibrosis registry
records.summary: Objectives: To compare longitudinal clinical trends in people with cystic fibrosis (pwCF) treated or not treated with elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor (ETI) using Italian Cystic Fibrosis Registry data over the two years before and after 2022. Methods: Registry data from 2022 were analyzed for pwCF not treated with ETI (No ETI; n=1830; 53% male; 44.5% pancreatic insufficient (PI)) and pwCF initiating ETI in 2022 (ETI; n=621; 47% male; 75% PI). records.title: Longitudinal clinical outcomes of N1303K cystic fibrosis patients in the era of CFTR modulators
records.summary: Background: The N1303K variant is one of the most second frequent variant and is associated with a severe cystic fibrosis (CF) phenotype. Longitudinal real-world data on clinical outcomes in patients carrying the N1303K variant remain limited, particularly in the context of evolving regulatory approval and access to CFTR modulator therapies. Objectives: To describe long-term clinical characteristics and pulmonary function changes in patients with the N1303K CFTR variant and to compare these findings with F508del homozygous patients. records.title: Clinical, genetic, and epidemiological characterisation of cystic fibrosis in Morocco: foundations for a national paediatric registry aligned with international standards
records.summary: Background: Cystic fibrosis (CF) has long been considered rare in Morocco; however, its apparent scarcity largely reflects underdiagnosis linked to the absence of newborn screening, limited access to sweat testing, and restricted availability of molecular analyses. The lack of a national registry hampers accurate assessment of disease burden and standardization of pediatric care. Objectives: To characterize the clinical, genetic, and epidemiological profiles of patients followed in five Moroccan centers and to assess the operational feasibility of implementing a national CF registry aligned with international standards. records.title: Results of an on-site data validation in the cystic fibrosis registry of Türkiye for 2024 data
records.summary: Objective: The Cystic Fibrosis Registry of Türkiye(CFRT) was established in 2017 and contains longitudinal demographic and clinical data on people living with cystic fibrosis(CF).This study aimed to evaluate the quality of records in the CFRT through on-site data verification visits within the framework of the European Cystic Fibrosis Society Patient Registry project,which aims to implement measures to improve data reliability for registry based research. records.title: Demographic and socioeconomic characteristics of adults with cystic fibrosis: annual registry from an Argentinian cystic fibrosis center
records.summary: Objective: To describe the demographic, educational, employment, and family characteristics of adult patients with cystic fibrosis (CF) attending a specialized center in Argentina during 2025. records.title: Characteristics of adult pwCF in Kazakhstan
records.summary: Background: Since 2023, data has been collected to maintain the CF Registry in Kazakhstan in accordance with ECFSPR standards. Objective: To present the clinical characteristics of adult pwCF in Kazakhstan in 2025. Methods: By the end of 2025, 252 pwCF were registered, 37 of them (14.68%) were 18 years of age and older. Results: 37 adults (20 men), aged 23.94 ± 4.95 (min 18, max 45), median age at diagnosis 13.2 years (min 1, max 36 years). Six patients reached the age of 18 in 2025. records.title: Uruguayan experience with the use of CFTR modulators in the paediatric population
records.summary: Background: CFTR modulators have significantly changed the clinical course of cystic fibrosis (CF), however, there is limited evidence in the Latin American pediatric population Objective: To evaluate the clinical effects of lumacaftor/ivacaftor and elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor after one year of treatment in children with CF in Uruguay. records.title: Real-word clinical effectiveness of elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor on children with cystic fibrosis: a prospective registry analysis
records.summary: Background: Long-term clinical trials of ETI have demonstrated sustained improvements in lung function, nutritional status, symptoms, and sweat chloride levels in people with cystic fibrosis (CF). Methods: This single-centre prospective registry-based study enrolled children with CF on ETI therapy. records.title: Respiratory morbidity and chronic respiratory infections in cystic fibrosis patients from the Republic of Moldova
records.summary: Chronic respiratory infections and pulmonary exacerbations remain a major driver of morbidity and mortality in cystic fibrosis (CF). records.summary: Objectives: Elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor (ETI) has transformed cystic fibrosis (CF) care, representing a major therapeutic milestone. records.title: Characteristics of patients with cystic fibrosis in the Russian Federation according to the 2024 registry data
records.summary: Objective: To describe the characteristics of patients (pts) with cystic fibrosis and the scope of therapy. records.title: Mortality in cystic fibrosis: analysis of causes and predictors of death in patients from the Republic of Moldova
records.summary: Objectives: Cystic fibrosis(CF) is a multisystem genetic disease associated with significant morbidity and mortality, mainly due to progressive lung damage and infectious complications. Despite therapeutic advances and the introduction of CFTR modulator therapies, death still occurs at young ages. Mortality analysis provides valuable insight into the natural course of the disease and its clinical and imaging prognostic factors. The aim is to evaluate mortality characteristics among patients with cystic fibrosis in the Republic of Moldova. records.title: Baseline nutritional and pulmonary status in Ukrainian individuals with cystic fibrosis prior to CFTR modulator introduction: registry-based analysis
records.summary: Objectives: Nutritional status and lung function (LF) are key prognostic factors in cystic fibrosis (CF). records.title: Effects of air pollution on pulmonary exacerbations: longitudinal analysis of the CF registry
records.summary: Objectives: Cystic fibrosis (CF) exhibits heterogeneous lung disease progression, which may be worsened by environmental exposures such as air pollution. We aimed to evaluate the non-linear and age-specific effects of PM₁₀, PM₂.₅, and NO₂ on pulmonary exacerbations in children with CF using data from the French CF Registry. Methods: Data from 909 children (ages 6–12) were analyzed. Pulmonary exacerbations were modeled with a negative binomial generalized linear mixed model accounting for nested clustering. Non-linear pollutant effects and age interactions were assessed using departmental air quality data. records.why_it_matters: Age-specific pollution effects on exacerbations could inform environmental risk counselling for children with CF.

records.title: Regional differences in waitlist and lung transplant outcomes for adults with cystic fibrosis: the United States experience
records.summary: Objective: Cystic fibrosis (CF) is a key indication for lung transplantation (LT). Despite medical advances, many adults develop end-stage lung disease and require LT. The geographical impact on access to LT is unclear. We aimed to quantify differences in waitlist outcomes by geographical region, as defined by the United Network for Organ Sharing (UNOS). Methods: We utilized the UNOS database, including adults listed for single-organ LT from January 2005 to November 2023. We analyzed baseline characteristics with Chi-square and Kruskal-Wallis tests. records.title: Your Life and CF: a longitudinal summary
records.summary: Background: For four years, Cystic Fibrosis Trust (CFT) has run Your Life and CF, an annual survey capturing the lived experiences of people with cystic fibrosis (CF) across the UK. While triple-therapy CFTR modulators have improved clinical outcomes for many, CF remains a lifelong, multisystem condition requiring intensive specialist care. Significant financial pressures, treatment burden, and psychosocial challenges persist. Your Life and CF builds on the 2022 Cost of CF baseline to track these experiences over time. records.title: Impact of parenthood on pulmonary exacerbation frequency among people with cystic fibrosis: findings from the HOPeCF retrospective cohort study
records.summary: Objective: Using Health Outcomes of Parents with CF (HOPeCF) retrospective data, we examined pulmonary exacerbation (PEx) rates among parents and nonparents with CF (PwCF; NPwCF). records.title: Pregnancy care and outcomes in patients with cystic fibrosis at a UK specialist centre
records.summary: Objectives: Advances in cystic fibrosis (CF) therapies, including highly effective modulator therapy, have resulted in increased pregnancies among people with CF (pwCF). Currently there are no clear guidelines on the management of pregnancy in pwCF. The Oxford Adult Cystic Fibrosis Centre (OACFC) provides regional specialist care for pwCF. We aimed to evaluate pregnancy management, and maternal and infant outcomes in pwCF under our care. records.title: Pregnancy under elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor: frequency, outcomes and impact on clinical practice in a single adult cystic fibrosis centre
records.summary: Background: The introduction of elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor (ETI) has improved health and fertility in women with cystic fibrosis (CF). records.title: Effects of discontinuing inhaled corticosteroids: a target trial emulation using the UK Cystic Fibrosis Registry
records.summary: Objectives: Inhaled corticosteroids (ICS) remain commonly prescribed in cystic fibrosis (CF) despite limited evidence of benefit and substantial treatment burden. We aimed to estimate the causal effect of discontinuing ICS on clinical outcomes using real-world data, applying a target trial emulation framework. Methods: We conducted a population-based cohort study using UK CF Registry data (2016-2018), emulating a target trial based on CF-WISE. records.title: Investigating CFTR modulator effectiveness with target trial emulation: lessons from the Dutch CF Registry
records.summary: Objectives: CFTR modulator therapy, such as elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor (ETI), has greatly improved treatment of people with Cystic Fibrosis (pwCF). records.title: Incidence, prevalence and patterns of Stenotrophomonas maltophilia infection in people with cystic fibrosis across Europe
records.summary: Introduction: Stenotrophomonas maltophilia (SM) is increasingly detected in people with cystic fibrosis (pwCF), but its clinical significance remains uncertain. records.title: UNC bug bank and the NTM cohort experience: automated integration of clinical data of NTM isolates in CF and non-CF cohorts
records.summary: Objectives: Access to bacterial isolates and clinical data is essential for advancing research, yet researchers often face challenges. records.summary: Objectives: To assess whether elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor (ETI) is associated with decreased odds of respiratory pathogens P. aeruginosa and methicillin-sensitive Staphylococcus aureus (MSSA) in 6-17-year-old persons with cystic fibrosis (pwCF). records.title: Antimicrobial coatings on adhesive copper tapes as a method for preventing hospital-acquired infections and cross-contamination of patients
records.summary: Nosocomial infections (NIs) remain a major healthcare challenge and pose a major threat to cystic fibrosis patients. records.title: Where do we stand in immunisation? Vaccination status and attitudes in cystic fibrosis
records.summary: Objectives: Cystic fibrosis (CF) is a lifelong genetic disease in which respiratory infections substantially contribute to morbidity and mortality.
