records.summary: Objectives: A 5 year follow-up of US participants in The International Depression Epidemiological Study (TIDES) found a significant increase in mortality among people with CF (PWCF) who screened positive for depression, especially those with severe symptoms. records.title: Evaluation and updating of a prediction model for advanced cystic fibrosis liver disease in children: a 10-year analysis from the Irish longitudinal cohort
records.summary: Objectives: Advanced cystic fibrosis liver disease (aCFLD) is a major cause of morbidity and mortality in people with cystic fibrosis (CF). Identification of those at risk of aCFLD may facilitate earlier management and potentially avoid complications. The objective was to validate a previously published predictor model for aCFLD in a prospective dataset. Methods: This study is a prospective cohort of children with CF, with follow-up conducted every 5 years. Liver-focused data are collected at each visit. Primary outcome is the development of aCFLD. records.title: Elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor corrects hippocampal deficits in humanized mice models of cystic fibrosis
records.summary: Objective: To investigate whether elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor (ETI) improves hippocampal function in humanized cystic fibrosis (CF) mice, by modulating chloride homeostasis and synaptic activity. Our study provides a potential insight for the neuropsychiatric symptoms reported in people with CF. Methods: Hippocampal tissue was obtained from humanized CFTR mice (male, 13-18 weeks) expressing either wild-type CFTR (hWT) or homozygous F508del CFTR (hCF). ETI exposure was either in vitro (48 hours) or in vivo (4 days). records.summary: Objectives: Neurodiversity refers to differences in brain function and behavioural traits, including conditions such as attention deficit hyperactivity disorder (ADHD) and autism. Emerging studies suggest higher rates in pwCF than in the general population and extra burden/ barriers to healthcare. We developed a care pathway to help navigate support for neurodivergent patients who may experience additional challenges to daily life, function and CF management. records.title: Exercise capacity vs. sleep time in pwCF vs. healthy controls
records.summary: Background: Low exercise capacity is associated with increased morbidity and mortality in the general population and people with Cystic fibrosis (pwCF). In previous studies, reduced sleep led to decreased lung functions in pwCF, while sleep disorders were associated with exercise intolerance and pulmonary exacerbations. Our aim was to assess effects of sleep time and disturbed sleep (nocturnal cough and snoring) on exercise capacity using cardio-pulmonary exercise testing (CPET) in young pwCF compared to healthy controls. records.title: Quantifying CF gut dysbiosis: a reproducible and treatment-responsive microbiome index
records.summary: Objectives: Gut microbial dysbiosis is common in cystic fibrosis but is typically assessed using non-specific diversity metrics or study‑specific taxonomic signatures, limiting comparability across studies and interventions. We developed a metagenomic, species-level Cystic Fibrosis Microbial Dysbiosis Index (CF-MDI) to enable quantitative assessment of gut microbial imbalance in children with CF (CwCF). Methods: Stool metagenomic data from the PEARL-CF study (ACTRN12616000797471), including CwCF and age- and gender-matched healthy controls (HC), were analysed using MaAsLin2 to identify taxa enriched or depleted in CF (q<0.05, |coefficient| ≥ 4). records.title: Multi-omics analysis of airway epithelial cells following elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor (ETI) therapy
records.summary: Objectives: ETI restores anion transport in the most common F508del CFTR mutant; however, some cystic fibrosis (CF) patients continue to experience lung infection and inflammation after 3.5 years of treatment. The variability in phenotype-genotype correlations in patient lung suggests that additional genetic or environmental factors influence clinical outcomes. Here, we aim to identify genome and epigenome changes in airway epithelial cells following CFTR rescue. Methods: Primary Human Bronchial Epithelial Cell (HBEC) cultures were generated from 6 CF patients and 5 non-CF (NCF) donors. 1 CF patient (referred to as CF1) carried the “2184ΔA/W128A” mutation, while 5 patients were homozygous for F508del (referred...

records.title: Immune-modulatory effects of CFTR triple therapy in cystic fibrosis
records.summary: Cystic fibrosis (CF) is a life-limiting genetic disease associated with altered immune regulation. CFTR triple modulator therapy (elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor, ETI) improves clinical outcomes in people with CF (pwCF). However, its impact on adaptive immunity remains unclear. We analyzed peripheral blood CD4⁺ T cells from healthy controls (n=22) and pwCF (n=21) at baseline and 3, 6, and 9 months after ETI initiation. CD4⁺ T cells were immunophenotyped by flow cytometry directly after isolation and after 48 hours in vitro culture with or without activation (αCD3/CD28). records.title: 'Knowing I am different makes me strong': a UK survey of children and young people with cystic fibrosis
records.summary: We aimed to find out what was important to children and young people with CF in the UK. We wanted to hear their feelings and thoughts about CF, as well as the positive things in their lives. We ran one survey for children aged 6-11 and another for those aged 12-17. We worked with our Youth Advisory Group to write the questions, based on topics they felt strongly about. 166 children responded. Respondents gave us an insight into the richness and positivity of their lives. Their hobbies and sources of pride are exciting and diverse. “The best thing about life is knowing that I am different to others. records.summary: Gastrointestinal responses to elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor (ETI) therapy in individuals with cystic fibrosis (CF) have shown variability across studies. records.title: Synonymous CFTR variants contribute to functional variability in human nasal epithelial cells
records.summary: Objectives: Human nasal epithelial (hNE) cells are a reliable ex-vivo model to study CFTR function. Defining reference CFTR activity in non-CF individuals is crucial to identify subtle functional alterations and to evaluate CFTR rescue strategies. This study aimed to characterize CFTR functional variability in hNE cultures and explore potential genetic determinants of reduced activity. Methods: hNE cells were collected from ~50 non-CF donors and differentiated in air–liquid interface. CFTR-dependent chloride secretion was measured by short-circuit current under symmetrical chloride (SC) and after imposing a chloride gradient (GC) to enhance CFTR-mediated anion transport. CFTR gene analysis by NGS was performed...
records.primary_theme: Precision CFTR biology and rare variants
records.why_it_matters: Human nasal epithelial models showed that even synonymous CFTR variants may modulate channel function, strengthening personalized functional assessment.
records.confidence_reason: Structured objectives, methods, results, and conclusion describe model, genetic findings, and interpretation.

records.title: Functional characterization of rare variants c.4055A>C and c.3352T>C in the CFTR gene using an intestinal organoids model
records.summary: Objective: To define the functional impact and therapeutic potential of two uncharacterized rare CFTR variants, c.4055A>C (p.Q1352P) and c.3352T>C (p.S1118P), using a patient-derived intestinal organoid model. These specific variants have not been previously described in major cystic fibrosis databases. Method: We utilized the forskolin-induced swelling assay in intestinal organoids from patients with genotypes c.1392+1G>A/c.4055A>C and c.3528delC/c.3352T>C. Organoids homozygous for F508del were used as a control. Functional response was quantified before and after treatment with CFTR modulators: ivacaftor (IVA), lumacaftor/ivacaftor (LUM/IVA), tezacaftor/ivacaftor (TEZ/IVA), and elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaft...
records.primary_theme: Precision CFTR biology and rare variants
records.why_it_matters: Patient-derived organoids helped classify two rare variants as mild and predicted strong modulator responsiveness.
records.confidence_reason: Structured abstract gives variant-specific model, comparator, modulator testing, and conclusions.

records.title: In the presence of the D110H mutation: how is the clinical course shaped?
records.summary: Objectives: The D110H mutation is a rare variant on exon 4 of the cystic fibrosis transmembrane conductance regulator (CFTR) gene. It is more prevalent in Southern Europe, Turkey, Italy, and Iran, and has generally been associated with pseudo-Bartter syndrome (PBS) in the literature. While D110H is defined as "CF-causing" in the CFTR2 database, it is classified as "disease-causing with varying clinical consequence" in CFTR-Fr. This study evaluates the clinical and laboratory findings of cystic fibrosis (CF) patients with the D110H mutation to assess its impact on disease progression. Methods: We retrospectively analysed 24 years of data from 21 patients carrying the D110H mutation. Results: Median age at diagn...
records.primary_theme: Precision CFTR biology and rare variants
records.why_it_matters: Long-term D110H data link phenotype to the accompanying allele, supporting careful genotype interpretation and follow-up.
records.confidence_reason: Structured retrospective cohort includes clinical features, follow-up, and genotype-phenotype conclusions.

records.title: Clinical impact of large CFTR gene deletions and duplications in children with cystic fibrosis: A national registry–based study
records.summary: Objectives: Large deletions and duplications (copy number variations, CNVs) in the CFTR gene are rare in cystic fibrosis (CF) compared with common mutations. This study aimed to evaluate the frequency of large CFTR CNVs in Türkiye and their clinical impact using data from the National Cystic Fibrosis Registry (NCFR). Methods: NCFR data from 2024 were retrospectively analyzed. Among 2,150 CF patients followed in 36 centers, those with identified large CFTR CNVs were included. Demographic, clinical, microbiological, and treatment-related characteristics were recorded. Results: A total of 43 patients (2.00%) had large CNVs, 53.4% of whom were male. Median (min-max) age at diagnosis was 0.33 (min-max: 0.08-9) year...
records.primary_theme: Precision CFTR biology and rare variants
records.why_it_matters: National registry data show large CFTR CNVs are uncommon but linked with meaningful pediatric disease burden.
records.confidence_reason: Structured registry analysis reports sample frame, frequency, clinical characteristics, and conclusion.

records.title: Clinical significance of 5T polymorphism genotype in a cohort of cystic fibrosis Portuguese patients
records.summary: Objectives: A common genetic variant among patients with cystic fibrosis (CF) is 5T. This variant exhibits a highly variable clinical phenotype, particularly when present in combination with other CFTR alleles. The aim of this study was to characterize the clinical and genetic features of Portuguese patients carrying this variant. Methods: We conducted a retrospective analysis of children and adults followed in 5 CF- Reference Centers in Portugal, who carried at least one 5T variant in trans with another CFTR variant. Results: Fifteen patients were included (7 males), with a median age 22 years [IQR 13–45], and median age at diagnosis of 13 years [IQR 3–33]; two patients had a positive neonatal screening. Eigh...
records.primary_theme: Precision CFTR biology and rare variants
records.why_it_matters: A Portuguese cohort shows the 5T variant can carry clinically relevant respiratory involvement, reinforcing full genotype interpretation.
records.confidence_reason: Structured multicentre retrospective data support the theme and population classification.

records.title: Body composition in patients with cystic fibrosis and its association with polymorphic variants of energy metabolism genes: PPARG, PPARD, UCP2, UCP3.
records.summary: Objective: To conduct an association study of PPARG, PPARD, UCP2, and UCP3 gene variants with nutritional status and body composition in children with cystic fibrosis (CF). Methods: Patients with CF (F508del/F508del) (n=478) aged 0 to 18 years (11.8 ± 3.8 years). Body composition was analyzed before and after 1 year of targeted therapy. Polymorphisms (SNPs) in the PPARG, PPARD, UCP2, and UCP3 genes were tested using PCR and RFLP analysis. Results: After 1 year of targeted therapy, body weight in boys significantly increased by 5.3 (±2.5) kg and height by 5.7 (±1.7) cm (p<0.01), while in girls, body weight increased by 4.5 (±1.9) kg and height by 4.8 (±1.3) cm (p<0.01). In boys, %FM increased from -0.23 to 0.13...
secondary_themes: Precision CFTR biology and rare variants
records.why_it_matters: Energy-metabolism polymorphisms may help explain body composition changes after targeted therapy in children with CF.
records.confidence_reason: Structured abstract gives cohort size, genetic associations, and body composition outcomes.

records.title: Vanzacaftor/tezacaftor/deutivacaftor versus elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor in two children homozygous for the complex allele p.Leu467Phe-p.Phe508del
records.summary: Objectives: Homozygosity for the complex allele p.Leu467Phe-p.Phe508del (L467F-F508del) is associated with failure to respond to elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor (ETI) in vitro and a below average response in vivo. Clinical and functional CFTR response to the newly available vanzacaftor/tezacaftor/deutivacaftor (VTD) in vivo in homozygous carriers of these mutations is unknown. We report clinical and functional responses towards ETI and VTD of two L467F-F508del homozygous children. records.title: Ivacaftor versus elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor in CFTR gating mutations in combination with a class I variant: insights from patient-derived intestinal organoids
records.summary: Objectives: To evaluate the potential added benefit of elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor (ETI) and vanzacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor (VTI) compared to ivacaftor in people with cystic fibrosis (pwCF) carrying a class III and class I mutation, and to assess whether patient-derived intestinal organoids (PDIOs) can inform individualised modulator therapy decisions. Methods: PDIOs were generated from 4 pwCF carrying a CFTR class III mutation in trans with a class I mutation: G178R/Q493X (case 1), S1251N/G542X (case 2), R347H/2183AA>G (case 3) and Q359R/1717-1G>A (case 4). secondary_themes: Precision CFTR biology and rare variants

records.title: Differential response to elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor in children and adolescents with cystic fibrosis: a two-year real-world comparison by CFTR genotype
records.summary: Background: Elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor (ETI) has revolutionized cystic fibrosis (CF) care; however, its efficacy in pediatric subjects carrying one ETI-responsive and one non-responsive CFTR variant remains incompletely characterized. This study compared the realworld response to ETI using sweat chloride concentration (SCC) as a key biomarker of CFTR function. Methods: In this retrospective, single-center study, pediatric subjects aged 6–18 years, carrying at least one p.Phe508del variant and on ETI, were included. Participants were stratified by genotype into those with two responsive variants or one responsive variant. SCC, percent predicted FEV1 (ppFEV1), BMI z-score, and pulmonary exacerbations were...
secondary_themes: Precision CFTR biology and rare variants
records.why_it_matters: Genotype-stratified pediatric ETI data show sweat chloride may detect biochemical differences before clinical outcomes diverge.
records.confidence_reason: Structured abstract includes cohort, follow-up timepoints, genotype groups, and outcome comparisons.

records.title: Alterations in lung function and lung ventilation after commencement of elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor in a paediatric cohort
records.summary: Background: X-ray Velocimetry (XV) is a functional X-ray imaging method that measures local lung ventilation during breathing. XV technology has been validated in animal models and is FDA approved for use in adult humans. In Australia elexacaftor+tezacaftor+ivacaftor (ETI) was approved for children aged 2-6 years in August 2024. The aim of this study was to assess how XV ventilation parameters change in response to ETI. Methods: This prospective, single-center cohort study aimed to recruit children that were eligible to commence ETI. Children had XV imaging and pulmonary function testing (spirometry and nitrogen multiple breath washout) performed prior to starting ETI, and at 1-3 months post-ETI commencement....
records.why_it_matters: X-ray velocimetry may detect regional ventilation abnormalities in young children starting ETI despite normal standard lung tests.
records.confidence_reason: Structured partial abstract provides prospective design, paired imaging, and preliminary findings.

records.title: Assessing lung function in adults and children with cystic fibrosis receiving highly effective modulator therapy using 129Xe MRI and pulmonary function testing
records.summary: Objectives: To use 129Xe ventilation MRI and pulmonary function tests to assess lung function in people with cystic fibrosis (CF) receiving highly effective modulator therapy (HEMT). Methods: Ten children (M=6, F=4) and 18 adults with CF (M=10, F=8) were assessed using Xe-MRI, spirometry and lung clearance index (LCI). records.title: Adherence with baseline and follow-up screening for cataracts in patients on modulators containing ivacaftor.
records.summary: Objectives: Ivacaftor-containing CFTR modulators from infancy are standard care for most children with cystic fibrosis (CF). Current guidance recommends pre-treatment (baseline) ophthalmology screening and repeat screening after one year. records.title: Cystic fibrosis newborn screening pilot study in Estonia
records.summary: Introduction: Until 2023, Cystic Fibrosis (CF) Newborn Screening (NBS) was not available in Estonia and diagnosis was often delayed. To improve early diagnosis and access to treatment, a national pilot CF NBS programme was initiated on 1 April 2023. Objective: To evaluate and summarise the outcomes of the Estonian CF NBS pilot programme. Methods: We retrospectively analysed data from the CF NBS pilot conducted in 2023–2025. records.title: Twelve years of newborn screening for cystic fibrosis in Portugal: impact of algorithm optimization
records.summary: Objectives: Newborn screening (NBS) for cystic fibrosis (CF) has been implemented nationwide in Portugal since 2013. Until 2022, the screening algorithm was based on an IRT/PAP/IRT strategy. In order to improve the positive predictive value (PPV), genetic testing was incorporated into the screening algorithm in 2023. In 2024, the currently used algorithm was finally established. This study aims to analyse the results of CF newborn screening since 2024, and to compare the performance of the current algorithm with that of previous screening strategies. records.title: Thirty years of newborn screening for cystic fibrosis in Buenos Aires Province, Argentina: evolution of incidence in a long-term population-based experience
records.summary: Introduction: Newborn Screening (NBS) for Cystic Fibrosis (CF) is a cornerstone of public health, enabling early diagnosis, timely treatment, improved quality of life and while facilitating genetic counselling. Although CF NBS has been mandatory in Argentina since 1994, its regulation in Buenos Aires Province (BA) was implemented only in 2008. In July 2010, CF NBS was included in the Programme for the Diagnosis and Treatment of Congenital Diseases (PRODyTEC), Ministry of Health - BA. Objective: To describe the evolution of CF NBS in BA over a 30-year period, focusing on cumulative incidence trends up to 2024. records.title: Critical assessment of newborn screening data in a low-income country: insights from a Brazilian reference center
records.summary: Newborn screening (NBS) for cystic fibrosis (CF) is crucial to ensure early diagnosis and timely treatment. However, the multistep process involving IRT1, IRT2, sweat chloride testing may compromise its effectiveness in large and resource-limited countries such as Brazil with centralized screening process. Objectives: To analyze NBS performance in a public CF center between 2022 and 2025, focusing on diagnostic timelines, nutritional status, early symptoms, and microbiological findings at first assessment. records.title: Newborn screening for Cystic Fibrosis (CF-NBS) in Wallonia-Brussels Federation (Belgium): evaluation after six years of implementation
records.summary: Objective: To evaluate the performance of the cystic fibrosis newborn screening (CF-NBS) program using an IRT–DNA algorithm, officially implemented in the Wallonia–Brussels Federation (WBF) since January 2020. Methods: Immunoreactive trypsinogen (IRT1) was measured on dried blood spots collected 48–96 hours after birth. DNA analysis using a 12-mutation CFTR panel was performed when IRT1 was ≥ 99th percentile (P99). Infants with one or two CFTR variants were referred for a sweat test (ST). When IRT1 was ≥P99.9 with negative mutation screening, a failsafe strategy was applied using a repeat IRT at day 21. ST was performed if IRT21 remained > P99. records.title: Cystic fibrosis cases not detected by newborn screening: a single-centre cohort analysis
records.summary: Objectives: Universal Cystic fibrosis (CF) newborn screening (NBS) was introduced in the UK in 2007. However, a small proportion of cases are missed. Methods: A retrospective cohort study used laboratory NBS and clinical data from 2007 to 2025. Results: Twenty-two children born between 2009-2025 were identified. records.summary: Introduction: A patient was diagnosed with cystic fibrosis after positive newborn screening (Immunoreactive trypsinogen 153 ug/L, Pancreatitis associated protein 4.6 ug/L). records.title: Impact of timing of cystic fibrosis diagnosis on clinical outcomes: a comparative analysis of early- and late-diagnosed patients
records.summary: Introduction: The timing of cystic fibrosis (CF) diagnosis is key to disease progression and clinical outcomes. This study aimed to assess the effects of early (≤2 months) and late (>2 months) CF diagnosis on the clinical course and lung function in patients registered in the Turkish National Cystic Fibrosis Registry(CFRT). Methods: All patients registered in CFRT as of 2024 were included in the study and divided into two groups based on age at CF diagnosis: ≤2 months (Group 1) and >2 months (Group 2). records.title: Ethnic differences in the frequency of CFTR gene variants in the Russian Federation
records.summary: Objectives: Cystic fibrosis (CF) is a genetically inherited disease caused by mutations in the CFTR gene. The Russian Federation (RF) is home to more than 190 ethnic groups, including small indigenous and autochthonous nations. records.title: Genetic characteristics of patients with CF according to the Russian Federation Registry 2024
records.summary: Cystic fibrosis is a genetic, inherited disorder characterized by a wide range of clinical manifestations and genetic variants. Methods: The 2024 Registry included data from 3,573 patients and 271 patients who were not followed up that year. Results: The overall cumulative frequency of identified alleles was 95.7%. 97.1% of alleles were identified in children and 92.5% in adults. A total of 304 variants were identified, 159 of which were detected repeatedly. The most frequent variants were: F508del, CFTRdele2.3, E92K, 1677delTA, 3849+10kbC->T, 2143delT, 2184insA, W1282X, L138ins, N1303K. 33 variants are not included in international databases. records.title: Genetic epidemiology of cystic fibrosis in Lebanon
records.summary: Background: Cystic fibrosis (CF) is an autosomal recessive disease caused by pathogenic variants in the CFTR gene. While ΔF508 is the predominant mutation in European populations, the distribution of CFTR mutations exhibits significant geographic and ethnic variability. records.title: CFTR gene variants in the group of adult cystic fibrosis patients in Kazakhstan
records.summary: Background: CFTR genotyping has become especially important recently due to the possibility of targeted therapy for CF patients. records.summary: Background: CFTR genotyping is essential for accurate diagnosis of cystic fibrosis (CF) and for identifying patients eligible for mutation-specific therapies. records.summary: Objectives: Cystic Fibrosis, CF is usually diagnosed in children and adolescents, but rarely in adults. records.title: Characteristics of people with cystic fibrosis in the pediatric clinic in Cuenca, Ecuador
records.summary: Background: Cystic fibrosis (CF) is an autosomal recessive genetic disease caused by mutations in the CFTR gene, with varied clinical expression, which presents a diagnostic challenge. records.title: Optimising the diagnosis and management pathway for adults referred into the West Midlands adult cystic fibrosis centre
records.summary: Background: Accurate diagnosis of Cystic Fibrosis (CF) versus CFTR-Related Disorder (CFTR-RD) is complex, given the spectrum of CFTR protein dysfunction. CFTR-RD is defined as evidence of CFTR protein dysfunction without meeting the diagnostic criteria for CF. Misdiagnosing those with milder CFTR dysfunction as CF, can cause increased health anxiety and over-medicalisation. This Quality Improvement (QI) project aimed to standardise diagnostic practice for new referrals by implementing the European Cystic Fibrosis Society (ECFS) standards of care. records.title: The new face of cystic fibrosis after 24 years of population genetic carrier screening
records.summary: Objective: Population genetic carrier screening (PGCS) for cystic fibrosis (CF) has been offered in Israel since 1999 and funded by the Ministry of Health since 2008. records.summary: Objective: Cystic Fibrosis (CF) and Primary Ciliary Dyskinesia (PCD) are both recessive genetic disorders with overlapping clinical manifestations including mucociliary dysfunction leading to chronic respiratory infections, sinus disease, and early-onset of bronchiectasis. records.summary: Newborn screening (NBS) in UK for CF was introduced nationally in 2007. The UK CF Trust reports that for 300 babies that have a positive screening result, 225 are diagnosed with CF, 15 have CFSPID, and 60 do not have either (a false positive). records.summary: Objectives: To describe respiratory manifestations and clinical course in a Spanish cohort of children with Cystic Fibrosis Screen Positive, Inconclusive Diagnosis (CFSPID). records.title: Metabolomic profiles of newborns with elevated immunoreactive trypsinogen without cystic fibrosis diagnosis
records.summary: Objectives: Increased immunoreactive trypsinogen (IRT) concentrations detected through newborn screening (NBS) are commonly associated with cystic fibrosis (CF) but may also occur in infants without CF, resulting in false-positive findings. records.title: Too little to test: a single centre retrospective review of ‘quantity not sufficient’ sweat test samples and associated clinical characteristics in adults
records.summary: Introduction: Sweat testing (ST) is a key diagnostic test for cystic fibrosis. records.title: Primary nasal epithelial cells for personalized medicine in cystic fibrosis patients with rare mutations
records.summary: Objectives: Elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor (ETI) was developed to improve F508del-CFTR function in people with CF. Recently, ETI was approved for people with CF and at least one non-class I mutation. However, the efficacy of many rare variants is unknown. Therefore, we tested the rescue of CFTR activity by ETI in vitro and in vivo in non-F508del people with CF to improve understanding of mutation-specific CFTR modulator responses. Methods: Highly differentiated primary nasal epithelial cells (pHNEC) were established from 54 people with CF and at least one rare variant and no F508del allele and 29 healthy controls. CFTR activity was assessed by Ussing chamber measurements after ETI/DMSO incubation and express...
records.primary_theme: Precision CFTR biology and rare variants
records.why_it_matters: Primary nasal epithelial testing linked in vitro rare-variant ETI response with in vivo biomarker and lung-function improvement.
records.confidence_reason: Structured abstract includes sample size, functional testing, clinical follow-up subgroup, and conclusion.

records.title: CFTR function in airway epithelial cells derived from CFSPID/CRMS individuals
records.summary: Objectives: Infants with positive newborn screening for cystic fibrosis (CF) but low or intermediate sweat chloride levels and/or inconclusive CFTR genotypes are classified as CFSPID (CF screen-positive, inconclusive diagnosis) or CFTR-related metabolic syndrome (CRMS). While most individuals remain healthy, 10–40 % later develop CF or CFTR-related disorders (CFTR-RD). Reliable predictors of disease progression are lacking. We evaluated CFTR function in human nasal epithelial cell (HNEC) cultures from CFSPID/CRMS individuals using Ussing chambers and assessed their response to elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor (ETI) to explore whether in vitro functional profiling could stratify disease risk. Methods: HNECs fro...
secondary_themes: Precision CFTR biology and rare variants
records.why_it_matters: HNEC functional profiling may stratify CFSPID/CRMS children by progression risk before clinical disease is obvious.
records.confidence_reason: Structured abstract reports CFSPID samples, controls, functional range, ETI response, and reclassification pattern.

records.title: Evaluation of HE4 expression associated with epithelial mesenchymal transition in cystic fibrosis epithelial cells
records.summary: Objective: Impaired CFTR function results in elevated HE4 expression in cystic fibrosis (CF) and triggered via NF-κB in p.Phe508del-CFTR CFBE 41o− cells in vitro. CFTR dysfunction was associated with epithelial mesenchymal transition (EMT) in CF, but the potential involvement of HE4 in CF-related EMT is still unclear. Methods: The degree of EMT was compared between CFBE 41o− cells carrying the p.Phe508del-CFTR and wt-CFTR through the level of epithelial and mesenchymal markers by fluorescence microscopy, RT-qPCR and ELISA. EMT was further induced by TGFβ1 in p.Phe508del-CFTR CFBE cells from 24-120h to investigate the relationship between the development of EMT and HE4/MMP9 expression. CFTR dysfunction was rest...
records.primary_theme: Precision CFTR biology and rare variants
records.why_it_matters: HE4 and EMT biology may reveal downstream epithelial remodeling pathways linked to CFTR dysfunction.
records.confidence_reason: Structured cell-model abstract details assays, modulator reversal experiments, and mechanistic conclusions.

records.title: Pregnancy in women with cystic fibrosis: a four-case series in a Portuguese CF centre
records.summary: Background: As survival improves in cystic fibrosis (CF), pregnancy has emerged as a clinical scenario with heterogeneous maternal and fetal outcomes. Objectives: To compare maternal and neonatal outcomes in four pregnancies in women with CF. Methods: Retrospective case series from a CF centre. Results: Four women (P1–P4) with heterogeneous genotypes and disease severity were included. P1 (F508del/W1282X; FEV₁ ~85–90% predicted) had severe exocrine pancreatic insufficiency requiring long-term gastrostomy feeding and poor nutritional reserve. She remained on elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor (ETI) during pregnancy with stable respiratory status but inadequate weight gain; intrauterine fetal death occurred at 32+...
records.primary_theme: Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
records.why_it_matters: Pregnancy outcomes varied more with baseline disease severity, nutrition, and adherence than with genotype or modulator exposure alone.
records.confidence_reason: Structured four-case series provides maternal, neonatal, ETI, nutrition, and respiratory details.

records.title: Efficacy and stability of a novel microbial enzyme ANG003 measured in gastrointestinal chyme compared to pancrelipase.
records.summary: Objectives: Direct measurement of lipolysis, proteolysis and amylolysis in chyme from different gastrointestinal compartments could ensure a natural environment to measure efficacy and stability of enzymes from pancreatic enzymes replacement therapy (PERT) in the upper small intestine. Stability and efficacy of a novel broad spectrum microbially-derived product (ANG003) and porcine-derived pancrelipase was estimated in chyme from exocrine pancreatic insufficient (EPI) pig model. Methods: Five EPI pigs were tested. Four weeks after pancreatic duct ligation, cannulas for chyme collection were placed in the stomach, duodenum and ileum. Pigs were fed a high fat meal (1) without enzymes, (2) with two doses of ANG00...
secondary_themes: Emerging genetic, RNA, and cell-model therapies
records.why_it_matters: A microbial enzyme showed earlier digestive activity than pancrelipase in EPI pigs, suggesting a possible future PERT alternative.
records.confidence_reason: Structured preclinical study reports model, treatments, compartment sampling, and enzyme activity findings.

records.title: The Adult Home Sputum-Induction Trial (CF-Adult_HomeSpIT). Feasibility and acceptability of early morning sputum-induction at home in adults with cystic fibrosis. Preliminary results.
records.summary: Objectives: The CF-SpIT trials in children show sputum-induction to be an effective approach to pathogen detection both in clinic and at home. CF-Adult_HomeSpIT [ISRCTN86523335] is a prospective internally-controlled interventional trial in 100 adults comparing home-sampling using spontaneous early morning cough-clear (emCC) and home sputum-induction (hSI) with matched same-day clinic sampling using cough swab (CS), spontaneous clinic cough-clear (cCC) and clinic sputum-induction (cSI). Here we present preliminary qualitative data exploring feasibility & acceptability of home sampling in adults with CF. Methods: Patients at the All Wales Adult CF Centre already using hypertonic saline (HTS) were eligible for r...
records.primary_theme: Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
records.why_it_matters: Home sputum induction appears feasible and acceptable, which could expand microbiological monitoring outside clinic visits.
records.confidence_reason: Structured preliminary trial data include design, sampling procedures, feasibility ratings, and patient acceptability.

records.title: Triple combination therapy modulates airway inflammation and reduces Pseudomonas aeruginosa burden in pediatric cystic fibrosis
records.summary: Objectives: P. aeruginosa (Pa) lung infections are the leading cause of mortality in Cystic Fibrosis (CF), typically chronic and punctuated by exacerbations. The advent of triple combination therapy (ETI) has reshaped the clinical course of CF, with substantial airway microbiome changes, including a marked reduction in Pa. Here, we aimed to evaluate the effects of ETI on Pa burden and innate immune responses in children with CF. Methods: Longitudinal sputum cultures were analysed at baseline and at 1 year of ETI therapy allowing classification of patients as chronic, intermittent, or negative. Sputum concentrations of antimicrobial peptides (including Secretory leukocyte protease inhibitor (SLPI)) were measure...
records.why_it_matters: ETI may reduce Pseudomonas burden partly by restoring host antimicrobial defenses such as SLPI.
records.confidence_reason: Structured abstract combines pediatric sputum data with epithelial-cell experiments and mechanistic testing.

records.title: Saliva qPCR as a non-invasive approach for detecting Pseudomonas aeruginosa lung infection in people with cystic fibrosis
records.summary: Objectives: Accurate detection of pathogens in cystic fibrosis (CF) lung infection is essential and traditionally relied on sputum culture. Saliva represents a non-invasive, easily obtainable alternative sample for people with CF (pwCF). This study aimed to determine the limit of detection (LoD) of Pseudomonas aeruginosa (Pa) in spiked saliva samples using real-time quantitative PCR (qPCR). Methods: Spiking assays (n=4) were performed using Pa reference isolate (ATCC 27853) and a clinical isolate (0293A) across a dilution range of 1 x 108 – 1 x 101 CFU/mL in pooled saliva from healthy volunteers (n=6). Total viable counts (TVC) were determined by culture on Pseudomonas selective agar. Genomic DNA was extracted...
records.why_it_matters: Saliva qPCR could offer a less burdensome way to detect Pseudomonas when sputum is hard to obtain.
records.confidence_reason: Structured assay-development abstract reports spiking methods, limit of detection, and next-step clinical relevance.

records.title: How keep identifying chronic Pseudomonas aeruginosa colonisation in the ETI era
records.summary: The introduction of elexacaftor-tezacaftor-ivacaftor (ETI) therapy significantly reduced sputum production in patients with cystic fibrosis (pwCF). This has made it difficult to recover previous colonising pathogens and to use current chronic infection definitions. In order to monitor chronic Pseudomonas aeruginosa (PA) colonisation in patients receiving ETI treatment, we used droplet digital PCR (ddPCR) to detect PA in culture-negative respiratory samples. We developed an in-house triplex ddPCR assay targeting the oprL, ecfX and gyrB genes, with a detection limit of 3 copies/mL, 100% specificity and 91.7% sensitivity. The assay was applied to 30 culture-negative samples from 18 pwCF on ETI with a history of p...
records.why_it_matters: Highly sensitive ddPCR may help detect chronic Pseudomonas colonisation when ETI reduces sputum production and culture yield.
records.confidence_reason: Unstructured summary includes assay performance and sample application but lacks full visible results and conclusion.

records.title: Assessing population-level antibiotic susceptibility in heterogeneous Pseudomonas aeruginosa infections: implications for cystic fibrosis care
records.summary: Objectives: Chronic lung infection with Pseudomonas aeruginosa is a major cause of morbidity in individuals with chronic lung infections like cystic fibrosis (CF). Extensive phenotypic and genotypic adaptation during chronic infection results in highly heterogeneous bacterial populations with varying susceptibility to antibiotics. Traditional antibiotic susceptibility testing (AST) is typically performed on a very limited number of individual isolates and therefore often fails to accurately predict clinical responses in chronic infections, as it does not capture population-level behaviour. The aim of this project was to investigate the extent to which individual isolates reflect the antibiotic susceptibility o...
records.why_it_matters: Population-level susceptibility testing may better capture within-host Pseudomonas heterogeneity than single-isolate AST.
records.confidence_reason: Structured abstract describes isolate collection, MIC testing, model results, and clinical implication.

records.title: Early detection of cystic fibrosis airway pathogens in paediatric patients: added clinical value of molecular testing beyond culture
records.summary: Objectives: This longitudinal study aimed to compare conventional culture and targeted quantitative Polymerase Chain Reaction (qPCR) for the early detection of CF-associated pathogens and to characterise airway microbial community composition. Methods: Fifteen paediatric CF patients attending Galway University Hospital (GUH) provided 4 sputum samples each during clinically stable visits over a one-year period. Samples (n=60) were tested using culture and qPCR to detect and quantify Pseudomonas aeruginosa (PA), Haemophilus influenzae (HI) and Staphylococcus aureus (SA). Airway microbial community composition was assessed using 16S rRNA gene sequencing. Ethical approval was obtained from the GUH Clinical Researc...
records.why_it_matters: qPCR detected low-level Pseudomonas DNA in culture-negative pediatric samples, raising questions about earlier pathogen signals.
records.confidence_reason: Structured longitudinal study reports culture, qPCR, sequencing methods, and pathogen detection differences.

records.title: Paired culture-based analysis reveals heterogeneous airway microbiota restructuring after elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor (ETI) therapy in adult people with cystic fibrosis (pwCF)
records.summary: Chronic airway infection in pwCF is characterised by person-specific microbial communities often dominated by pathogenic taxa. While ETI therapy improves clinical outcomes, its effects on airway microbial ecology remain poorly understood. Using paired culture-based samples, we assessed within-subject changes in airway bacterial community composition pre- and post-ETI therapy. Paired sputum samples were analysed from 14 pwCF immediately pre- and post-ETI initiation (> 12 months minimum). Viable bacterial communities were characterised using extended aerobic and anaerobic quantitative culture with species identification by MALDI-TOF. Absolute abundances (CFU/g or CFU/mL, log₁₀-transformed) were aggregated at spe...
records.why_it_matters: Paired pre/post ETI cultures can show whether airway communities restructure uniformly or remain patient-specific.
records.confidence_reason: Unstructured summary provides design and methods but visible outcome detail is limited.

records.title: Therapeutic drug monitoring–guided beta-lactam dosing in pediatric cystic fibrosis: a case series
records.summary: Objectives: Despite advances with CFTR modulators, children with cystic fibrosis (CF) continue to experience recurrent bacterial pulmonary exacerbations, increasingly complicated by reduced β-lactam susceptibility. Our aim was to evaluated pharmacokinetic/pharmacodynamic (PK/PD) target attainment following implementation of a therapeutic drug monitoring (TDM) service, using free trough-to-MIC ratios (fCmin/MIC) ≥1 and ≥4 as targets. Methods: A center-specific β-lactam TDM protocol was introduced alongside multidisciplinary education. Meropenem and piperacillin–tazobactam were initially administered as 60-minute infusions. When free drug concentrations failed to meet PK/PD targets, infusion duration was prolong...
secondary_themes: Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
records.why_it_matters: Pediatric beta-lactam TDM can reveal underexposure and guide individualized infusion strategies for resistant exacerbations.

records.title: ​​​​​​Prevalence of mucoid Staphylococcus aureus from CF airway specimens in the era of modulator therapy
records.summary: The emergence of a mucoid phenotype is a well‑described adaptive mechanism observed across multiple bacterial pathogens, where increased exopolysaccharide production enhances persistence and biofilm formation. We previously reported the prevalence of mucoid Staphylococcus aureus (Sa) and its association with lung function in people with cystic fibrosis (pwCF). Since the introduction of highly efficient modulator therapy (HEMT), many studies have investigated shifts in the frequency of classical CF pathogens, demonstrating a clear reduction in the isolation of Pseudomonas aeruginosa (Pa) and, to a lesser extent, Sa. As HEMT fundamentally alters the unique environment of the CF lung, an important question arises...
records.why_it_matters: Mucoid Staphylococcus aureus may represent an adaptive persistence phenotype that needs tracking in the HEMT era.
records.confidence_reason: Only a partial unstructured background is visible; results and conclusion are absent.

records.title: Pulmonary exacerbations and microbiological surveillance in children with cystic fibrosis: lessons from a national newborn screening programme in North Macedonia
records.summary: Background: Cystic fibrosis is a multisystem genetic disorder in which progressive pulmonary involvement remains the leading cause of morbidity and mortality. Pulmonary exacerbations accelerate lung function decline. Early diagnosis through newborn screening enables timely intervention and systematic follow-up. This study evaluated the frequency of pulmonary exacerbations and associated microbiological findings in children diagnosed through newborn screening. Methods: A retrospective observational study included 19 children diagnosed via national newborn screening and followed at the paediatric cystic fibrosis centre, University Children’s Clinic, Skopje, North Macedonia. All pulmonary exacerbations recorded b...
records.why_it_matters: Children diagnosed by newborn screening still had frequent exacerbations linked to persistent airway infection, supporting close surveillance.
records.confidence_reason: Partial structured abstract provides cohort, exacerbation counts, microbiology, and conclusion.

records.title: Impact of adherence to inhaled antibiotics on lung function and pulmonary exacerbations in cystic fibrosis (CF) patients taking elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor (ETI).
records.summary: Objectives: Improvements in lung function (ppFEV1), airway microbiology and pulmonary exacerbations (PE) are established benefits of ETI. Reducing treatment burden is a key CF care priority, with recent studies reporting non-inferiority from stopping nebulised mucolytics. Long-term value of inhaled antibiotics in people taking ETI is unclear, with some opting to discontinue therapy. This retrospective, single-centre observational study assessed impact of inhaled antibiotic adherence on ppFEV1 and PE frequency during the first 60 months of ETI treatment in CF patients. Methods: CF patients prescribed ETI and inhaled antibiotics at King’s College Hospital (2020-2025) were classified as adherent or poor/discontin...
secondary_themes: Care burden, adherence, and psychosocial impact
records.why_it_matters: Inhaled antibiotic adherence on ETI may help sustain lung-function gains, but exacerbation benefit remains uncertain.

records.title: Review of intravenous antibiotic use before and after initiation of elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor in people with cystic fibrosis
records.summary: Background: The Antibiotic Spectrum Index (ASI) is a measure of antimicrobial breadth and potential antimicrobial resistance (AMR) burden in people with cystic fibrosis (pwCF). Following the introduction of elexacaftor-tezacaftor-ivacaftor (ETI), hospitalisations for pulmonary exacerbations have fallen substantially, but the impact on antibiotic selection and spectrum remains unclear. This study aim to review intravenous (IV) antibiotic use at a large adult CF centre, focusing on ASI before and after ETI initiation. Methods: All inpatient IV antibiotic courses between 1 June 2013 and 30 September 2025 were reviewed. Data collected included admission and discharge dates, ETI initiation dates, and CF-specific AS...
records.why_it_matters: ETI reduced IV antibiotic admissions but not admission-level antibiotic spectrum, shifting stewardship toward lifetime AMR burden.
records.confidence_reason: Partial structured study includes long time window, large admission count, ASI analysis, and conclusion.

records.title: Quantification of antimicrobial resistance in the CFTR modulator era: a retrospective analysis using the relative resistance index
records.summary: Introduction: Little is known about the dynamics of antimicrobial resistance (AMR) in P.aeruginosa (Pa) with long-term elexacaftor/ivacaftor/tezacaftor (ETI) treatment. The Relative Resistance Index (RRI) is a validated AMR quantification tool in Pa. This study therefore explored the impact of ETI on RRI. Study design: A retrospective, microbiological surveillance study was conducted at our large regional adult CF centre including consecutive isolates from people living with CF (pwCF) who started ETI. RRI was calculated from antibiograms of all Pa isolates using scores of 0,0.5,1 for reported S/I/R respectively and scored cumulatively for ciprofloxacin, ceftazidime, meropenem, piperacillin/tazobactam and tobra...
records.why_it_matters: Persistent Pseudomonas after ETI may be enriched for higher antimicrobial resistance, requiring continued surveillance.
records.confidence_reason: Partial structured microbiological surveillance study reports large isolate count and pre/post ETI RRI changes.

records.title: Sustained fluid-associated weight gain following cystic fibrosis transmembrane conductance regulator modulator therapy
records.summary: Objective: Cystic fibrosis transmembrane conductance regulator (CFTR) modulators improve lung function and nutritional status in cystic fibrosis (CF). Weight gain is commonly attributed to improved appetite and metabolism, yet sustained hypervolemic edema or fluid-associated weight gain has not been described in clinical trials and remains under-recognized. We report four cases of clinically significant swelling and fluid-associated weight gain following CFTR modulator therapy. Methods: We retrospectively reviewed four patients with CF or CF-related disorder who developed significant fluid-associated weight gain after CFTR modulator initiation. Data collected included demographics, CFTR genotype, pre- and post...
records.why_it_matters: Rapid edema-associated weight gain after CFTR modulators may be an under-recognized adverse effect requiring dose changes.
records.confidence_reason: Structured case series gives temporal association, evaluation for alternatives, dose responses, and causality scores.

records.title: Safety and outcomes with the discontinuation of inhaled antibiotics in the CFTR modulator era: A Retrospective cohort study at a regional adult Cystic Fibrosis centre
records.summary: Objectives: Little is known about the consequences of inhaled antibiotic discontinuation for people receiving CFTR modulators. Within our large adult CF Centre, we reviewed the patient outcomes following the discontinuation of their nebulised antibiotic therapy. Methods: Completing a retrospective analysis at our large regional adult CF centre we reviewed all the cases of nebulised antibiotic discontinuation between 2000 and 2025. We categorised them into 3 groups; Group 1 (Patient choice), Group 2 (Low adherence but clinically stable), Group 3 (Clinician led due to clinical stability). The outcomes between the 3 groups, with a focus on the re-initiation of a nebulised antibiotic. Results: Of the 420 pwCF at o...
secondary_themes: Care burden, adherence, and psychosocial impact
records.why_it_matters: Most adults who stopped nebulised antibiotics did not restart, but lower lung function predicted greater need to resume therapy.
records.confidence_reason: Structured retrospective cohort reports discontinuation groups, restart rates, and predictors.

records.title: Temporal impact of elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor (ETI) on oral and IV antibiotic use at a single CF center in the US
records.summary: Objectives: ETI has been demonstrated to have a significant impact on lung function, symptoms and exacerbation rates. We aimed to assess prescription trends at the University of Washington adult CF center. We hypothesized that there would be an initial drop in oral antibiotic use with ETI and the Covid pandemic, but that it would slowly rebound in the adult population. Methods: We performed a longitudinal single center cohort study assessing the temporal impact of ETI on the use of oral antibiotics from December 2014 to December 2024. We included patients who received an ETI prescription during this period. To assess temporal trends, we calculated monthly oral antibiotic prescription rates per patient encounte...
records.why_it_matters: Oral antibiotic use fell after ETI and the pandemic but rebounded by 2024, showing ongoing treatment demand.
records.confidence_reason: Structured longitudinal cohort reports prescribing trends, sample size, and interpretation.

records.title: Multicenter experience with Burkholderia infection in cystic fibrosis: microbiological outcomes and impact of CFTR modulators
records.summary: Objetives: To evaluate the clinical, functional and microbiological evolution of cystic fibrosis (CF) patients with chronic Burkholderia cepacia complex (Bcc) infection following initiation of CFTR modulator therapy. Methods: Multicenter observational study including 16 CF patients with chronic Bcc infection followed at referral centers in Portugal. Patients under follow-up between 2020 and the present who initiated elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor (ETI) were identified. Clinical data, respiratory cultures and spirometric parameters (FEV1 and FVC, % predicted) were retrospectively reviewed to assess the impact of ETI on microbiological status and lung function. Results: Mean age at Bcc identification was 24.8...
records.why_it_matters: ETI was associated with lung-function improvement and Burkholderia clearance in many chronically infected patients.
records.confidence_reason: Partial structured multicentre study includes cohort, ETI exposure, lung function, microbiological clearance, and caveat.

records.title: Respiratory microbiological profile changes in people with cystic fibrosis after three-year of elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor treatment
records.summary: Objectives: Elexacaftor–tezacaftor–ivacaftor therapy (ETI) significantly improves lung function and reduces pulmonary exacerbations in people with cystic fibrosis (pwCF). However, its long-term effects on airway colonization are incompletely defined. This study aims to evaluate longitudinal changes in expectorating ability (EA) and respiratory microbiological profiles during the first three years of ETI. Methods: A prospective study was conducted at the Regional Cystic Fibrosis Center in Milan from 1st September 2020 to 30th September 2025. A cohort of pwCF starting ETI were followed for up to 36 months. EA and the prevalence of key respiratory pathogens (from sputum and nasopharyngeal aspirates) were evaluate...
records.why_it_matters: Three-year ETI data show reduced expectoration and pathogen shifts, creating a need for new respiratory sampling standards.
records.confidence_reason: Structured prospective cohort reports 367 participants, longitudinal pathogen trends, and sampling implications.

records.title: Comparison of clinical outcomes in adults with cystic fibrosis with Pseudomonas aeruginosa on and off prophylactic antibiotics: a retrospective cohort study
records.summary: Objectives: Inhaled prophylactic antibiotics remain recommended for people with cystic fibrosis (CF) with chronic Pseudomonas aeruginosa (Pa) infection. In the post-CFTR modulator era, some patients discontinue or do not initiate prophylaxis despite Pa infection, often by patient choice rather than clinical recommendation. This study examined the impact of this on lung function, antibiotic use, and antimicrobial resistance. This study aimed to compare outcomes in adults with CF and Pa receiving versus not receiving prophylactic antibiotics in a UK adult CF centre. Methods: Annual review data from 161 adults (2022 and 2024) were analysed using Mann–Whitney U tests for FEV₁% predicted, change in FEV₁% predicted,...
secondary_themes: Care burden, adherence, and psychosocial impact
records.why_it_matters: Adult post-modulator data question how prophylactic inhaled antibiotics should be reviewed without prematurely withdrawing recommended care.

records.title: Reduced sputum pathogen presence but persistent Staphylococcus aureus in throat swabs after three years of elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor and its association with lung function
records.summary: Objectives: Elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor (ETI) reduces sputum production in people with cystic fibrosis (pwCF). This study retrospectively examined longitudinal changes in pathogen detection in sputum and throat swab cultures before and up to three years after ETI initiation. Methods: Microbiological sputum and throat swab cultures of adult pwCF treated at two Dutch CF centres were collected from one year prior to three years after ETI initiation and grouped into six-month intervals. records.title: Clinical predictors of long-term outcomes following initial Pseudomonas aeruginosa eradication in children with cystic fibrosis: a 10-year retrospective study
records.summary: Objectives: To evaluate clinical characteristics,eradication success,and long-term microbiological outcomes in pediatric cystic fibrosis(CF)patients following first-time Pseudomonas aeruginosa(Pa)isolation and tobramycin-based treatment. Results: Median age at first Pa isolation was89months(IQR: 88.25); median diagn...
records.why_it_matters: Lower BMI and mucoid phenotype at first Pseudomonas isolation may predict recurrence after eradication therapy.

records.title: To be or not to be? Evaluating the efficacy of nebulised tobramycin regimens for Pseudomonas aeruginosa eradication in children with cystic fibrosis following a reduction of oral ciprofloxacin use
records.summary: Background: Paediatric patients provide respiratory samples for microbiology testing when symptomatic and at routine clinic visits. Eradication is attempted for all first isolates of Pseudomonas aeruginosa (Pa). First-line treatment is three-months oral ciprofloxacin plus nebulised colistimethate sodium but prescribing of ciprofloxacin has declined following safety warnings. Alternative regimens we use include intravenous (IV) antibiotics, nebulised tobramycin for 28 days or a novel extended regimen of alternate-month nebulised tobramycin for six months in younger children where nebuliser technique may be less effective. Treatment choice is patient-focused following multidisciplinary team review. Methods: We e...

records.title: Retrospective review of treatment success comparing nebulised colistimethate sodium versus tobramycin for Pseudomonas aeruginosa eradication in paediatric cystic fibrosis in the post modulator era
records.summary: Early eradication of Pseudomonas aeruginosa (Pa) following initial isolation remains central to cystic fibrosis (CF) management, aiming to prevent chronic infection and mitigate long‑term lung damage. In the post‑modulator era, the optimal strategy for Pa eradication remains uncertain. Insufficient evidence exists on the optimal antibiotic regimen for Pa eradication (Hewer et al, 2023). Although modulators have reduced Pa burden, persistent and chronic infections continue to be observed as described by Pescaru et al, 2025. We compared eradication success between 3-months nebulised colistimethate sodium and 1-month nebulised tobramycin, with 3-weeks oral ciprofloxacin in both regimes, in young people with CF at...
records.why_it_matters: Compares pediatric Pseudomonas eradication regimens in the post-modulator era, where optimal antibiotic strategy remains unsettled.

records.title: Some results of long-term CFTR modulator therapy in adults with cystic fibrosis, chronically infected with Burkholderiales
records.summary: Burkholderiales are the most dangerous of CF pathogens. Over the past decade, the therapeutic landscape for CF has expanded to include CFTR modulators, of which adult pwCFs in Russia use IVA, LUM/IVA, and ELX/TEZ/IVA. Modulator therapy (MT) improves respiratory function, sweat chloride levels, and quality of life in pwCFs. The objective of our study was to investigate the effect of combination therapy, including MT, on the sputum microbiome composition in adult pwCFs infected with Burkholderiales since childhood. Sputum samples were collected over 7-10 years from pwCFs with B. multivorans ST835 (35 years old, non-F508del/non-F508del), B. cenocepacia ST709 (35 years old, F508del/F508del), and A. ruhlandii ST262...
records.why_it_matters: Long-term modulator exposure in adults chronically infected with Burkholderiales may reshape sputum microbiome composition.

records.title: Early MRSA therapy in CF (streamlined eradication with repeat cycle) (STaph Aureus Resistance-Treat Early and Repeat (STAR-TER))
records.summary: Objectives: Chronic respiratory infection with methicillin resistant Staphylococcus aureus (MRSA) is associated with worse clinical outcomes in CF (higher mortality). Yet, uncertainty exists about early treatment of asymptomatic MRSA positive patients. Our prior randomized trial (STAR-too) using an intense eradication regimen showed higher rates of MRSA eradication in the treatment compared to no-treatment arm. This follow-up study evaluated the microbiologic outcome of oral antibiotic and topical decontamination in subjects with incident MRSA. Methods: Open label multi-center treatment study with comparison to control group from the STAR-too trial. Inclusion criteria were incident (first ever or at least 2 ye...

records.why_it_matters: A simplified repeated MRSA eradication regimen showed limited benefit, informing whether asymptomatic incident MRSA should be treated this way.

records.title: Co-treatment with glatiramer acetate enhances the efficacy of tobramycin against other cystic fibrosis pathogens including Staphylococcus aureus
records.summary: Objectives: Identifying therapies to reduce the cumulative antibiotic burden in people with cystic fibrosis (pwCF) is a priority, particularly in chronic lung infections that drive adverse side effects and antimicrobial resistance. Glatiramer acetate (GA) shares similarity to antimicrobial peptides and enhances tobramycin (TOB) activity against the Gram-negative Pseudomonas aeruginosa (Pa). GA’s proposed mechanism is via interaction with lipopolysaccharides (LPS). Thus, its effect on common co-infecting Gram-positive (G+) organisms like Staphylococcus aureus (Sa), is unclear. records.why_it_matters: Glatiramer acetate may potentiate tobramycin against Staphylococcus aureus and other CF pathogens, suggesting an antibiotic-adjuvant strategy.

records.title: Agr typing of Staphylococcus aureus isolated from patients with cystic fibrosis
records.summary: A key role in the pathogenesis of staphylococcal infections belongs to the "quorum-sensing" system, which includes the accessory gene regulator (agr), coordinating the expression of virulence factors. The functional state of agr determines the bacterial strategy — from acute aggression to chronic persistence. Objective: To define the population structure of S. aureus from cystic fibrosis (CF) patients by agr types and assess links to methicillin resistance. records.why_it_matters: Agr typing can reveal virulent and adaptive Staphylococcus aureus subpopulations relevant to surveillance and novel therapeutics.

records.title: Staphyloxanthin provides intra-species protection from oxidative killing of Staphylococcus aureus.
records.summary: Objectives: Staphylococcus aureus (Sa) is the predominant pathogen in people with Cystic Fibrosis (pwCF). Most isolates produce the carotenoid pigment staphyloxanthin (STX), which confers resistance to oxidative stress and neutrophil mediated killing. Clinically, Sa strains with and without pigmentation frequently coexist, suggesting an advantage of heterogeneity. The aim of this study was to demonstrate the role of STX at the population level and to explore the loss of pigmentation as a potential adaptive process. Methods: Prevalence of crt deficient Sa strains were analyzed using Whole Genome Sequence (WGS) data from CF samples. records.title: Surveillance of Staphylococcus aureus in a large paediatric cystic fibrosis centre
records.summary: Objectives: Cystic fibrosis (CF) affects over 10,000 in the UK. S. aureus, a common pathogen in CF, damages lung epithelium and worsens pulmonary decline. Methods: Royal Manchester Children’s Hospital (RMCH), a major tertiary CF centre in northwest England, cares for 330 children with CF, including 200 through direct care and 130 via shared arrangements. records.title: Evaluating the role of the gut as a reservoir for non-tuberculous mycobacteria in people with cystic fibrosis
records.summary: Objectives: Non-tuberculous mycobacteria (NTM) represent a significant disease burden in the cystic fibrosis (CF) population. Whilst the acquisition of NTM to the respiratory tract is predominantly attributed to environmental exposures, the contribution of the gut-lung axis to NTM pulmonary disease remains poorly understood. Methods: In this study, we retrospectively examined 1285 stool and 223 respiratory microbiomes from 145 children and 52 adults with CF, and 171 healthy control (HC) children, to assess the prevalence, abundance and clinical significance of NTM in gut. records.why_it_matters: Microbiome data suggest the gut is unlikely to be a meaningful reservoir for pulmonary NTM in CF.

records.title: Management and outcomes of Mycobacterium abscessus complex (MABc) infections in patients with cystic fibrosis: A 10-year Belgian single-centre review
records.summary: Objectives: Mycobacterium abscessus complex (MABc) remains a challenging pathogen in cystic fibrosis (CF), given the heterogeneous disease course, lack of biomarkers to guide treatment decisions, and substantial treatment toxicity. records.title: Mycobacterium abscessus clearance post initiation of elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor in patients with cystic fibrosis
records.summary: Objectives: Mycobacterium abscessus (MABSC) isolation in people with cystic fibrosis (pwCF) can have major implications including reduced lung function, high treatment burden, transplant exclusion, and stricter infection control requirements. records.title: Signed, Sealed, Sputum: detecting non-tuberculosis Mycobacteria by post in cystic fibrosis
records.summary: Introduction: Sputum microbiological surveillance underpins Cystic Fibrosis (CF) care, but the move towards virtual clinics makes this challenging. records.title: Non-tuberculous mycobacteria in adult cystic fibrosis patients before and after elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor: a real-world cohort analysis
records.summary: Objectives: Non-tuberculous mycobacteria (NTM) are increasingly detected in adults with cystic fibrosis (CF). records.title: Patient-focused Target Product Profiles for the development of phage therapies to treat lung infections in cystic fibrosis
records.summary: Objective: Despite advances in cystic fibrosis (CF) care, lung infections are a leading cause of morbidity and mortality for people with CF (pwCF). records.summary: Objectives: Stenotrophomonas maltophilia (Sm) is an opportunistic Gram-negative MDR pathogen that causes life-threatening infections in cystic fibrosis patients. records.title: ‘One Bronchiectasis’ in clinical trials of anti-pseudomonal agents: an evidence based approach?
records.summary: CFTR modulator treated (MT) cystic fibrosis (CF) is considered by some to be clinically similar to other causes of bronchiectasis (Bx); people with CF (pwCF) generally do not eradicate bacterial airway infection. Some recent trials of anti-inflammatory agents are cohorting MT-CF with other causes of Bx under the umbrella concept: ‘One Bx’. records.title: Utilising loop-mediated isothermal amplification (LAMP) to detect Pseudomonas aeruginosa in saliva
records.summary: Objectives: Pseudomonas aeruginosa (Pa) remains a key respiratory pathogen in people with cystic fibrosis (pwCF). Following the introduction of CFTR modulator therapies, sputum production has decreased in pwCF, highlighting the need to explore alternative sample types such as saliva. This study aimed to develop a loop-mediated isothermal amplification (LAMP) assay for the rapid detection of Pa in saliva samples. LAMP utilises four to six primers to amplify DNA at a single temperature, reducing assay time and enabling simpler workflows compared to PCR, whilst maintaining sensitivity. records.title: Metabolic adaptation in sulfur-rich cystic fibrosis airways shapes pulmonary pathogen dynamics
records.summary: Objectives: Pulmonary infection in people with Cystic Fibrosis (pwCF) shifts from early Staphylococcus aureus (Sa) to later Pseudomonas aeruginosa (Pa) dominance, exacerbating airway inflammation. records.title: Modulation of antimicrobial susceptibility in cystic fibrosis pathogens via polymer-based nanohydrogels delivery
records.summary: Objectives: Cystic fibrosis (CF) lungs are characterized by a dense mucus layer that promotes chronic colonization by opportunistic pathogens, where Staphylococcus aureus (Sa) and Pseudomonas aeruginosa (Pa) are predominant pathogens strongly associated with increased morbidity and mortality. records.title: Decade-long dynamics in lung sampling and microbiological outcomes at a large adult cystic fibrosis centre
records.summary: Background: Elexacaftor/Tezacaftor/Ivacaftor (ETI) has transformed microbiological landscape in people with cystic fibrosis (pwCF). records.title: Changes in faecal antibiotic resistance genes pre and post elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor (ETI)
records.summary: Objectives: Antibiotics have been a mainstay treatment for the pulmonary manifestations of cystic fibrosis (CF). records.title: Microbiological changes in cystic fibrosis: the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic and CFTR modulators
records.summary: Objectives: The lung microbiota in people with Cystic Fibrosis (CF) is shaped by environmental and host factors. The COVID-19 pandemic altered healthcare delivery and social behaviour, while the introduction of Elexacaftor–Tezacaftor–Ivacaftor (ETI), in Norway from July 2022, transformed CF care. records.title: Impact of elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor therapy on routine respiratory microbiology results in cystic fibrosis: a real-world retrospective study
records.summary: Objective: Triple CFTR modulator therapy with elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor (ETI) has substantially changed CF management but its real-world impact on respiratory microbiology remains incompletely characterized. records.title: Real-world outcomes and tolerability of newer antipseudomonal β-lactams (ceftolozane/tazobactam, ceftazidime/avibactam and cefiderocol) in cystic fibrosis pulmonary exacerbations.
records.summary: Background: Pulmonary exacerbations (PEx) in people with cystic fibrosis (pwCF) are frequently complicated by multidrug-resistant (MDR) Gram-negative infection and advanced lung disease. records.title: Not a classical pathogen, but a persistent one: Escherichia coli in cystic fibrosis
records.summary: Escherichia coli has not traditionally been considered a typical cystic fibrosis (CF) pathogen. However, epidemiological surveillance around the world has revealed its increasing prevalence in respiratory samples from people with CF (pwCF). records.title: Investigating linear trends in ABPA markers in cystic fibrosis patients at a large tertiary paediatric CF centre
records.summary: Objectives: Allergic bronchopulmonary aspergillosis (ABPA) is a hypersensitivity to Aspergillus fumigatus, mainly affecting those with Asthma or Cystic Fibrosis (CF). A retrospective analysis used data from an electronic record. Patients under 1 year old or with data gaps over 3 years were excluded. Biomarkers, including eosinophil count, total IgE, and A. fumi...

records.title: Vaping is associated with CF-relevant gut-lung microbiome and metabolic remodeling in mice
records.summary: Background: Cystic fibrosis (CF) is characterized by chronic inflammation, gut-lung axis dysfunction, microbial dysbiosis, and systemic metabolic abnormalities. Human and animal studies suggest that CF-relevant microbiome and metabolomic alterations can arise early and may be influenced by environmental modifiers. Vaping, also known as electronic nicotine delivery system (ENDS) use, is increasingly prevalent, including among individuals with CF, yet its impact on systemic metabolism and gut-lung microbial ecology remains poorly understood. records.title: Pneumocystis jirovecii in Russian cystic fibrosis patients: a pilot study on colonization prevalence and clinical associations
records.summary: Pneumocystis jirovecii, a fungal pathogen, is capable of causing infection beyond severely immunocompromised patients, including in those with relatively intact immunity. records.title: Dynamics of microbiological parameters in cystic fibrosis patients according to the Russian Federation Registry data for 2011–2024
records.summary: Objective: to assess the dynamics of microbiological parameters in cystic fibrosis patients in the Russian Federation over the period 2011–2024. records.title: Intraspecific variation is key for predicting the outcome of bacterial species interactions in the cystic fibrosis lung
records.summary: Background: Cystic fibrosis (CF) is a common recessively inherited disease characterised by consistent chronic lung infections. records.title: Ecological and evolutionary strain-level dynamics of key pathogens in the cystic fibrosis lung microbiome
records.summary: Recent advancements in therapeutics for cystic fibrosis (CF) have drastically extended the lifespan of people with CF; however, periodic pulmonary exacerbations, in part driven by chronic bacterial infection, remain the leading cause of morbidity and mortality. records.title: Validation of an in vitro cystic fibrosis gut microbiome model to investigate drug-microbiome interactions
records.summary: Objectives: Gut symptoms and negative alterations to the gut microbiome in people with cystic fibrosis (CF) persist, despite the use of CFTR modulator therapies. records.title: Incidence and clinical impact of respiratory viruses in adults with cystic fibrosis, preliminary data from the CF-Tracker study
records.summary: Background: Viral respiratory infections (VRIs) are a common potential trigger of pulmonary exacerbations in adults with cystic fibrosis (CF) and are associated with significant morbidity. records.title: Mechanistic and patient-derived insights into virus-bacteria-host interactions during pulmonary exacerbations in children with cystic fibrosis

records.title: Main pathogens of bacterial infections in the lungs of children with cystic fibrosis
