records.confidence_reason: Structured abstract reports isolate counts, organism distribution, susceptibility testing, and resistance findings.

records.title: The link between pulmonary exacerbations and respiratory viruses in young children with cystic fibrosis
records.summary: Objectives: The objective of this study is to assess if viral infections will trigger a Pulmonary Exacerbation (PEx) in children with Cystic Fibrosis (CF) and to identify patient-related risk factors. Methods: Children with CF, aged zero to five years, were included at the pediatric CF clinic of Antwerp University Hospital (UZA) between January 2024 and 2026. Oropharyngeal swabs for the detection of respiratory viruses by PCR were obtained during routine visits and at the time of PEx. Patients were divided into two groups (with and without PEx) and the following patient-related factors was recorded: age, gender, CFTR gene mutation, bacterial colonization, lung function, BMI and socioeconomic factors. Results:...
records.why_it_matters: Virus detection was much more common during exacerbations in preschool children, supporting viral testing in early CF exacerbation assessment.
records.confidence_reason: Structured prospective pediatric study reports cohort size, PCR strategy, odds of viral detection during PEx, and virus types.

records.title: Design of a life-course study of cardiovascular risk and preclinical atherosclerosis in children and adults with cystic fibrosis
records.summary: Objective: With increasing life expectancy, people with CF may face a growing burden of cardiovascular disease (CVD). Insight into cardiovascular risk and early vascular changes from childhood into adulthood is currently limited. This study aims to assess cardiovascular risk factors and preclinical atherosclerosis across the life course in CF. Methods: The study consists of two complementary prospective cohort studies: SMART-Youth, including children with CF aged 8–18 years, and SMART-CF, including adults with CF (≥18 years). Both cohorts undergo comparable cardiovascular assessments. Cardiovascular risk profiling includes blood pressure, BMI, anthropometric measurements (chest, waist and hip circumference), a...
records.why_it_matters: A harmonized pediatric-adult cardiovascular cohort can define when CF care should begin monitoring emerging atherosclerotic risk.
records.confidence_reason: Structured abstract is a study design with ongoing enrollment and no outcome data yet.

records.title: Gathering strengths to improve digestive health
records.summary: Objectives: Despite the advent of highly effective CFTR modulators, digestive symptoms (DS) remain frequently reported by patients with cystic fibrosis (CF). The French patient organisation Vaincre la Mucoviscidose, in collaboration with healthcare professionals, launched a project to better characterize and manage DS in CF. Methods: A core committee conducted two surveys. Questionnaire 1 collected patient-reported outcomes from people with CF or parents of children with CF over the previous four weeks, focusing on DS, their impact on quality of life, and current treatments. Questionnaire 2 gathered clinical experience and care management issues related to DS from CF care teams across 47 CF centers. Finally, a...
secondary_themes: Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
Care burden, adherence, and psychosocial impact
records.why_it_matters: Patient and care-team surveys show digestive symptoms remain common on modulators and expose gaps in specialist gastroenterology access.
records.confidence_reason: Structured project includes large patient survey, national care-team input, and multidisciplinary priority setting.

records.title: Impact of elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor on serum lipid profiles in adults with cystic fibrosis
records.summary: Objectives: Elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor (ETI) has substantially improved outcomes in people with cystic fibrosis (pwCF) but is increasingly associated with metabolic changes, including alterations in serum lipid profiles. The effects on lipid parameters remain unclear. We evaluated changes in serum lipid levels following initiation of ETI. Methods: All adults with CF from a single centre who initiated ETI and had serum lipid measurements (total cholesterol (total-c), low-density cholesterol (LDL-c), high-density cholesterol (HDL-c) and triglycerides (TG)) and body mass index (BMI) at baseline and follow-up were included. Measurements were obtained 0-2 months before ETI initiation and 10-18 months after in...
records.why_it_matters: ETI-associated lipid changes may shift adult CF care toward cardiovascular risk monitoring and dietary counseling.
records.confidence_reason: Structured adult pre/post ETI study reports lipid and BMI changes with clear metabolic interpretation.

records.title: An explorative study on the association between hepatic steatosis and glucometabolic profile in children and adults with cystic fibrosis
records.summary: Objectives: Cystic Fibrosis Liver Disease (CFLD) comprises a spectrum of hepatobiliary manifestations, including hepatic steatosis, biliary disease and cirrhosis, and is an independent risk factor for CFRD. This exploratory study aims to address the knowledge gaps in the link between early glucose abnormalities and hepatic steatosis. Methods: All CF patients (n= 252) attending annual review in 2024 at Ghent University Hospital were eligible, excluding CFRD (n=30), CFLD (n=18), and prior liver transplantation (n=2). Assessments included transient elastography (TE), controlled attenuation parameter (CAP), relative fat mass (FM%) and fat mass index (FMI) measured by BIA, HbA1c, and OGTT. Non-parametric statistics...
records.why_it_matters: Exploring links among steatosis, body composition, and glucose markers may refine cardiometabolic risk assessment in CF liver disease.
records.confidence_reason: Structured abstract reports eligibility, elastography, body composition, glucose testing, and regression findings.

records.title: Oesophageal inflammatory biomarkers correlate with PedsQL gastrointestinal symptom scores in patients with cystic fibrosis on ETI
records.summary: Objectives: Gastrointestinal (GI) manifestations remain burdensome for people with cystic fibrosis (pwCF) as symptoms and inflammation persist despite elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor (ETI) use. While stool calprotectin correlates with patient-reported outcomes, data on upper GI symptom burden and esophageal inflammatory markers remain unexplored. We evaluated correlations between proximal (PE) and distal esophagus (DE) inflammation using a novel minimally invasive esophageal string test (EST) and GI-related quality of life (QoL) in pwCF on and off ETI vs. healthy controls (HC). Methods: This cross-sectional study included pwCF aged 6–25 years on or off ETI and healthy controls. PedsQL™ Gastrointestinal Sympto...
records.why_it_matters: Esophageal inflammatory markers linked to GI symptom scores suggest upper-GI inflammation may persist even during ETI therapy.
records.confidence_reason: Structured cross-sectional study reports age range, ETI groups, biomarker panel, PedsQL correlations, and limitations.

records.title: Is vitamin D deficiency really the primary reason for raised parathyroid hormone (PTH) in people with cystic fibrosis (PwCF)?
records.summary: Objectives: European guidelines recommend routine monitoring of PTH to optimise treatment with vitamin D (vitD). However we observed that PwCF with high PTH were often not vitD deficient. We sought other explanations. Methods: Blood results from Annual Review of adult PwCF between 2016-2025 were analysed. PTH was compared with paired vitD, calcium, phosphate (PO4) and magnesium (Mg). Oral calcium intake was assessed in a subgroup. PwCF with primary hyperparathyroidism were excluded. Results: PTH was raised above 7.2 pmol/l (laboratory ULN) in 159/560 (28%) samples from 47/83 PwCF. There was only weak correlation (r=-0.28) between PTH and vitD. Only a minority (27/159) of raised PTHs were paired with deficient...
records.why_it_matters: Weak PTH-vitamin D correlation questions whether raised PTH should be treated as a straightforward marker of inadequate vitamin D replacement in CF.
records.confidence_reason: Structured adult annual-review analysis reports sample counts, paired labs, pancreatic status associations, and assay concerns.

records.title: Prevalance of autoimmun diseases in children with cystic fibrosis: a prospective registry data analysis
records.summary: Objective: Immune dysregulation in cystic fibrosis (CF) may increase susceptibility to autoimmune diseases (AID). Undiagnosed AID could worsen persistent inflammation, nutritional and endocrine dysfunction, treatment burden, and misinterpretation of CF disease progression. We assessed the prevalence of celiac disease (CD), hypothyroidism requiring thyroid hormone replacement, and pancreatic islet autoantibodies in a pediatric CF cohort. Methods: Single-centre prospective registry data (2019-2025) from 150 children were analysed. CD screening followed ESPGHAN guidelines. Thyroid function was monitored annually via TSH, with hypothyroidism requiring levothyroxine defined as persistently elevated TSH plus low fre...
records.why_it_matters: Prospective pediatric registry screening suggests autoimmune comorbidities and islet autoantibodies may be more common than expected in CF.
records.confidence_reason: Structured registry abstract reports screening definitions, cohort size, and prevalence for celiac, thyroid, and islet autoimmunity.

records.title: Polyketide synthase island-positive E. coli is strongly associated with ileocolonic neoplasia in adults with cystic fibrosis
records.summary: Objectives: Polyketide synthase island-positive (Pks+) E. coli is a genotoxic gut bacterium linked to colorectal cancer (CRC) tumorigenesis via the genotoxin colibactin. In adults with CF, there is an increased incidence and earlier development of CRC, which includes an accelerated progression of adenomatous polyps. We aimed to determine the prevalence of pks+ E. coli in adults with CF and identify microbial factors that promote a tumorigenesis-permissive niche in the gut. ​Methods: Stool samples and DNA were analyzed from the SCREEN-CF study cohort (X18-0379 & HREC/18/RPAH/536). Intestinal inflammation was assessed via fecal calprotectin. Metagenomic taxonomic libraries were prepared using Illumina protocols...
records.why_it_matters: Pks-positive E. coli may become a risk biomarker or intervention target for early colorectal neoplasia in adults with CF.
records.confidence_reason: Structured SCREEN-CF analysis reports colonoscopy groups, pks testing, odds ratio, and biomarker interpretation.

records.title: Assessment of abdominal symptoms and intestinal inflammation in children and adolescents with cystic fibrosis before and after initiation of elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor therapy
records.summary: Introduction: Cystic fibrosis (CF) is a rare genetic disease caused by mutations in the CFTR gene that result in dysfunctional protein and affect several body systems, including the gastrointestinal tract. The changes caused by CF in the GI tract include dysmotility, dysbiosis, intestinal inflammation, and abdominal symptoms such as pain, changes in bowel habits, and distension, which are frequent in people with CF. The study aims to establish the prevalence of these complaints in children with CF and investigate associations between symptoms, clinical conditions, and marker of intestinal inflammation before and after the initiation of therapy with CFTR modulator drug (elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor - ETI)....
records.why_it_matters: High pediatric intestinal inflammation with reduced calprotectin after ETI supports monitoring GI inflammation as part of modulator-era care.
records.confidence_reason: Structured pediatric study reports symptoms, fecal calprotectin, pre/post ETI results, and symptom persistence.

records.title: Assessment of serum liver and kidney function tests 18 months after initiation of elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor in patients with cystic fibrosis
records.summary: Objectives: The novel triple modulatory therapy Elexacaftor/Tezacaftor/Ivacaftor (ETI) has shown clinical efficacy in patients with cystic fibrosis (pwCF). Our aim was to evaluate the impact of ETI on basic serum liver and kidney function tests in pwCF in the University Clinic for Respiratory Diseases in Children - Kozle in Skopje, Republic of North Macedonia 18 months after initiation of the therapy. Methods: Data of 28 pwCF eligible for ETI was obtained. We have monitored aspartate aminotransferase (AST), alanine aminotransferase (ALT), total bilirubin (TB), urea and creatinin on the start and after 18 months of the therapy with ETI. We have used a significant threshold of p<0.05. Sub analysis of age, sex an...
records.why_it_matters: Routine liver and kidney labs after ETI were mostly stable except bilirubin, informing safety monitoring in clinical practice.
records.confidence_reason: Structured single-center pre/post study reports monitored labs, cohort characteristics, and statistical findings.

records.title: Impact of elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor on cholesterol loading and efflux capacity in a cohort of adults with cystic fibrosis
records.summary: Objectives: The aim of this longitudinal study was to describe changes in HDL Cholesterol Efflux Capacity (CEC) and Cholesterol Loading Capacity (CLC) in adults with CF (awCF) after starting therapy with Elexacaftor/Tezacaftor/Ivacaftor (ETI). Methods: Serum of awCF was collected before starting ETI therapy and 6 months later, a pool of healthy controls was also enrolled for healthy serum comparison. HDL was isolated and incubated for 6 hours with different cell lines, to evaluate their capacity to promote cholesterol efflux. ABCA1-mediated CEC was studied on J774A.1 cell line, while ABCG1-mediated CEC was studied using CHO-K1 and CHO-L3 cell lines. CLC was instead measured using macrophages derived from THP-1...
records.why_it_matters: Improved HDL efflux after ETI suggests CFTR correction may alter cardiovascular biology, not just conventional lipid levels.
records.confidence_reason: Structured longitudinal adult serum study reports cell-based cholesterol efflux/loading assays before and after ETI.

records.title: Gastrointestinal manifestations assessed by the CFAbd-Score in patients with cystic fibrosis
records.summary: Introduction: Gastrointestinal (GI) manifestations are common in patients with Cystic Fibrosis (CF). Their etiology can be attributed to three pathophysiological factors: inflammation, dysbiosis, and GI dysmotility. Better understanding the GI symptom context and developing more specific and early treatments are priorities for managing these patients. Objective: To evaluate the prevalence and factors associated to gastrointestinal manifestations in patients with Cystic Fibrosis. Methods: Cross-sectional observational study, conducted between April and September 2024, after approval from the Ethics Committee of the Institution. We included 59 children and adolescents with Cystic Fibrosis treated at the Pediatri...
records.why_it_matters: CFAbd-Score captured high GI symptom prevalence and links with abdominal pain, supporting routine symptom measurement in pediatric care.
records.confidence_reason: Structured cross-sectional pediatric study reports questionnaire use, cohort size, symptom prevalence, and correlations.

records.title: Effect of metformin on clinical outcomes in adults with cystic fibrosis diabetes (CFD)
records.summary: Objective: Cystic fibrosis diabetes (CFD) is a common complication of cystic fibrosis (CF), affecting 40-50% of adults with CF (1). Guidelines state: “insulin may not always be the preferred therapy, and consideration should be given to dietary modification and other available anti-diabetic agents” (2). The aim was to assess the impact of metformin on clinical outcomes of adults with CFD, in a single centre. Method: A retrospective analysis of patients’ data pre- and post-metformin initiation was conducted. Changes in body mass index (BMI), HbA1c, Libre™ time in range (TIR), FEV1 and insulin requirements were evaluated and analysed using paired t-test or Wilcoxon’s signed rank test using SPSS v31. Results: Twe...
records.why_it_matters: Metformin may have a role in selected adult CFRD management, but evidence remains limited by sample size and non-randomized design.
records.confidence_reason: Structured retrospective study reports clinical metrics before and after metformin, with small sample and mostly non-significant tests.

records.title: Changes in fat-soluble vitamin serum concentrations in pediatric patients after introducing cystic fibrosis transmembrane conductance regulator modulator therapy – a single center experience
records.summary: Introduction: Cystic fibrosis (CF) is a multisystem genetic disorder commonly associated with pancreatic insufficiency and impaired absorption of fat-soluble vitamins. Supplementation is guided by annual measurements. This study evaluated the effect of one year CFTR modulator (CFTRm) therapy on serum levels of vitamins A, D, and E. Methods: Serum levels of 25-hydroxyvitamin D [25(OH)D], vitamin A, and vitamin E were measured before initiation of CFTRm therapy (elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor) and after one year. Changes were analyzed using paired-samples t-tests; effect sizes were expressed as Cohen’s d (p<0.05). Statistical analyses were performed using R. Results: Thirty pediatric patients (7-18 years, medi...
records.why_it_matters: Vitamin A and E shifts after pediatric modulator therapy reinforce the need for active fat-soluble vitamin monitoring and dose adjustment.
records.confidence_reason: Structured single-center pediatric pre/post study reports vitamin levels, statistics, and adherence interpretation.

records.title: Appropriateness and acceptability of colonoscopy screening in people with cystic fibrosis (pwCF)
records.summary: Objectives: Because of an increased risk of colonic cancer, colonoscopy screening is recommended for pwCF from the age of 40, and from the age of 30 years post-transplantation. A shared decision approach is advised as pwCF may have other health priorities, complications and co-morbidities. We reviewed the clinical reasons why some pwCF were considered unsuitable for colonoscopy and why some declined the procedure. Results: Of 366 pwCF at our Centre, 84 fell in the age category for considering screening colonoscopy; 17 (20%) were post lung transplant. Fifteen (18%) pwCF were considered unsuitable for screening colonoscopy for medical reasons (11 had advanced lung disease with mean FEV1 24% predicted; 4 had sign...
secondary_themes: Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
records.why_it_matters: Colonoscopy screening decisions must account for advanced lung disease, comorbidities, and patient priorities, with alternatives needed for unsuitable patients.
records.confidence_reason: Structured service review reports eligibility, reasons for unsuitability or refusal, uptake, and screening implications.

records.title: Bowel preparation quality for colonoscopy in adults with cystic fibrosis: real-world outcomes and evaluation of a centre-level CF-specific protocol
records.summary: Objectives: Adequate bowel preparation is critical for effective colorectal polyp and cancer surveillance in people with cystic fibrosis (pwCF). CF-specific bowel preparation regimens require extended duration and higher cumulative laxative exposure. Real-world preparation quality and protocol standardisation are poorly described. This study aimed to characterise bowel preparation quality across CF centres and evaluate a CF-specific protocol at a single centre. Methods: A multi-centre retrospective cohort study of pwCF undergoing colonoscopy across three Australian adult CF centres between 2014–2024 was performed. Bowel preparation quality was categorised using the Aronchick scale (excellent, good/satisfactory...
records.primary_theme: Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
records.why_it_matters: CF-tailored bowel preparation protocols may improve colonoscopy quality and reduce missed lesions or repeat procedures.
records.confidence_reason: Structured multicenter retrospective cohort reports preparation quality and pre/post protocol effects.

records.title: Patterns of referral, investigation, and diagnosis in a specialist adult cystic fibrosis gastroenterology service
records.summary: Background: Gastrointestinal (GI) symptoms are common complications of cystic fibrosis (CF), contributing significantly to disease burden and impacting on quality of life. This has led to the development of a CF gastroenterology outpatient clinic within our adult CF service, led by a specialist gastroenterologist and CF dietitian. Objectives: To describe referral indications, investigations required, and diagnoses made within a specialist CF gastroenterology clinic. Methods: Clinical activity from the monthly hybrid CF gastroenterology clinic was retrospectively reviewed, using electronic medical records (Jan-Nov 2025). Results: Over 11 months, nine gastroenterology clinics with 84 appointments were scheduled....
records.primary_theme: Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
secondary_themes: Care burden, adherence, and psychosocial impact
records.why_it_matters: A specialist CF gastroenterology clinic addresses complex symptom referrals and highlights the value of embedded GI expertise in adult CF services.
records.confidence_reason: Structured clinic review reports appointment activity, referral indications, investigations, diagnoses, and future satisfaction assessment.

records.title: Dynamics of fibrotic changes in the liver according to transient elastography at the start and after 2 years of elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor therapy
records.summary: Hepatic involvement is frequent in patients with cystic fibrosis (CF), with focal biliary cirrhosis being the pathognomonic hepatic manifestation. Transient elastography (TE) (Fibroscan) is a non-invasive, user-friendly and quick technique that provides an objective and reproducible measure of liver stiffness. The aim is to assess the condition of the liver parenchyma according to TE at the start and after 2 years of elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor (ETI) therapy. Objectives: 24 children with CF (10.0±3.8 years), 8 boys and 16 girls were examined before and after 2 years of ETI therapy. Methods: In each case, transient elastography measurement was performed immediately after the routine annual ultrasound evalu...
records.why_it_matters: Improved liver stiffness after two years of ETI suggests some pediatric CF liver changes may be modifiable, though severe cases still need close monitoring.
records.confidence_reason: Structured pediatric pre/post elastography study reports METAVIR shifts, kPa changes, and a cirrhosis case caveat.

records.title: Analysis of fat-soluble vitamin levels during CFTR modulator therapy
records.summary: Patients with cystic fibrosis (CF) have an increased risk of fat-soluble vitamin deficiency, primarily due to malabsorption syndrome and pancreatic insufficiency. Both a deficiency of fat-soluble vitamins and their excess can have a negative impact on health. The aim of the study was to investigate the effect of elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor (ETI) administration in children with CF on levels of fat-soluble vitamins (FSVs) A, D, E, K. Objectives: Based on the results of examination of 117 children (9.4±4.6 years). Methods: Serum vitamin A, E, and K levels were determined before and after 1 year CFTR modulator therapy using HPLC-MS (high-performance liquid chromatography with mass-selective detection), and vi...
records.why_it_matters: Rising high vitamin A and E levels during ETI show supplementation protocols may need reassessment to avoid over-replacement.
records.confidence_reason: Structured pediatric study reports 117 children, HPLC/ELISA measurements, pre/post vitamin changes, and safety concern.

records.title: Cystic fibrosis and bone mineral density: heterozygous residual function CFTR variant not protective against bone disease
records.summary: Objective: Low bone mineral density (BMD) is a common comorbidity in people with cystic fibrosis (pwCF), as CFTR dysfunction impairs osteoblastic bone formation. While the "recessive rescue" phenomenon—where a single residual function (RF) variant preserves pancreatic function—is well established, its impact on skeletal health is unclear. Methods: We retrospectively evaluated adult pwCF at our clinic who completed DEXA scans in the era of highly effective modulator therapy (HEMT). We analyzed BMD at the femoral neck and lumbar spine and stratified pwCF by variant severity: severe/severe (two Class I-III, s/s, n=31), mild/severe (one RF/one Class I-III, m/s, n=37), and mild/mild (two RF, m/m, n=10). Results: 78...
secondary_themes: Precision CFTR biology and rare variants
records.why_it_matters: Residual-function genotype does not necessarily protect against low bone density, so skeletal monitoring should not be relaxed based on pancreatic sufficiency or BMI.
records.confidence_reason: Structured adult DEXA analysis reports genotype strata, BMD differences, and clinical interpretation.

records.title: HbA1c-guided use of CGM for CFRD screening: a single-centre service evaluation
records.summary: Objectives: Regular screening for cystic fibrosis related diabetes (CFRD) is essential in cystic fibrosis (CF) care. The oral glucose tolerance test (OGTT) is the gold standard but is logistically difficult to deliver due to resource constraints. Alternative screening methods include continuous glucose monitoring (CGM) already used locally. The “Cystic Fibrosis–related Diabetes: A First Canadian Clinical Practice Guideline” (2025) recommends an HbA1c-first screening strategy, with OGTT reserved for individuals at higher CFRD risk. This service evaluation assessed CGM-diagnosed CFRD and corresponding HbA1c values to determine whether an HbA1c-first strategy could identify all patients requiring treatment. Metho...
secondary_themes: Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
records.why_it_matters: An HbA1c-first pathway may reduce CFRD screening burden if CGM is targeted to those most likely to need treatment.
records.confidence_reason: Structured service evaluation reports screened adults, CGM-diagnosed CFRD, HbA1c strata, and treatment implications.

records.title: Intestinal inflammation in pediatric cystic fibrosis - Impact of elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor therapy
records.summary: Objective: Cystic fibrosis (CF) is associated with chronic inflammation affecting both the respiratory and gastrointestinal (GI) tract. CFTR modulators may alter intestinal inflammation, though evidence in young children remains scarce. In this study, we examined changes in three complementary fecal inflammatory markers before and during elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor (ETI) therapy to explore whether this treatment is associated with reduced intestinal inflammation. Methods: Fecal samples were collected from 14 children with CF (aged 1.9–6.6 years; all with ≥1 F508del variation; 5 with pancreatic insufficiency) before and ≥6 months after initiation of ETI. The inflammatory markers analyzed were calprotectin...
records.why_it_matters: Declines in fecal inflammatory markers during ETI suggest early modulator treatment may reduce intestinal inflammation in young children.
records.confidence_reason: Structured paired pediatric biomarker study reports calprotectin, neopterin, myeloperoxidase, and significance by marker.

records.title: To assess the impact structured Cystic Fibrosis Diabetes (CFD) education sessions have on glycaemic control using HbA1C as a measurement in the adult Cystic Fibrosis (CF) unit in Northern Ireland (NI)
records.summary: Objective: NICE guideline NG17 (2022) recommends that structured education be an integral part of diabetes care. Further research is needed to identify the requirements of structured educational programmes for people with CFD according to CF Trust guidelines (2022). We have created and implemented a structured CFD education programme in the adult CF unit in NI. Methods: Analysis of electronic care record (ECR) to illustrate most recent HbA1C levels prior to the CFD education sessions. These sessions were presented via Microsoft Teams in a group or on a one-to-one basis as per individual preference, with interaction encouraged. The HbA1C result post education sessions was analysed to help identify changes in gl...
records.primary_theme: Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
secondary_themes: Care burden, adherence, and psychosocial impact
records.why_it_matters: Structured CFRD education may improve glycemic control for some adults while providing a scalable education model for CF diabetes services.
records.confidence_reason: Structured programme evaluation reports participation, HbA1c direction, delivery format, and rollout implications.

records.title: Traditional cardiometabolic risk factors trajectories before and after elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor initiation
records.summary: Objectives: Elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor (ETI) has demonstrated significant improvements in respiratory function and weight gain among people living with cystic fibrosis (pwCF). Several studies have also shown changes in traditional cardiometabolic risk (CMR) factors. However, these were conducted over short time periods and often without longitudinal pre-ETI clinical measurements. We aimed to compare changes in CMR factors over 3 yrs before and after ETI initiation and identify predictors of post-ETI changes. Methods: Ongoing systematic bioclinical data collection within the Montreal Cystic Fibrosis Cohort has allowed access to both pre- and post-ETI data in adult pwCF. In our analyses, we included pwCF w...
records.why_it_matters: Longitudinal pre/post ETI cardiometabolic trajectories suggest baseline characteristics shape post-modulator metabolic risk and monitoring needs.
records.confidence_reason: Structured cohort analysis reports three-year pre/post data, mixed models, and predictors of HbA1c and fructosamine slopes.

records.title: Dynamics of pancreatic function in CFTR modulator therapy: a single-centre analysis of 132 patients
records.summary: Objectives: Pancreatic insufficiency (PI) is a characteristic of cystic fibrosis (CF) and traditionally considered irreversible once established. Cystic fibrosis transmembrane regulator (CFTR) modulator therapies have prompted reconsideration of this assumption, as emerging reports suggest potential improvement in exocrine pancreatic function. However, natural fluctuation of faecal elastase (FE) concentrations has also been observed in people with CF (pwCF), even without targeted treatment. We present a single-centre analysis evaluating longitudinal FE measurements in patients receiving CFTR modulators. Methods: We retrospectively analysed 2203 FE values in pwCF 5 years before and during CFTR modulator initiat...
records.why_it_matters: A subset, especially younger patients, may recover or fluctuate in pancreatic function on modulators, supporting regular fecal elastase reassessment.
records.confidence_reason: Structured single-center analysis reports 2203 fecal elastase values, 132 patients, pancreatic status categories, and enzyme implications.

records.title: Trends in emergency department visits and hospitalizations for gastrointestinal complications in pediatric patients with cystic fibrosis pre and post modulator therapy: a multicenter analysis
records.summary: Objectives: Highly effective modulator therapy (HEMT) has significantly improved pulmonary outcomes in pediatric patients with cystic fibrosis (PwCF), but its effect on gastrointestinal complications remains unclear. This study aims to evaluate changes in emergency department (ED) visits and hospitalizations for constipation and intestinal obstruction before and after the introduction of HEMT. Methods: We conducted a retrospective cohort study using the Pediatric Health Information System (PHIS®) database, including PwCF aged 2–18 years during the pre-HEMT (2014–2018) and post-HEMT (2020–2024) eras. records.why_it_matters: Multicenter administrative data suggest pediatric constipation and obstruction encounters declined after HEMT, extending modulator benefits beyond lungs.
records.confidence_reason: Structured PHIS database study reports era definitions, encounter counts, and statistically significant declines.

records.title: Frequency of liver involvement and its association with genotype in patients with cystic fibrosis
records.summary: Background: Liver disease is a relevant complication of cystic fibrosis (CF); however, the association between CFTR genotype and liver involvement remains unclear. This study evaluated the frequency of liver involvement in CF patients and its relationship with genotype using both classical and functional protein-based classifications. Methods: A total of 457 patients with genetically confirmed CF were included. Liver involvement was classified as cystic fibrosis-related liver disease (CFLD) or non-cirrhotic hepatobiliary involvement. records.primary_theme: Precision CFTR biology and rare variants
records.why_it_matters: Protein-level CFTR genotype classification may better identify patients at risk for hepatobiliary involvement than traditional variant classes.

records.title: Prolonged neonatal jaundice in infants with cystic fibrosis: A retrospective cohort study
records.summary: Background: Neonatal jaundice is common and usually self-limiting; however, persistence may indicate an underlying disorder, including cystic fibrosis, whose role in prolonged jaundice remains unclear. Objectives: To evaluate the frequency of neonatal and prolonged jaundice in people with cystic fibrosis (pwCF) and to assess whether prolonged jaundice should be considered a typical early manifestation of the disease. Methods: We retrospectively compared 298 individuals with cystic fibrosis and 251 healthy children with regard to neonatal jaundice. records.why_it_matters: Prolonged neonatal jaundice was less common in CF than controls, arguing against treating it as a typical early CF sign while still evaluating cholestatic cases.
records.confidence_reason: Structured retrospective cohort compares CF and healthy children with jaundice duration and clinical interpretation.

records.title: Changes in pancreatic function and enzyme requirements in children with cystic fibrosis treated with CFTR modulators
records.summary: Background: CFTR modulator therapy has been associated with improvements in pancreatic function in some children with cystic fibrosis (CF). This study evaluates changes in pancreatic status and pancreatic enzyme replacement therapy (PERT) requirements following initiation of CFTR modulators. Twelve patients were receiving CFTR modulators (9 elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor [ETI], 3 lumacaftor/ivacaftor [Orkambi]); three were not on modulators.
