records.why_it_matters: Some children reduced or stopped PERT after modulators, showing that clinical enzyme needs may change even when fecal elastase remains low.
records.confidence_reason: Structured pediatric retrospective review reports modulator exposure, pancreatic status conversions, PERT dose changes, and FE findings.

records.title: Developing CF-specific diabetes education: insights from a patient involvement group
records.summary: Objective: Despite recommendation in national guidance, there is no structured education for people with cystic fibrosis related diabetes (CFRD). A patient discussion group was undertaken to explore what additional education formats and content are valued alongside existing CF-specific one-to-one (1:1) education clinic to inform development of supplementary. Method: Participants were recruited in CF clinics. Virtual focus group was facilitated by a CF Diabetes Nurse and CF Dietitian exploring five domains: format, content, peer support, family involvement and accessibility. Transcripts were analysed thematically using Copilot-assisted analysis with manual sense-checking. Results: Seven participants (25–52 year...
records.primary_theme: Care burden, adherence, and psychosocial impact
secondary_themes: Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
records.why_it_matters: Patient input favors brief, CF-specific, on-demand diabetes resources as adjuncts to individualized care rather than replacements.
records.confidence_reason: Structured focus-group abstract reports participant characteristics, themes, preferred formats, and co-design implications.

records.title: Effectiveness of bowel preparation for colonoscopy in people with cystic fibrosis after lung transplantation
records.summary: Background: People with cystic fibrosis (pwCF) are at increased risk of colorectal cancer (CRC) with risk increasing following lung transplantation (LT). The Cystic Fibrosis Foundation recommends colonoscopy-based CRC screening with intensive bowel preparation. Altered gastrointestinal physiology and treatment-related factors may reduce bowel preparation quality after LT. Data on bowel preparation effectiveness in post-LT pwCF are limited. Methods: We evaluated bowel preparation quality in our CRC screening protocol for post–LT pwCF using the Boston Bowel Preparation Scale (BBPS) (scores ≥6 adequate) plus subjective assessment by the endoscopist. Extended CF-specific bowel preparation regimen comprised a low-r...
records.primary_theme: Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
records.why_it_matters: Post-lung-transplant CF patients need tailored bowel preparation because inadequate prep remains common despite intensive regimens.
records.confidence_reason: Structured retrospective screening-protocol evaluation reports post-transplant colonoscopy counts, BBPS scores, and repeat-procedure rates.

records.title: Colonic polyp burden after lung transplantation in people with cystic fibrosis
records.summary: Background: In people with cystic fibrosis (pwCF) post lung transplant (LT) the risk of colorectal neoplasia is 25-30 times the risk of the general population, likely due to immunosuppression and accelerated adenoma formation. However limited data describe the prevalence, multiplicity, and histological characteristics of colonic polyps in post-LT pwCF. Methods: We retrospectively reviewed colonoscopy findings in post-LT pwCF managed at a large adult CF center. Indications included CRC surveillance and symptom-driven assessment. Outcomes included polyp detection rate, number of polyps per patient, and histological features. Findings were interpreted in the context of published CF screening cohorts. Results: Nin...
records.why_it_matters: High adenoma and polyp burden after lung transplant supports early, intensive, ongoing colonoscopic surveillance in CF transplant recipients.
records.confidence_reason: Structured retrospective colonoscopy review reports eligible patients, polyp detection, histology, and comparison with CF Foundation data.

records.title: HbA1c in the diagnosis of CFRD: potential for undetected cases
records.summary: Objective: With increasing life-expectancy, prompt diagnosis of CFRD is important to prevent long duration of untreated hyperglycaemia which can lead to microvascular and macrovascular disease. UK guidelines recommend OGTT as gold standard with CGM as a supported screening tool. Canadian guidelines use a two-step approach from age 10 with HbA1c as the initial screen and OGTT reserved for HbA1c 37–48 mmol/mol. We assessed whether this strategy could delay CFRD diagnosis. Method: We retrospectively reviewed adults diagnosed with CFRD over 4 years using OGTT (0,1,2h) plus CGM analysed with Brompton criteria. HbA1c at diagnosis was recorded to determine eligibility under Canadian guidelines. Outcomes included init...
records.why_it_matters: HbA1c-first CFRD screening could miss many CGM-diagnosed cases, highlighting tradeoffs between reduced burden and early detection.
records.confidence_reason: Structured retrospective adult study reports diagnostic pathway comparison, HbA1c strata, medication initiation, and complications.

records.title: Impact of Hybrid Closed Loop (HCL) insulin pump on CFRD management
records.summary: Introduction: Cystic Fibrosis–related Diabetes (CFRD) is one of the most common comorbidities in adults with Cystic Fibrosis (CF). Recent advances have introduced hybrid closed-loop (HCL) continuous subcutaneous insulin infusion (CSII) systems, now available in the United Kingdom. Adults with CFRD (pwCFRD) attending our Adult CF Centre (n=420) using standard CSII were offered transition to HCL systems, which provide automated insulin delivery and the potential to reduce treatment burden. Objectives: To evaluate glycaemic outcomes in adult pwCFRD transitioning from standard CSII to HCL, focusing on time in range (TIR), total daily insulin dose (TDD), glycosylated haemoglobin (HbA1c), BMI, and FEV₁. Methods: Twe...
secondary_themes: Care burden, adherence, and psychosocial impact
Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
records.why_it_matters: Hybrid closed-loop insulin systems may change CFRD management patterns and engagement, but larger studies are needed for sustained clinical impact.
records.confidence_reason: Structured adult pre/post HCL analysis reports device transition, glycemic metrics, insulin dosing, and follow-up duration.

records.title: PwCF satisfaction of Hybrid Closed Loop insulin delivery system for CFRD management
records.summary: Introduction: Cystic Fibrosis Related Diabetes (CFRD) is a common complication of CF, with treatment options including oral medication, insulin injections and Continuous Subcutaneous Insulin Infusion (CSII). Hybrid Closed Loop (HCL) CSII has recently become available in the UK, with evidence of improved glycaemic control and quality of life. At our large adult CF centre (n=420), 22 people with CF (pwCF) [13 male, mean age 37 years)] are using HCL; we evaluated their experience of transitioning from standard CSII to HCL. Method: An online anonymous survey was sent to 22 pwCF using HCL (13 male, mean age 36.7 years), with a 73% (n=16) response rate. The survey assessed treatment burden, glucose control and satis...
records.primary_theme: Care burden, adherence, and psychosocial impact
secondary_themes: Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
records.why_it_matters: High satisfaction with hybrid closed-loop therapy suggests diabetes technology may reduce perceived CFRD burden when multidisciplinary support is available.
records.confidence_reason: Structured survey reports response rate, satisfaction, perceived burden, recommendations, and support themes.

records.title: Is there a role for C-peptide in the management of cystic fibrosis diabetes?
records.summary: Objectives: Measurement of C-peptide can be used to assess endogenous insulin secretion and extent of residual beta-cell secretion in diabetes. It has been used to identify diabetes type and guide suitable treatment options with non-CF populations, reducing unnecessary insulin therapy and episodes of hypoglycaemia. Little is known about its use in cystic fibrosis diabetes (CFD). We investigated C-peptide levels in our adult CF cohort and its association with treatment management of CFD. Methods: Ninety-six pwCF (mean age 35.2years [SD 11.8], 42% female) had a non-fasting C-peptide test alongside their routine annual screen. Clinical thresholds for serum C-peptide were extrapolated from type-1 diabetes with C p...
records.why_it_matters: C-peptide may help stratify insulin deficiency and tailor CFRD treatment, complementing glucose metrics.
records.confidence_reason: Structured adult cohort reports C-peptide thresholds, treatment groups, odds of insulin use, and HbA1c association.

records.title: Long-term trajectories of bone health in the CFTR modulator era
records.summary: Objectives: CF related bone disease is a significant comorbidity amongst adult patients with CF. This may prove more problematic with increased life expectancy and additional effects of aging. Pulmonary and nutritional complications have shown substantial improvement with CFTR modulators but the effect on bone health remains unclear. We reviewed the progression of bone density, alongside clinical parameters, in adults with CF following introduction of Elexacaftor/Tezacaftor/Ivacaftor (ETI) therapy. Methods: Retrospective longitudinal data was collated for consecutive patients undergoing routine dual-energy X-ray absorptiometry (DEXA) scans at our large adult CF centre. Participants with DEXA at 3 time points (...
records.why_it_matters: Femoral bone density loss persisted after ETI, supporting continued bone surveillance even as other CF outcomes improve.
records.confidence_reason: Structured longitudinal adult DEXA study reports multiple timepoints, post-ETI trajectories, and risk-factor modeling.

records.title: Integrating elastography into annual clinical assessments for adults with cystic fibrosis
records.summary: Objectives: To describe our experience of integrating Vibration Controlled Transient Elastography (VCTE) into the CF annual clinical assessment pathway. Background: CF-related liver disease (CFLD) remains a major cause of mortality in pwCF. However, progression of CFLD remains poorly understood, with investigations such as ultrasonography and liver function often being the mainstay of monitoring. The advent of non-invasive VCTE provides measures of steatosis (controlled Attenuation Parameter (CAP)), and liver stiffness, both of which have been validated in non-CF liver disease. Methods: Three respiratory physicians at the Leeds Adult CF Unit were formally trained and certified in undertaking VCTE using Fibrosc...
records.primary_theme: Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
records.why_it_matters: Embedding elastography into annual adult CF assessment could improve practical liver-disease surveillance beyond routine labs and ultrasound.
records.confidence_reason: Structured service implementation abstract reports training, scan workflow, early results, and monitoring rationale.

records.title: Assessment of uptake and barriers to bowel cancer screening in patients with cystic fibrosis
records.summary: Objectives: People with cystic fibrosis (CF) have an increased incidence of colorectal cancer (CRC). Screening colonoscopies are currently the gold-standard screening test. Screening is commonly offered from 40yr in the UK, local practice is to offer screening from 30yr. We audited how many eligible patients at our centre had been offered screening, the proportion of patients undergoing screening, and reasons for patients declining screening. Methods: Audit of clinical notes (2017-2025) of CF patients ≥30yr at a single UK adult CF centre. The number of patients offered screening, completing screening, and declining screening were recorded. A sample of patients who did not consent to referral were asked why. Re...
secondary_themes: Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
records.why_it_matters: Bowel cancer screening audits can reveal missed eligible patients and documentation gaps that are fixable through review workflows.
records.confidence_reason: Structured audit reports eligible population, offer and consent rates, barriers, and process changes.

records.title: Central precocious puberty in cystic fibrosis: an uncommon finding raising questions in the CFTR modulator era
records.summary: Cystic fibrosis (CF) has been associated with delayed puberty, mainly related to malnutrition, respiratory disease severity, and metabolic comorbidities. Over recent decades, improvements in comprehensive care and the introduction of CFTR modulators have allowed pubertal progression in many patients to more closely resemble that of the general population. However, central precocious puberty remains an exceptionally rare finding in this population, with limited available evidence and a lack of studies specifically evaluating its association with CF or CFTR modulator therapy. Case report: 9 year old female patient with a neonatal diagnosis of CF (homozygous F508del), pancreatic insufficiency, and moderate respir...
records.why_it_matters: A rare precocious puberty case flags uncertainty about pubertal development patterns as nutrition and modulator exposure change in CF.
records.confidence_reason: Evidence is a single case report with explicit statement that causal inference is not possible.

records.title: Modified, activated and natural clinoptilolite might influence adsorption capacity and anti-inflammatory effect on the intestinal mucosa in CF
records.summary: Objectives: TOXAPREVENT® MEDI contains the active ingredient MANC® (modified, activated, natural clinoptilolite), which selectively binds harmful substances such as histamine, ammonium, heavy metals and mycotoxins in the gastrointestinal tract and can remove them from the body – without depriving it of nutrients. The combination of adsorption capacity and anti-inflammatory effect on the intestinal mucosa could be particularly beneficial for cystic fibrosis patients. Therefore, we evaluated the use of MANC® in people with CF (pwCF) who received the standard therapy in terms of their gastrointestinal complaints and disease. Methods: PwCF who received standard of care therapy due to gastrointestinal symptoms, did...
secondary_themes: Emerging genetic, RNA, and cell-model therapies
records.why_it_matters: Clinoptilolite therapy may reduce GI symptom burden in selected adults, but prospective controlled evidence is still needed.
records.confidence_reason: Structured retrospective observational study reports CFAbd-Score response but lacks a control group and has small sample size.

records.title: Longitudinal impact of CFTR modulator therapy on hepatobiliary biochemistry and liver stiffness in children with cystic fibrosis: prospectively collected single-centre observational data
records.summary: Background: Clinically significant hepatobiliary involvement (HBI) affects 15–30% of children with cystic fibrosis (cwCF). It is unclear whether CFTR modulator therapy alters the natural course of CF-related hepatobiliary disease (CFHBI), and long-term effects on hepatobiliary biomarkers, including liver stiffness, remain largely unknown. We aimed to evaluate the impact of 36-month CFTR modulator therapy on CFHBI in cwCF. Methods: In this single-centre prospective study, liver biomarkers including aspartate aminotransferase (AST), alanine aminotransferase (ALT), gamma-glutamyl transferase (GGT), AST to platelet ratio index (APRI), GGT to platelet ratio (GPR), and liver stiffness measurement by 2D-shear-wave el...
records.why_it_matters: Three-year pediatric data suggest modulators improve liver biochemistry but may not reverse liver stiffness, refining expectations for hepatobiliary monitoring.
records.confidence_reason: Structured prospective single-center study reports biomarkers and elastography before and after 36 months.

records.title: Effect of CFTR modulators on glucose tolerance in children with cystic fibrosis: an age- and sex-matched cohort study
records.summary: Objectives: Cystic fibrosis–related abnormal glucose tolerance (CF-AGT) is a progressive, age-dependent complication, with ~50% of patients developing cystic fibrosis–related diabetes (CFRD) by their forties. Despite widespread CFTR modulator use, the impact on glucose tolerance remains uncertain. Most studies use self-controlled pre–post designs that fail to disentangle treatment effects from natural CF-AGT progression. We hypothesised modulators mitigate age-related CF-AGT worsening. We aimed to isolate modulator effects in a prospective pediatric registry-based matched control analysis. Methods: We analysed prospectively collected registry data comparing CFTR modulator-treated children with untreated contro...
records.why_it_matters: Matched pediatric registry data did not show clear glucose-metabolism benefit from modulators, cautioning against assuming CFRD risk is reduced.
records.confidence_reason: Structured matched cohort analysis reports treated and control groups, OGTT data availability, and null metabolic findings.

records.title: Determinants of vitamin D status in children with cystic fibrosis: a GEE-based analysis
records.summary: Objectives: Vitamin D insufficiency is common in children with cystic fibrosis (CF). Despite routine supplementation, maintaining adequate serum 25-hydroxyvitamin D (25(OH)D) levels remains challenging. The aim of this study was to evaluate determinents of vitamin D status, and examine the relationship between vitamin D supplementation dose and vitamin D status. Methods: Repeated measurements from 275 children with CF are used to classify vitamin D status as sufficient (>30), insufficient (20–30), and deficient (<20). For determinants of this outcome we applied a Generalized estimating equations (GEE)-based ordinal (cumulative logit) model with an exchangeable working correlation structure in order to account...
records.why_it_matters: Persistent vitamin D insufficiency despite supplementation suggests dosing and pancreatic-status assumptions may need revision in children.
records.confidence_reason: Structured repeated-measures pediatric analysis reports sample size, GEE model, determinants, and estimated dose implications.

records.title: Empowering people with cystic fibrosis (CF) related diabetes: preliminary findings from the rollout of CF specific structured diabetes education sessions
records.summary: Objective: Structured diabetes education is recommended by the UK National Institute for Health and Care Excellence, as it supports effective self-management and improves outcomes such as HbA1c. A third of people with CF aged ≥16 years receive treatment for CF-related diabetes (CFRD) in the UK. To promote equitable access to structured education for people with CFRD (pwCFRD), we developed a session modelled on existing courses for Type 2 Diabetes. Method: CF dietitians designed a structured, interactive session covering CFRD aetiology, monitoring, complications, treatments, carbohydrate awareness, glycaemic index (GI) and food labels. Since late 2023, sessions have been delivered online in small groups or indi...
records.primary_theme: Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
secondary_themes: Care burden, adherence, and psychosocial impact
records.why_it_matters: Online structured CFRD education may improve confidence, knowledge, and access to self-management support across adult services.
records.confidence_reason: Structured rollout evaluation reports session content, feedback, confidence changes, and HbA1c comparison plan.

records.title: Prevalence of hypercalciuria in the era of highly effective CFTR modulators
records.summary: The introduction of Elexacaftor/Tezacaftor/Ivacaftor (ETI) has significantly improved the prognosis for People with Cystic Fibrosis (pwCF). However, increased survival has shifted clinical attention toward long-term complications, including metabolic and renal disorders. Among these, hypercalciuria, defined as elevated urinary calcium excretion in the absence of hypercalcemia, may predispose to nephrocalcinosis, kidney stones, and potential impairment of renal function. In pwCF not treated with ETI, the reported prevalence of hypercalciuria ranges from 30% to 60%. In the era of highly effective CFTR modulators, its prevalence and clinical relevance remain largely unknown. Aim: To evaluate the prevalence of hyp...
records.why_it_matters: Hypercalciuria prevalence on ETI appeared low, but asymptomatic stones support continued renal-metabolic monitoring as CF patients age.
records.confidence_reason: Structured cross-sectional study reports prevalence and imaging findings, but population spans children and adults and clinical impact is limited.

records.title: The syndrome of electrolyte disturbances (Pseudo-Bartter's syndrome, PBS) in children with cystic fibrosis from various subjects of the North Caucasus
records.summary: Objectives: In some previous articles, it was shown that the main feature of cystic fibrosis in patients from the Chechen Republic is the predominance of PBS. Currently, there are no studies aimed at analyzing the prevalence of PBS in other regions of the North Caucasus and its association with CFTR gene mutations has not been established. Aim: To investigate the prevalence of PBS among patients in the North Caucasus and establish its association with CFTR gene mutations. Methods: This study included 160 patients from various republics of the North Caucasus: Chechen – 83 patients, Karachay-Cherkess – 28, Dagestan – 22, Ingushetia – 13, North Ossetia-Alania – 9, Kabardino-Balkaria – 5. In Chechen, correlation a...
secondary_themes: Precision CFTR biology and rare variants
Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
records.why_it_matters: High Pseudo-Bartter frequency in North Caucasus children appears driven more by climate and care practices than CFTR variant, informing prevention counseling.
records.confidence_reason: Structured regional pediatric analysis reports 160 patients, republic-level frequencies, genotype tests, and environmental interpretation.

records.title: Liver involvement in people with cystic fibrosis treated with elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor: a retrospective single-center study
records.summary: Objectives: Elexacaftor/Tezacaftor/Ivacaftor (ETI) is approved for people with CF (pwCF) ≥2 years with at least one F508del variant. This retrospective single-center study aimed to evaluate liver involvement in pwCF treated with ETI. Methods: PwCF treated with ETI at the CF Center of the Basilicata Region were included. Demographic data and laboratory parameters (gamma-glutamyl transferase (GGT), alanine aminotransferase (ALT), aspartate aminotransferase (AST), total bilirubin (TB), and platelet count) were collected at baseline and after 6, 12, and 24 months. Non-invasive biomarkers of CF–related liver disease (CFLD) were calculated, including the AST-to-platelet ratio index (APRI), the GGT-to-platelet ratio...
records.why_it_matters: ETI showed a favorable liver safety profile over two years, supporting routine but not alarmist hepatobiliary monitoring.
records.confidence_reason: Structured retrospective study reports 73 patients, serial labs, non-invasive fibrosis indices, FibroScan subset, and conclusion.

records.title: The impact of elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor on bone mineral density in children and adults with cystic fibrosis
records.summary: Objectives: The highly effective cystic fibrosis transmembrane conductance regulator (CFTR) therapy elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor (ETI) has transformed the management of people with Cystic Fibrosis (PwCF). However, its effects on bone mineral density (BMD) remain uncertain. Methods: We conducted a longitudinal cohort study assessing changes in BMD before and after ETI initiation in children and adults with CF across five Irish hospitals. Participants were identified from RECOVER, a multicentre observational study of real-world ETI outcomes. Dual-energy X-ray absorptiometry (DXA) scans were analysed both pre- and post-ETI initiation. Paired t-tests assessed BMD changes, with effect sizes calculated using Coh...
records.why_it_matters: Multicenter DXA data found no significant BMD change after ETI, showing bone benefits may require longer follow-up or targeted interventions.
records.confidence_reason: Structured RECOVER cohort analysis reports paired adult and child DXA data, follow-up intervals, and biochemical findings.

records.title: Comparing gastrointestinal symptoms (CFAbd-Score) in patients with cystic fibrosis and with inflammatory bowel disease – Early results (COMET-CF-IBD)
records.summary: Objectives: Chronic intestinal inflammation is a hallmark of CF and is associated with a markedly elevated burden of abdominal symptoms (AS) in people with CF (pwCF). Consequently, comparing AS in pwCF, people with inflammatory bowel disease (pwIBD) and healthy controls (HC) may provide new insights and contribute to a better understanding of GI involvement in pwCF. COMET-CF-IBD (Comprehensive Omic and SyMptom Evaluation, a Translational study) aims to improve understanding of chronic gastrointestinal inflammation and its clinical impact in pwCF. Methods: Using the CFAbd-Score, the first CF-specific GI-PROM developed and validated following FDA-guidelines, we assessed and compared AS in children and adults wit...
records.presenter: Jochen G. Mainz (Brandenburg an der Havel, DE)
records.why_it_matters: Comparing CFAbd-Score across CF, IBD, and controls contextualizes CF GI symptom burden and possible improvement during ETI.
records.confidence_reason: Structured COMET-CF-IBD abstract reports groups, CFAbd-Score domains, ETI subgroup, and statistical comparisons.

records.title: Bone density, physical activity levels, and nutritional status among adolescents and young adults with cystic fibrosis
records.summary: Aim: To assess the bone health of adolescents and young adults with CF, and explore the correlation between bone mineral density, physical activity, and markers of nutritional status. Methods: Our study included 30 patients with CF. Bone mineral density was assessed by dual-energy X-ray absorptiometry (DXA). Measurements were performed at the right and left femoral bones and the lumbar spine; results were expressed as Z-scores. Physical activity levels were assessed using the Godin Leisure-Time Activity Questionnaire. With respect to nutritional markers, we examined body mass index (BMI), 25-hydroxyvitamin D, calcium, phosphorus, and handgrip strength (HGS). HGS was assessed by using an electronic hand dynamom...
records.why_it_matters: Reduced bone density in adolescents and young adults supports ongoing bone-health monitoring even when associations with activity or nutrition are unclear.
records.confidence_reason: Structured observational study reports DXA and activity/nutrition measures but small sample limits correlation findings.

records.title: Elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor effects on pancreatic involution in cystic fibrosis: a longitudinal CT study
records.summary: Objective: To evaluate the potential influence of elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor (ETI) therapy on the progression of pancreatic imaging abnormalities in patients with Cystic Fibrosis. Methods: A total of 196 patients with Cystic Fibrosis treated with ETI and who underwent High-Resolution Computed Tomography (HRCT) were included (93 males, 103 females; mean age at first CT 15 years; mean age at start of therapy 24 years). A longitudinal evaluation of the pancreatic parenchyma included in the caudal portions of chest HRCT scans was performed using available pre- and post-therapy examinations (mean follow-up 88 months; 774 CT scans analyzed). Pancreatic parenchyma was independently segmented by two readers, and...
records.why_it_matters: Longitudinal CT suggests ETI changes the trajectory of pancreatic involution, adding imaging evidence to pancreatic-function questions.
records.confidence_reason: Structured imaging study reports 196 patients, 774 CT scans, segmentation methods, and significant slope changes.

records.title: Changing pancreatic status of patients in a UK paediatric cystic fibrosis network from diagnosis to present
records.summary: Objectives: Pancreatic insufficiency (PI) is a common complication in cystic fibrosis (CF), historically considered irreversible in most patients. However, with earlier diagnosis and improved therapies, pancreatic status may change over time, particularly in patients who are able to start highly effective modulator therapy (HEMT) at a younger age. Our aim was to evaluate changes in pancreatic status among paediatric CF patients within our regional network, from diagnosis to present. Methods: We undertook a retrospective audit of all faecal elastase (FE-1) levels measured in all CF patients in a paediatric network, from their diagnostic test, up to present. Exclusion criteria were patients with a Cystic Fibrosi...
records.why_it_matters: Pancreatic status can change in young children, especially after early HEMT, supporting routine fecal elastase monitoring in pediatric networks.
records.confidence_reason: Structured pediatric network audit reports 282 patients, direction of status changes, and age/timing patterns.

records.title: Reviewing the necessity for annual liver ultrasounds in children with cystic fibrosis - A comparison to the Cystic Fibrosis Standards of Care
records.summary: Objectives: National CF standards recommend liver ultrasound (USS) from age three, with further imaging as clinically indicated. At CUH, a paediatric CF centre, local guidance mandates annual liver USS from age one, creating a more intensive surveillance programme. This audit assessed adherence to both national and local standards and explored whether surveillance frequency could be reduced whilst still enabling earliest possible detection of liver complications. Methods: Electronic records for all children with CF at CUH (2014-2025) were reviewed. Exclusions: aged <12 months, >16 years, or CF-SPID. Data collection included USS frequency per patient, positive USS liver findings, age at detection, and if subseq...
records.primary_theme: Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
records.why_it_matters: Risk-stratified liver ultrasound surveillance could reduce unnecessary imaging while preserving detection of higher-risk pediatric liver disease.
records.confidence_reason: Structured audit reports national versus local standards, ultrasound findings, missed scans, and consultant recommendations.

records.title: Comparison of carbohydrate metabolism in children with cystic fibrosis with and without variant-specific therapy
records.summary: Objectives: carbohydrate metabolism disorders (CMD) in people with cystic fibrosis (CF) remain clinically relevant even in the era of variant-specific therapy (VST). Despite the pronounced clinical effect of VST in CF, published data on its impact on glucose metabolism are contradictory, and the prevalence and patterns of glucose abnormalities in children are still insufficiently studied. Therefore, assessment of glucose metabolism in children with CF (cwCF) receiving VST is of interest. Methods: twenty cwCF and elevated HbA1c were included in the study. Group I — 10 children receiving VST (median age (Me) = 14.27 years [11.64–14.73]; 2 children on two-component and 8 on three-component regimens); Group II — 1...
records.why_it_matters: Variant-specific therapy may improve pediatric glucose patterns, but all children still require metabolic monitoring regardless of therapy status.
records.confidence_reason: Structured comparative study reports OGTT patterns in only 20 children and notes continued follow-up is needed.

records.title: Clinical impact of elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor on growth and nutritional parameters in children with cystic fibrosis aged 6-12 Years
records.summary: Objectives: Cystic fibrosis (CF) can lead to nutritional challenges in pediatric patients, including malnutrition and growth failure. The advent of CF modulators, such as elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor (ETI), has brought significant improvements in lung function and nutritional status. The aim of our study is to explore the impact of ETI treatment on growth parameters in children aged 6-12 years (yrs) with CF. Methods: Patients with CF (pwCF) were evaluated before the start (T0), after 1 year (T1), and after 2 yrs (T2) of ETI treatment. At each time point, weight (kg), weight Z-score, height (cm), height Z-score, Body Mass Index (BMI, kg/m²), BMI Z-score, Fat Mass (FM, kg), Fat Free Mass (FFM, kg), Mid-Upper...
records.why_it_matters: Two-year pediatric growth and body-composition improvements after ETI highlight changing nutritional needs for children starting modulators early.
records.confidence_reason: Structured pre/post pediatric study reports serial anthropometry, body composition, lung function, and correlations.

records.title: EASE-CF: a randomised controlled feasibility trial of intentional weight loss in adults with cystic fibrosis
records.summary: Background and objectives: Approximately 40% of adults with cystic fibrosis (CF) now live with overweight or obesity due to advances in CF care and improved survival. Despite this emerging clinical issue, no trials of intentional weight loss strategies have been conducted in this population, and the feasibility , acceptability and safety of weight loss programmes remain unknown. The primary objective of this study is to determine the feasibility of progression to a definitive randomised controlled trial (RCT). Methods: This abstract describes the protocol for a 24-week, multicentre, unblinded, parallel-group randomised controlled feasibility trial with an embedded qualitative study. A sample of 30 adults with...
secondary_themes: Care burden, adherence, and psychosocial impact
Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
records.why_it_matters: A feasibility RCT will test whether intentional weight loss interventions are acceptable and safe for adults with CF as overweight becomes common.
records.confidence_reason: Structured protocol describes trial design, intervention, and approvals but has no outcome data yet.

records.title: A cystic fibrosis-specific nutritional screening questionnaire for early identification of patients at nutritional risk
records.summary: Objectives: To develop and evaluate a cystic fibrosis (CF) specific nutritional screening questionnaire for early identification of adult patients at nutritional risk in routine outpatient care.​ ​Methods: A CF-specific nutritional screening questionnaire was developed by a clinical dietitian and implemented in routine outpatient care over an 8-month period. The questionnaire assesses recent weight loss, appetite changes, gastrointestinal symptoms, stool consistency, and self-reported functional capacity. Items were weighted and scored, classifying patients as low (0–4 points), moderate (5–8 points), or high (≥9 points) nutritional risk. Four questionnaires were excluded due to incomplete completion. Data were...
secondary_themes: Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
records.why_it_matters: A CF-specific nutrition screening questionnaire may identify risk missed by BMI alone, enabling earlier dietetic intervention.
records.confidence_reason: Structured implementation abstract reports questionnaire domains, scoring, patient classification, and high-risk example.

records.title: Longitudinal trends in nutritional status and associations with lung function in adults with cystic fibrosis: data from the UK CF Registry
records.summary: Introduction: Optimising nutritional status (NS) remains a key priority for adults with CF (awCF). Reports on NS trends in the UK are limited and the relationship between BMI and lung function in the CFTR modulator era remains unclear. Aim: To describe longitudinal trends in NS in UK awCF and associations of BMI and BMI change with clinical outcomes by modulator use. Method: Data on all awCF aged ≥ 18 years recorded in the UK CF Registry between 2007-2023 were analysed. AwCF were categorised by BMI according to ESPEN-ESPGHAN 2024 definitions: underweight, target range, overweight, and obese. Linear models were used to study the associations of BMI and BMI change with lung function, by modulator use. Results: D...
records.why_it_matters: UK registry trends show adult CF nutrition is shifting from underweight toward overweight and obesity, changing the risk-benefit frame for BMI targets.
records.confidence_reason: Structured registry analysis reports 8,869 adults, 82,604 annual reviews, BMI trends, and lung-function associations.

records.title: Impact of early elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor treatment on growth and pancreatic function in children with cystic fibrosis
records.summary: Objectives: To evaluate real-world effects of elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor (ETI) on nutritional status and pancreatic exocrine function in children with cystic fibrosis (CF) following its introduction into clinical practice in the Czech Republic. Methods: A retrospective analysis was performed 12 months after ETI became available for children aged 2–6 years. Patients younger than 12 years at ETI initiation were included. Changes in body mass index (BMI, percentile) and faecal pancreatic elastase were analysed. Subgroup analyses were conducted according to prior treatment with lumacaftor/ivacaftor (LUM/IVA). Wilcoxon signed-rank test was used for paired comparisons. Results: The cohort comprised 38 children...
records.why_it_matters: Early ETI improved BMI and pancreatic elastase particularly in children without prior LUM/IVA, supporting early pediatric initiation.
records.confidence_reason: Structured retrospective pediatric study reports cohort, subgroup analyses, BMI percentile, and elastase changes.

records.title: Nutritional improvement after one year of elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor therapy in patients with cystic fibrosis in North Macedonia
records.summary: Background: Nutritional impairment remains a major challenge in patients with cystic fibrosis and is strongly associated with poorer pulmonary outcomes and reduced survival. The introduction of highly effective CFTR modulator therapy, particularly the triple combination elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor (ETI) has been associated with significant improvements in nutritional status, including weight gain and body mass index (BMI). Aim: To evaluate changes in BMI and nutritional status after 1 year of ETI in patients with cystic fibrosis. Methods: This retrospective observational cohort included 54 patients with cystic fibrosis (mean age 19.5±6.8 years; range 7–40 years). Anthropometric data were collected at base...
records.why_it_matters: North Macedonian real-world data show rapid BMI gains and reduced undernutrition after one year of ETI, supporting nutritional reassessment after initiation.
records.confidence_reason: Structured retrospective cohort reports 54 patients, repeated BMI timepoints, category shifts, and pediatric subgroup improvement.

records.title: Diet quality in Brazilian adolescents with cystic fibrosis
records.summary: Objectives: To describe the quality of the diet and its relationship with lung function and nutritional status in adolescents with cystic fibrosis (CF). Methods: A questionnaire was applied to 47 adolescents (12–19 years old) followed at a university reference center. Lung function impairment was classified as mild (FEV1pp≥60%), moderate (FEV1pp 41–59%) or severe (FEV1pp<40%) using spirometry. Food consumption frequency was classified as rarely, 1-2 times/week, 3-4 times/week, or ≥ 5 times/week. Linear regression analysis was used to model the relationship between lung function, BMI/age Z-score and food frequency. Principal component analysis (PCA) was performed to identify patterns of diet quality. Results: T...
records.why_it_matters: Diet-quality patterns in Brazilian adolescents link fruit, vegetables, and meat intake with BMI and lung-function measures, supporting dietary counseling beyond calories.
records.confidence_reason: Structured adolescent study reports questionnaire methods, regression models, PCA approach, and dietary associations.

records.title: Nutritional status and bone strength in children and adolescents with cystic fibrosis: an observational study
records.summary: Introduction: Cystic fibrosis (CF) is an autosomal recessive hereditary disease. Malnutrition is a common clinical manifestation, mainly due to malabsorption, decreased energy intake, and increased nutritional needs. Additionally, bone metabolism in these patients may also be compromised, resulting in low bone density and a consequent increased risk of fracture. This study aims to characterize the nutritional status and the bone strength (BS) of children and adolescents with CF. Materials and Methods: This is an observational, analytical, cross-sectional study. Clinical history (type of mutation, pharmacotherapy, FEV1, and serum parameters) was collected in 25 patients. Nutritional status was characterized by...
records.why_it_matters: Osteopenia can be present despite generally adequate nutrition, supporting preventive bone-health care in pediatric CF follow-up.
records.confidence_reason: Structured cross-sectional pediatric study reports nutrition status, bone ultrasound results, and FEV1 association.

records.title: Assessment of Fat Mass Index and Fat-Free Mass Index in adult patients with cystic fibrosis after initiation of triple modulator therapy
records.summary: Cystic Fibrosis (CF) is a hereditary disease characterized by a mutation in the Cystic Fibrosis Transmembrane Conductance Regulator (CFTR) gene. Progressive pulmonary disease, exocrine pancreatic insufficiency, and increased concentrations of chloride and sodium ions in sweat are the most evident manifestations of CF. Therapeutic approaches aim to delay pulmonary deterioration and maintain an adequate nutritional status. Advances in multidisciplinary CF care have been reflected in improved disease prognosis. Triple modulator therapy (TMT) is an effective therapeutic option, contributing to improvements in nutritional status. Objectives: Analyze the impact of TMT on Fat Mass Index (FMI) and Fat-Free Mass Index...
records.why_it_matters: Triple modulator therapy changed adult body composition, implying that post-modulator nutrition care should track fat and lean mass rather than BMI alone.
records.confidence_reason: Structured observational study reports significant FMI/FFMI changes but limited detail on magnitude and omitted lifestyle factors.

records.title: Consensus derived research gaps in the nutritional management of children with cystic fibrosis (cwCF) on elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor (ETI)
records.summary: Objectives: ETI has significantly altered the nutritional trajectory of cwCF, with clinical rollout outpacing the development of nutritional guidance. This study aimed to 1. systematically review the literature on ETI and nutrition 2. develop expert consensus-based guidance 3. highlight research gaps. Methods: This project was registered on PROSPERO (ID: 587618) and guided by a multidisciplinary panel of 22 clinicians and a parent representative. Research questions were developed using the PICO framework across domains including anthropometry, body composition, vitamins, minerals, cholesterol, diet, endocrine and exocrine pancreatic status and ETI administration. A literature search was conducted across Medlin...
secondary_themes: Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
records.why_it_matters: Consensus research gaps show pediatric ETI nutrition guidance is lagging behind clinical rollout, especially around BMI targets and pancreatic reassessment.
records.confidence_reason: Structured systematic review and Delphi process reports panel composition, domains, and prioritized consensus statements.

records.title: A retrospective analysis of the relationship between body composition, muscle strength and function in cystic fibrosis
records.summary: Objectives: Reduced muscle mass, strength and function are associated with poor exercise tolerance, and survival in people with CF. This study aimed to characterise and explore the relationship between body composition, muscle strength and function in people with CF (pwCF). Methods: Retrospective data was collected from clinically stable adults who attended annual review assessments in 2024 and 2025. Data on age, genotype, CFTR modulator use, FEV1, exocrine pancreatic status, diabetes status and P. Aeruginosa colonisation was recorded. Body composition was measured using bioelectrical impedance analysis (Seca M515). Muscle strength was assessed using Peak Torque at 60°/s (Biodex® PRO 3), and muscle function us...
records.why_it_matters: Common muscle impairment despite normal BMI and lung function supports routine body composition and strength assessment plus targeted exercise-nutrition interventions.
records.confidence_reason: Structured retrospective adult review reports body composition, strength, CPET measures, and significant associations.

records.title: Dietetic prescribing for the nutritional management of cystic fibrosis
records.summary: Objectives: Non-medical prescribing (NMP) can improve service efficiency, innovation and is cost saving (Babashahi et al, 2025). In the UK, legislation changed in 2016 and dietitians can now train to become supplementary prescribers (SP). Cystic fibrosis (CF) is a multisystem disease involving pancreatic exocrine insufficiency, diabetes and gastrointestinal symptoms such as reflux and constipation. The CF Dietitian has the specialist knowledge to prescribe medications related to the nutritional management of patients with CF (NHS England, 2015). SP prescribe medications from a clinical management plan (CMP) agreed between a doctor and the SP. There are no specific formulary restrictions, though the SP must pre...
records.primary_theme: Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
records.why_it_matters: Dietitian supplementary prescribing may streamline CF nutrition-related medication management, particularly during PERT supply constraints.
records.confidence_reason: Structured service evaluation reports prescribing episodes, medication classes, consent/CMP process, and absence of recorded errors.

records.title: The impact of modulator therapies on pancreatic function in children with cystic fibrosis
records.summary: Background: Recent studies show that CFTR modulator use in children with Cystic Fibrosis (CF) improves gastrointestinal symptoms, reduces pancreatic enzyme use, improves vitamin status and may lead to recovery of pancreatic function (Rosenfeld Lancet Resp Med 2018). A 2022 review at our centre found 3/9 children with CF on modulators, who had a faecal elastase (FE) sample collected had become pancreatic sufficient (PS; FE >200μg/g). Since 2022, modulator therapy has been extended to children aged over 1 or 2 years, depending on genotype. Aim: To assess the sustained effects of modulator therapy on pancreatic function in children who became PS and to determine whether earlier modulator initiation increases PS r...
records.why_it_matters: Real-world pediatric data show pancreatic recovery can occur years after modulator start, while partial recovery may create vitamin excess risk.
records.confidence_reason: Structured pediatric review reports baseline PI cohort, fecal elastase categories, sustained sufficiency, and vitamin dose reductions.

records.title: Lumacaftor/ivacaftor at 1 year of age and improved pancreatic function
records.summary: Background: In the UK the licence for prescribing lumacaftor/Ivacaftor (Orkambi®) was extended to children over 1 year with a diagnosis of cystic fibrosis homozygous for F508del in early 2023 (Rayment AM J Respir Crit Care Med 2022). Our large tertiary paediatric centre and shared care network routinely offer initiation of Orkambi® to eligible patients age one year, prior to initiation of ETI at two years. Aim: We aimed to look at trends in pancreatic function in children with pancreatic insufficiency in the first year of life who were commenced on Orkambi® prior to age two years. Methods: Patients born between January 2021 and June 2024 were identified from our patient cohort and information on age at initiat...
records.why_it_matters: Starting lumacaftor/ivacaftor around age one may preserve or restore pancreatic function before ETI eligibility in some children.
records.confidence_reason: Structured pediatric network review reports early Orkambi initiation, fecal elastase testing, pancreatic sufficiency, and PERT discontinuation.

records.title: Service evaluation of a virtual ‘vitamin club’ for optimising fat-soluble vitamin management in children with cystic fibrosis
records.summary: Background: Annual assessments at our paediatric cystic fibrosis (CF) centre include serum vitamins A, D and E to guide supplementation. With widespread CFTR modulator use (such as elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor [ETI]), studies report elevated fat-soluble vitamin levels, especially vitamin A [1], reinforcing the need for monitoring. A fortnightly virtual ‘vitamin club’ was introduced to streamline result follow-up, comprising of a specialist paediatric respiratory dietitian and prescribing pharmacist. This multidisciplinary approach aimed to improve efficiency, reduce delays in decision making, and ensure consistent specialist-led care. Aim: To evaluate the ‘vitamin club’ by quantifying reviewed results, abn...
records.primary_theme: Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
records.why_it_matters: A virtual vitamin-club model streamlined pediatric fat-soluble vitamin follow-up and dose changes during widespread modulator use.
records.confidence_reason: Structured service evaluation reports children reviewed, completeness of results, intervention frequency, and medication changes.

records.title: Impact of elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor initiation on dietary intake, pancreatic enzyme replacement therapy and body composition in children aged 2–5 years with cystic fibrosis after 12 months
records.summary: Objective: To evaluate changes in pancreatic enzyme replacement therapy (PERT), dietary intake and body composition in preschool children aged 2–5 years with cystic fibrosis (CF) who initiated modulator therapy with elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor® (ETI), evaluated after 12 months of treatment. Materials and Methods: This was a prospective, single-centre pilot study. Variables analysed before (pre) and after (post) 12 months of ETI included body mass index (BMI) z-score, fat mass percentage, fat-free mass percentage, energy intake, van de Kamer test, fat intake, lipase units (LU) per kg per day and LU per gram of fat consumed. A 3-day dietary record including PERT was collected (nutritional analysis performed...
records.why_it_matters: In preschool children, ETI did not significantly change body composition or intake at 12 months but did alter PERT-to-fat matching.
records.confidence_reason: Structured prospective pilot reports 17 children, paired diet/body composition/enzyme variables, and a significant LU-per-fat change.

records.title: Nutritional status of cystic fibrosis patients in a country without neonatal screening: data from the Republic of Moldova
records.summary: Optimal nutritional status is a key determinant of pulmonary function and survival in cystic fibrosis (CF). Aim: To assess the nutritional status of CF patients followed at the national CF center in the Republic of Moldova (RM). Methods: We performed a retrospective cross-sectional analysis of CF patients registered at the Center for Cystic Fibrosis during the follow-up year 2024. Demographic data, age at diagnosis, anthropometric parameters and pancreatic enzyme replacement therapy use were analyzed descriptively. Results: A total of 60 patients were included, of whom 52 were actively followed. The mean age at follow-up was 14.6 years (range 0.6–38.7), and 29.8% of patients were adults. The mean age at diagno...
records.primary_theme: Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
records.why_it_matters: Marked nutritional compromise in Moldova links delayed diagnosis and limited therapy access to worse growth, emphasizing neonatal screening and comprehensive care needs.
records.confidence_reason: Structured national-center analysis reports follow-up cohort, delayed diagnosis, anthropometric deficits, and resource-limited context.

records.title: Exploring hand grip strength to promote a healthy weight and lifestyle in adults with cystic fibrosis
records.summary: Objectives: Hand grip strength (HGS) is an emerging biomarker associated with overall health status, mortality risk, disability, and nutritional assessment in the general population. Lower HGS has been linked to poorer clinical outcomes in individuals with cystic fibrosis (CF), including increased exacerbations, reduced lung function, lower BMI, and poorer bone health. This project aimed to explore associations between HGS, BMI and lung function, and assess patient acceptability. Secondary aims were to evaluate whether monitoring HGS has a place at annual review assessment, as a tool to motivate adults with cystic fibrosis (CF) to improve physical activity and nutritional status. Methods: 9 adults with CF atte...
secondary_themes: Care burden, adherence, and psychosocial impact
records.why_it_matters: Hand-grip testing may be an acceptable way to start practical conversations about strength, activity, nutrition, and weight management in adult CF clinics.
records.confidence_reason: Structured feasibility project reports only nine adults and no clear correlations, but strong acceptability findings.

records.title: Impact of nutritional quality improvement project: the first 1000 days of life
records.summary: Introduction: Newborn screening for cystic fibrosis (CF) offers an opportunity for early medical and nutritional interventions, potentially leading to improved clinical outcomes. Higher body mass index (BMI) percentiles at two years of age have been strongly associated with improved pulmonary function in later childhood. Epidemiological evidence indicates that enhancing the nutritional status of young children is linked to more favorable pulmonary outcomes. The aim of this study is to investigate the effects of the quality improvement project implemented at our center on children under the age of two who are closely monitored by dietitian. Methods: This is a single center QI project initiated in 2019. CF patie...
records.primary_theme: Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
records.why_it_matters: A first-1000-days nutrition QI program after early diagnosis improved growth percentiles, reinforcing intensive early dietitian follow-up.
records.confidence_reason: Structured QI abstract reports inclusion criteria, monitoring process, six-year cohort, and two-year growth percentile changes.

records.title: Leeds experience of PERT usage in the CFTRm era
records.summary: Objectives: Pancreatic Insufficiency (PI) was previously thought to be irreversible in Cystic Fibrosis (CF), but in the era of Cystic Fibrosis Transmembrane Conductance Regulator modulators (CFTRm), this is now being refuted (1). Our aim was to identify and review the clinical status of children with CF (cwCF) who are PI, on a CFTRm, who have been able to stop Pancreatic Enzyme Replacement Therapy (PERT), in our clinical cohort. Methods: A total of 160 cwCF were reviewed in our clinical cohort. PI was determined with a faecal pancreatic elastase-1 (FE-1) level of <100ug/g stool for moderate PI and <200ug/g stool for severe PI at diagnosis. Medical notes were reviewed for those who had stopped PERT, including g...
records.why_it_matters: Children stopping PERT after CFTR modulators need monitoring that balances fecal elastase results with constipation, symptoms, vitamins, and growth.
records.confidence_reason: Structured cohort review reports 160 children, PERT discontinuation cases, FE status, symptoms, and monitoring recommendations.

records.title: The effect of targeted therapy with CFTR modulators on the relationship between nutritional status, carbohydrate and lipid metabolism in children with cystic fibrosis
records.summary: Relevance: Targeted therapy (TT) has become integral to cystic fibrosis (CF) management by addressing the underlying defect. Alongside its pulmonary benefits, TT may systemically affect metabolic parameters such as glycemic control and lipid profiles, which are common complications impacting prognosis and quality of life in CF. Understanding this metabolic influence is essential for holistic patient care. Materials and methods: A total of 76 children with CF (cwCF) aged 6 to 17 years and 11 months were included in the comparative study. Among them, 39 cwCF (Group 1) received only basic therapy, and 37 were on TT (Group 2). The groups were comparable in terms of age and sex. CwCF in Group 2 had been receiving T...
records.why_it_matters: Targeted therapy improved BMI and HbA1c but raised cholesterol measures, supporting broader metabolic surveillance in treated children.
records.confidence_reason: Structured comparative pediatric study reports therapy groups, metabolic markers, lipid changes, and monitoring conclusion.

records.title: Essential fatty acid imbalance in a paediatric cystic fibrosis population: a single-centre study
records.summary: Introduction/Objectives: Essential fatty acids (EFA) imbalance, characterized by increased levels of arachidonic acid (AA) and decreased levels of docosahexaenoic acids (DHA), has been documented in cystic fibrosis (CF). EFA intestinal absorption is particularly compromised in CF due to pancreatic insuficiency. Despite this detrimental pro-inflammatory profile, DHA suplementation is not widely recommended. This study aimed to evaluate EFA levels in a paediatric CF population and look for associations with clinical features. Materials and Methods: Observational, prospective, single-centre study evaluating paediatric patients with CF. Data collected included serum levels of AA, EPA and DHA, inflammatory markers,...
records.why_it_matters: DHA deficiency was uncommon but associated with hepatobiliary involvement, suggesting targeted fatty-acid research may be most relevant in specific subgroups.
records.confidence_reason: Structured prospective pediatric study reports EFA levels, pancreatic and hepatobiliary status, and significant DHA-CFHBI association.

records.title: Early development profile in children with cystic fibrosis: findings of the ages and stages questionnaires inventory and affecting factors
records.summary: Objective: Cystic fibrosis (CF) is a multisystemic disease where malnutrition, recurrent infections, and frequent hospitalizations may negatively impact child development. The Ages and Stages Questionnaires (ASQ) are a reliable screening tool designed to evaluate communication, gross motor, fine motor, problem-solving, and personal-social domains. This study aims to examines developmental characteristics of children with CF using the ASQ inventory and investigates relationship with developmental scores and demographic, anthropometric, and clinical data. Method: Files of 20 CF patients evaluated at Developmental Pediatrics outpatient clinic between January 2019 and July 2025 were reviewed. ASQ domain scores, de...
records.primary_theme: Care burden, adherence, and psychosocial impact
records.why_it_matters: Developmental screening found delays linked to maternal education, daycare, and vitamin D, supporting early multidisciplinary monitoring beyond lung and GI outcomes.
records.confidence_reason: Structured pediatric chart review reports ASQ domains, clinical predictors, regression findings, and intervention implications.

records.title: Do pancreatic enzyme replacement therapy doses reduce in young children with cystic fibrosis post-elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor? A single-centre experience
records.summary: Objectives: CFTR modulators may improve or restore pancreatic function in young children with CF (cwCF)(1). There is limited evidence on pancreatic recovery and weaning pancreatic enzyme replacement therapy (PERT). Following our previous work on changes in PERT and faecal elastase-1 (FE-1) in cwCF aged 2-5 years post-elexacaftor/ivacaftor/tezacaftor (ETI) at 3 months, we sought to review changes in PERT through to 1 year. Methods: We retrospectively collected the following data: genotype, FE-1(µg/g), average daily PERT dose, anthropometry and prior modulator (PM) use in cwCF aged 2-5 years at baseline and 3 monthly intervals post-ETI. Pancreatic insufficiency (PI) was classified as (µg/g stool): <100 severe, 1...
records.why_it_matters: Young children on ETI showed lower PERT dosing without growth compromise, but pancreatic recovery was limited and individualized monitoring remains necessary.
records.confidence_reason: Structured sections report cohort size, serial PERT and fecal elastase follow-up, and a clear 1-year conclusion.

records.title: Change in fat soluble vitamin status in children with cystic fibrosis aged 2-5 years after initiation of elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor.
records.summary: Objectives: CFTR modulators may improve Fat Soluble Vitamin (FSV) absorption with risk of high levels (1). We investigated change in serum FSV levels A, D and E, and CF specific supplementation in a real world population of young children with Cystic Fibrosis (cwCF) after initiation of Elexacaftor/Tezacaftor/Ivacaftor (ETI). Methods: Retrospective data review was performed in pancreatic insufficient patients aged 2-5 years at baseline, 3, 6 and 12-months post ETI initiation. Routine non-fasting serum FSV levels taken when clinically stable were analysed against local reference ranges. Data collected also included genotype, prior modulator (PM) use and CF specific FSV supplementation dosing at baseline and 12-m...
records.why_it_matters: ETI may change vitamin absorption in preschool children, making dose reduction and early fat-soluble vitamin monitoring clinically relevant.
records.confidence_reason: Structured abstract provides methods, serial vitamin results, supplementation context, and practice-oriented conclusions.

records.title: Changes in vitamin levels in cystic fibrosis patients receiving CFTR modulator therapy
records.summary: Objectives: CFTR modulators improve pancreatic function and may increase fat-soluble vitamin levels in cystic fibrosis (CF). This study aimed to evaluate changes in vitamin levels in patients receiving CFTR modulator therapy. Methods: CF patients receiving modulator therapy for ≥3 months were retrospectively analyzed. Vitamin levels, BMI Z-score, sweat chloride, pulmonary function, fecal elastase, and bone mineral density (BMD) were assessed at baseline, 3 and 12 months. Results: Twenty-seven patients (66% male, mean age 12.2±5.2 years) were included. Nineteen patients (70%) received elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor + ivacaftor and the remaining patients received ivacaftor. BMI Z-score improved from −1.19±0.19...
records.why_it_matters: Vitamin A rose after modulator therapy, reinforcing the need to reassess routine supplementation as pancreatic and nutritional status change.
records.confidence_reason: Structured sections describe a modulator-treated cohort, repeated nutritional markers, and specific vitamin findings.

records.title: Body composition in adult cystic fibrosis patients, dynamics under targeted therapy.
records.summary: Objectives: To compare body composition in CF patients receiving standard and pathogenetic therapy. Methods: The study included 64 CF patients over 18 years of age; 40 of them received targeted therapy. Results: In the targeted therapy group (TTG), men had a BMI of 21.4 kg/m2 (15-32.8 kg/m2), with BMI below 22 kg/m2 = 68%; women had a BMI of 19 kg/m2 (16.6-24.8 kg/m2), with BMI below 22 kg/m2 = 81%. 52% of men had low FFM (fat-free mass) values; 24% of women had a FFM deficiency. %FM (the ratio of body fat to body weight) was normal in 58% of men, exceeded normal range in 32%, and was low in 10%. Among women, normal %FM values were observed in 62% of female patients, exceeded the norm in 33%, and low values we...
records.why_it_matters: Body composition differences under targeted therapy point toward metabolic risk that BMI alone may miss in adults with CF.
records.confidence_reason: Structured sections compare adult body composition measures by targeted therapy status and state dietary implications.

records.title: Improvement in nutritional status in Bulgaria: a success story of the ECFS Twinning project
records.summary: The ECFS twinning project gives an opportunity for mentee centers to gain experience and to have methodical support from the mentor CF centers. The CF centers of Rotterdam and Sofia were twinned, and visited each other in Oct 2024 and May 2025. Since November 2024 there have been regular multidisciplinary case discussions. There is a marked difference between the BMI’s of both centers (0.23 vs. -0.7 for children and 24 vs. 20.49 for adults). Since 2021 CFTR modulators (CFTRm) was introduced in Bulgaria for pwCF aged > 6 years of age. Aim: One of the goals of our twinning project was to improve the nutritional status in the people with CF (pwCF) in Sofia. Methods: For this purpose valuable recommendations and s...
records.primary_theme: Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
records.why_it_matters: The twinning project frames multidisciplinary mentorship as a route to improve nutritional care in a setting with historically lower BMI.
records.confidence_reason: Only a truncated summary is available, with limited detail on recommendations or measured post-intervention outcomes.

records.title: NutriActe: evaluation of the self-management of fat-soluble vitamin levels by a nutritionist at a paediatric cystic fibrosis clinic in Quebec, Canada.
records.summary: Objectives: Cystic fibrosis (CF) often causes pancreatic insufficiency and malabsorption of fat-soluble vitamins. Monitoring levels is vital for growth and bone health, yet results may take as much as 15 days, requiring later follow-up. This study evaluates the impact of systematic review by a nutritionist to adjust vitamin supplements at a pediatric CF clinic. Since 2019, the newly updated activities for nutritionists in Québec in 2019 includes the ability to prescribe vitamins and minerals. Methods: We reviewed pediatric CF charts for vitamins A, D, and E levels during pre- (PRE) (June 2018–May 2019) and post-implementation period (POST) (June 2024–May 2025), excluding the pandemic period. The primary object...
records.primary_theme: Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
records.why_it_matters: Expanded nutritionist authority sharply shortened vitamin-result follow-up, showing how scope-of-practice changes can improve routine pediatric CF care.
records.confidence_reason: Structured sections include pre/post periods, patient counts, turnaround outcomes, and the implementation context.

records.title: Is sweat test a reliable marker for adjusting sodium supplementation in CF patients treated with modulators?
records.summary: In the era of CFTR modulators, optimizing sodium intake in patients with cystic fibrosis (pwCF) remains a major clinical challenge. Excessive supplementation may cause long-term complications, while discontinuation increases the risk of dehydration. Sweat test (ST), historically used for diagnosis, varies across individuals and genotypes and could potentially guide salt intake. This study aimed to assess whether ST correlates with tools used to estimate sodium requirements in these patients. Methods: We conducted a retrospective analysis of sodium intake, urinary ionograms, and ST measurements in adult and pediatric patients followed at the Montpellier CF Reference Center. Correlation analyses, as well as sens...
records.why_it_matters: Using sweat testing to guide sodium supplementation could reduce both dehydration risk and unnecessary supplementation in the modulator era.
records.confidence_reason: The summary states the rationale and retrospective design but is truncated before correlation findings.

records.title: Effects of CFTR modulator therapy on pancreatic, and nutritional outcomes in adults with cystic fibrosis: a single-center study
records.summary: Elexacaftor/Tezacaftor/Ivacaftor (ETI) is a highly effective CFTR modulator for patients with cystic fibrosis (CF) carrying the F508del variant. However, data on extra-pulmonary outcomes in patients without this variant remain limited. This study aimed to confirm the beneficial effects of ETI in our cohort, which included 30% of patients without the F508del variant. Methods: We conducted a single-center observational study in adult CF patients (≥18 years) treated with ETI for at least 12 months at Lille University Hospital, France. All patients carried at least one ETI-responsive mutation. Data of annual report on respiratory function, nutritional status, body composition (assessed by bioimpedance analysis), p...
records.why_it_matters: Adult extra-pulmonary ETI outcomes, including pancreatic and nutritional measures, remain important for patients beyond classic F508del groups.
records.confidence_reason: The available summary gives the aim and design but is truncated before results and conclusions.

records.title: Triangulation of findings from patients and healthcare professionals on challenges in eating behaviours and evaluation of potential utility of mindful eating practices in supporting healthy eating.
records.summary: Objectives: To explore how patients and Health care professionals (HCP) understand and experience challenges in providing and receiving eating behaviours support. To explore barriers and facilitators to using mindful eating (ME) practices to support healthy eating behaviours. Methods: Triangulation was conducted across four data sources: patient evaluation of ME practices after a 5-day intervention (n=10), patient interviews (n=5), HCP ME workshops/focus groups (n=37), HCP ME practices evaluation (n=13). Each dataset was analysed independently and then compared using a triangulation matrix to identify convergence, complementarity, dissonance and silence. These comparisons were used to develop integrated meta-t...
records.primary_theme: Care burden, adherence, and psychosocial impact
secondary_themes: Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
records.why_it_matters: Legacy CF eating advice can complicate modern weight management, so dietary support needs both behavioral and emotional components.
records.confidence_reason: Structured sections triangulate patient and professional data and identify convergent barriers and training needs.

records.title: Audit of magnesium levels in UK paediatric cystic fibrosis patients: assessing the clinical value of routine testing
records.summary: Introduction: The prevalence and clinical significance of hypomagnesemia in paediatric cystic fibrosis (CF) patients remains unclear. There is variation in CF guidelines regarding recommendation for routine Magnesium (Mg) monitoring, raising questions about its value in clinical practice. Furthermore, the impact of the introduction of Highly Effective Modulator Therapy (HEMT) on Mg monitoring requires consideration. This audit aims to evaluate Mg levels in paediatric CF patients and determine if there is clinical justification for routine testing of patient’s Mg levels. Method: We conducted a retrospective audit of all Mg measurements taken from paediatric CF patients at Alder Hey Children’s Hospital over a 10...
records.why_it_matters: Routine magnesium testing is being questioned as pediatric care changes under highly effective modulators.
records.confidence_reason: The summary provides audit rationale and methods but is truncated before findings.

records.title: Eye-movement desensitisation and reprocessing as a treatment for medical trauma in cystic fibrosis
records.summary: Objectives: Medical traumatic stress refers to the psychological and physiological responses to pain, injury, serious illness, medical procedures and invasive or frightening treatment experiences. It is associated with decreased medication adherence and impaired relationships with the clinical team. People living with cystic fibrosis face a lifetime of illness related experiences that have the potential to be traumatic. Eye-Movement Desensitisation and Reprocessing (EMDR) is a widely used evidenced based therapy in the treatment of Post-Traumatic Stress Disorder and other anxiety based conditions. This presentation aims to explore the utility of EMDR as a treatment for symptoms of medical trauma in adults livi...
records.primary_theme: Care burden, adherence, and psychosocial impact
secondary_themes: Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
records.why_it_matters: EMDR may help adults with CF manage medical trauma that otherwise undermines care engagement and self-advocacy.
records.confidence_reason: Structured sections describe the intervention, outcome measures, symptom changes, and acceptability feedback.

records.title: Mental health and neurocognitive effects in children with cystic fibrosis taking elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor: A 12-month follow-up
records.summary: Objectives: Elexacaftor/Tezacaftor/Ivacaftor (ETI) therapy was extended to children with CF aged 6–11 years in October 2022. ETI is associated with clinical improvements and enhanced health-related quality of life. While potential mental health side effects have been reported in adults, data in pediatric populations remain limited. Methods: Children eligible for ETI and their parents completed longitudinal assessments during routine visits at baseline (T0), 3-mos (T1), 6-mos (T2), 12-mos (T3) after ETI. Measures: a) physical parameters (FEV₁, BMI); b) HRQoL (CFQ-R); c) mental health (PSC-17); d) parental depression (PHQ-9) and anxiety (GAD-7); and e) ad hoc neurocognitive side-effects questionnaire. Results: T...
secondary_themes: Care burden, adherence, and psychosocial impact
records.why_it_matters: Pediatric ETI monitoring should include behavior, sleep, and attention effects, not only lung and growth outcomes.
records.confidence_reason: Structured longitudinal data report repeated child, parent, mental health, and neurocognitive measures over 12 months.

records.title: Medical traumatic stress in cystic fibrosis: national survey of provider knowledge, barriers and clinical practice
records.summary: Objectives: People with cystic fibrosis (PWCF) face a lifetime of potentially traumatic, illness-related experiences including medical visits, lab tests and invasive procedures (PICC lines, surgeries). These can cause symptoms of trauma and medical traumatic stress (MTS), such as negative mood, anxiety, and hyperarousal which may lead to decreased adherence, avoidance of procedures, and worse mental health (MH). This study assessed knowledge, training, clinical practices and barriers to addressing MTS in a national US sample of CF providers. records.title: The effect of cognitive behavioural therapy in cystic fibrosis: a systematic review
records.summary: Objectives: Cystic fibrosis (CF), in addition to its multisystem involvement and increased life expectancy, is associated with psychological problems such as depression, anxiety, and difficulties coping with chronic illness. In line with The ECFS’s commitment to develop the next generation of CF researchers, we set up an Early Career Writers’ Forum (ECWF), a programme for early career (EC) researchers to expand their writing and presenting skills with the support of each other and the guidance of established researchers. records.title: Rebuilding a patient led cystic fibrosis community through a patient led focus group
records.summary: Objective: Our primary objective was to rebuild a Cystic Fibrosis (CF) community in a safe and sustainable way, giving people with CF (PwCF) the chance to lead conversations and shape their own care. We wanted to create a group where patients felt heard, valued, and connected and improve the experience of care and patient outcomes for PwCF. Methods: Patients were approached to be part of the CF patient focus group through face-to-face interaction and multiple social media and patient network platforms, to establish a patient representative group for PwCF. records.title: A tale of two cities: evaluating the twinning visit between the Royal Brompton Hospital, London, UK and the adult and paediatric cystic fibrosis centres in Lviv, Ukraine
records.summary: Objective: The ECFS Twinning Programme partners experienced & emerging sites to optimise CF care at the emerging site. Royal Brompton Hospital, London, & adult/paediatric CF centres in Lviv, Ukraine are twinned. Due to the war in Ukraine, the London team could not visit Lviv, so Lviv teams visited London. Here we evaluate the visit, sharing our findings for those interested in the programme. Methods: Surveys shared via Qualtrics, interviews conducted/transcribed in Teams. Interview transcripts & survey free-text coded in NVivo. Content analysis used to interpret themes. Results: Dec25-Jan26; 29 surveys(7 Lviv,22 London) & 4 interviews(1 Lviv,3 London); 4 themes. records.title: Treatment adherence and mental health in people with cystic fibrosis and advanced lung disease treated with elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor
records.summary: Objectives: Advanced lung disease (ALD) is a leading cause of mortality in CF. While Elexacaftor/Tezacaftor/Ivacaftor (ETI) has shown significant clinical benefits, little is known about treatment adherence neuropsychological side-effects in the ALD population following ETI. This study evaluated self-reported adherence, health-related quality of life (HRQoL), mental health, and ETI-related side-effects in patients with ALD. Methods: A prospective, cross-sectional study was conducted at the CF Center of Florence, Italy. Participants (N=31, age>12y) with ALD (ppFEV1<40% or transplant referral at baseline) were enrolled from an ETI compassionate use program. After 36m of therapy, patients completed the Treatment...
secondary_themes: Care burden, adherence, and psychosocial impact
records.why_it_matters: People with advanced lung disease may sustain ETI adherence and clinical gains while still needing monitoring for anxiety-linked side effects.

records.title: Patient-provider communication and shared decision-making in CF pain management
records.summary: Objectives: Sixty to 90% of adults with cystic fibrosis (AWCF) experience pain, often chronic in nature. Effective communication between patients and CF care teams is crucial for optimal pain management. Our goal was to learn from AWCF about their pain management experiences through in-depth interviews. Methods: We recruited 14 AWCF experiencing chronic pain via CF Foundation’s Community Voice and conducted semi-structured qualitative interviews about living with CF and pain, including questions about pain management and experiences of shared decision-making. We developed a codebook and conducted thematic analysis. Results: Participants were 23-64 years old; 10 identified as female, 3 male, 1 gender non-binary...
