records.title: The socioeconomic determinants of lung function in children with cystic fibrosis
records.summary: Objectives: Children with cystic fibrosis (CF) from socioeconomically deprived backgrounds have poorer health outcomes, including reduced lung function. This study assessed the relationship between socioeconomic status and lung function among children with CF at a large paediatric CF centre. Methods: Royal Manchester Children’s Hospital (RMCH), a leading tertiary CF centre in northwestern England, cares for 330 paediatric CF patients, including 200 receiving direct treatment and 130 through shared care. records.title: Knowledge level of home healthcare personnel on cystic fibrosis: evaluation before and after training
records.summary: Objectives: Advances in diagnosis and treatment have prolonged the life expectancy of patients with cystic fibrosis (CF). The increasing number of diagnosed cases underscores the importance of primary healthcare providers, including home healthcare personnel, in patient management. To evaluate the knowledge level of home healthcare staff regarding CF and to assess knowledge gain following a structured training session. Methods: A total of 35 healthcare professionals participated in the study. A 22-item questionnaire covering CF characteristics, treatment options, and clinical management was administered before and after a three-hour visual training program. records.title: Demographic and clinical factors associated with depression and anxiety symptoms among adults with cystic fibrosis in Ireland
records.summary: Objectives: While research has established that people with CF (PWCF) show elevated rates of anxiety and depression, gaps remain in understanding factors influencing mental wellbeing in the current era. This study aims to (1) explore associations between mental health and demographic and clinical factors in PWCF in Ireland and (2) develop a predictive model showing factors associated with anxiety and depression in PWCF in Ireland. Methods: Retrospective medical chart reviews were conducted from January 2023 to the present date, with data collection ongoing until April 2026. records.title: Cystic Fibrosis education: what do families want?
records.summary: Objectives: From the time of cystic fibrosis (CF) diagnosis, good quality information and education about CF are vital to support families. We sought to explore how we could improve our approach to information and education provision at the Royal Brompton Hospital paediatric CF centre. Methods: We used a semi-structured interview approach to explore parents’ experiences of information and educational materials about CF, and their ideas for improvements. Families were invited to complete an interview with a member of the CF team in clinic, by phone, or at a home visit. records.title: Medication burden in children and young people with cystic fibrosis following initiation of elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor
records.summary: Objectives: Elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor(ETI) has transformed outcomes in cystic fibrosis (CF), but its impact on long-term medication burden in children and young people(CYP) is incompletely described. We evaluated the extent and safety of deprescribing following ETI initiation in a paediatric CF cohort. Methods: We conducted a retrospective, single-centre observational study of CYP with CF aged 2–18 years who commenced ETI between 2020 and 2024 and received therapy for ≥1 year. The number and type of long-term medications stopped after ETI initiation were recorded. records.title: Evolving patient preferences in cystic fibrosis following introduction of highly effective CFTR modulators
records.summary: Objectives: In 2020, we ran a discrete choice experiment (DCE) that showed people with cystic fibrosis (pwCF) valued reductions in treatment burden, including inhaled therapies and physiotherapy, alongside clinical outcomes. Since then, introduction of highly effective CFTR modulators has changed clinical trajectories and daily treatment experience. This study aimed to reassess patient preferences for clinical and non-clinical treatment outcomes in a UK CF population following introduction of elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor (ETI). records.title: Does socio-economic status impact the efficacy of modulator therapy amongst adults with cystic fibrosis?
records.summary: Objectives: A literature review by McGarry et al. in 2019 concluded that low socio-economic status was associated with increased mortality and worse lung function amongst people with Cystic Fibrosis (CF). The objective of this study is to consider how socio-economic status may impact the efficacy of modulators amongst adults with CF. Method: Delta F508 homozygous patients on a full dose of Elexacaftor/Tezacaftor/Ivacaftor (ETI) were selected from our center. Index of Multiple Deprivation (IMD) scores were obtained, then allocated into two groups. records.title: Adherence, tolerability and clinical response to elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor in adults with cystic fibrosis: real-world findings from Stockholm CF Centre
records.summary: Objectives: To examine real-world adherence, tolerability, and clinical responses to ETI in adult pwCF. Methods: In total, 115 participants aged ≥18 years treated with ETI at Stockholm CF Centre were included from 1 December 2023 to 30 November 2025. Adherence was defined as taking the prescribed ETI dose within two hours of the scheduled time; participants with an adherence score ≥ 0.80 were considered adherent. A study-specific questionnaire was completed during clinical visits (1–6 per participant). Associations between adherence and changes in forced expiratory volume in one second (FEV₁) and weight were analysed using Spearman’s and Pearson’s correlation tests. records.title: Patient-reported outcomes following CFTR modulator therapy in adults with cystic fibrosis: a real-world study
records.summary: Background: CFTR modulators (CFTRm) have transformed CF care. Real-world patient-reported outcomes (PROs) are essential to understand improvements and unmet needs. This study evaluated PROs in adults receiving CFTRm and explored patient perception of effectiveness and drivers of dissatisfaction. Methods: Over a 3-month period, adults attending CF clinics completed a bespoke, non-validated questionnaire capturing PROs. Additional items assessed perceived effectiveness, side effects, adherence, and overall satisfaction. Demographic data and clinical history were recorded. Responses were analysed descriptively. records.summary: CF patients rely on the multidisciplinary team (MDT) for clinical support, advice, and care coordination though both scheduled and unscheduled contacts.. We previously reviewed and presented data on nature of unscheduled contacts in our service at ECFS 2019 (ref P084). This was prior to CF modulator era. records.title: Service evaluation on initiation of CFTR modulators in an adult cystic fibrosis population
records.summary: Introduction: Introduction of CFTR modulators has changed the care of people with cystic fibrosis (pwCF). As treatment options continue to expand, understanding pwCF perspectives on the initiation of these therapies through real-world patient-reported outcomes is key. Therefore, we aimed to evaluate the initiation of CFTR modulators in an adult CF population attending a regional CF centre. Method: A structured questionnaire was developed by CF multidisciplinary (MDT) team and over a three-month period was completed by eligible pwCF during clinic appointments. records.title: Democratizing cystic fibrosis care: effects of elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor on quality of life, depression, and anxiety in patients with cystic fibrosis in a low-income population
records.summary: Objective: To evaluate the impact of elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor (ETI) therapy on quality of life, depression, and anxiety in individuals with cystic fibrosis (CF). Methods: This was a prospective, longitudinal, observational study. Participants were recruited and assessed at baseline (T0) and after 3 (T1), 6 (T2), and 9 (T3) months of ETI use. Outcomes included quality of life (CF Questionnaire–Revised, CFQ-R), depression (Patient Health Questionnaire-9, PHQ-9), and anxiety (Generalized Anxiety Disorder-7, GAD-7). records.title: Perspectives and experiences of people with cystic fibrosis regarding long-acting reversible contraceptives
records.summary: Objectives: Improvements in life expectancy and quality of life among people with CF (PwCF) have led many to consider their reproductive needs and goals. Additionally, CF modulators have improved female fertility and pregnancy rates in the United States (US) have doubled in the modern era. We explored the experiences and decision-making of PwCF related to long-acting reversible contraception (LARC). Methods: We recruited PwCF aged 18-35 years from across the US who had ever used an intrauterine device (IUD) or implant to participate in semi-structured interviews related to LARC initiation, use, and removal. records.title: Improving inclusivity in cystic fibrosis care- medical considerations when treating transgender people with CF
records.summary: Background: In the UK, there are rising numbers of people with gender dysphoria and 0.5% of the population identify as transgender. Transgender people with CF (TpwCF) are becoming more common in paediatric and adult services with little research or guidance available into specific care for them. Many people with gender dysphoria seek help from online or private gender services, rather than their GP or CF medical teams- creating a vacuum of knowledge surrounding key parts of their care. Many health services operate binary sex based coding for all patients, and as such, physiological outcomes and automatic sex-based cancer screening services may become challenging for TpwCF. records.title: Romantic relationships in adults with cystic fibrosis: an interpretative phenomenological study
records.summary: Objectives: Romantic partners are a key source of support for adults with cystic fibrosis, therefore building and sustaining good quality romantic relationships is important. However, the physical and psychological demands of this life-limiting condition can complicate navigation of romantic relationships. Methods: This qualitative study aimed to explore the experience of romantic relationships for adults with cystic fibrosis. Semi-structured interviews were conducted and interpretative phenomenological analysis (IPA) was utilised to analyse the data from seven participants with cystic fibrosis. records.title: Perceptions of women with cystic fibrosis regarding their obstetrical care and healthcare providers' perspectives during the journey to parenthood
records.summary: Objectives: Improved life expectancy and the arrival of CFTR modulators have made motherhood a realistic goal for many women with cystic fibrosis (CF). This study explores the gap between the perceptions of women with CF regarding their maternity care and the perspectives of the healthcare professionals(HCPs) supporting them. Methods: A dual-methodological approach was employed : 1) A descriptive qualitative literature review of PubMed-indexed articles(IMRaD format) from the last five years. 2) Semi-structured interviews conducted with women living with CF and HCPs involved in their care to compare lived experiences with clinical approaches. records.title: Clinical and psychological factors influencing transition readiness in adolescents and young adults with cystic fibrosis
records.summary: Objectives: Advances in cystic fibrosis (CF) care have improved survival rates, leading to an increase in adolescents and young adults (AYAs) needing to transition from paediatric to adult healthcare. Transition readiness is crucial for maintaining continuity of care and optimising long-term health outcomes, yet its psychological and clinical factors in CF remain understudied. Methods: We conducted a cross-sectional observational study of AYAs with CF aged ≥12 years at a single CF centre. Transition readiness was evaluated using the Transition Readiness Assessment Questionnaire (TRAQ 6.0). Depressive and anxiety symptoms were measured with PHQ-9 and GAD-7. records.title: Strong beginnings: peer support for parents of babies and toddlers with CF
records.summary: Objectives: We developed Strong Beginnings so that families have a community of peer support from the start of their journey with CF. We aim to create connections between parents of children aged under 24 months, thereby improving wellbeing and reducing anxiety for parents in the early days of coping with the condition. Methods: We run one online event per month, for parents of children with CF aged up to 24 months. We run informal peer-support sessions, led by parent volunteers. records.title: An exploration of the experiences of childhood cystic fibrosis in family systems
records.summary: Objectives: Whilst treatment for Cystic Fibrosis (CF) has been recently transformed with developments in modulator drugs (De Boeck, 2020), it remains a serious genetic condition (Bowen & Hull, 2015). Moreover, previous research has mainly focused on individuals or dyads, but working together with whole families is key to improving clinical care (Hisert et al., 2023). records.title: Safety, tolerability, and pharmacokinetics of novel NBD1 stabilizers SION-719 and SION-451 from two Phase 1 first-in-human studies
records.summary: Objectives: At least two-thirds of people with CF (pwCF) on standard-of-care (SOC) modulators do not achieve normal CFTR function, defined by sweat chloride levels <30 mmol/L. Further, some do not tolerate these modulators. Therefore, a significant unmet need exists for novel modulators. The F508del mutation directly destabilizes the nucleotide-binding domain 1 (NBD1) of CFTR. Stabilization of NBD1 may be critical to achieving complete correction of the F508del-CFTR defect. None of the approved modulators directly stabilize NBD1. records.summary: Objectives: To examine whether older cystic fibrosis (CF) patients experience inferior outcomes following lung transplant (LT). records.title: Evolving diabetes management and prescribing practices in adults with cystic fibrosis
records.summary: Objectives: While insulin remains the mainstay of CF diabetes (CFD) management, increasing use of non-insulin glucose lowering therapies is emerging in clinical practice. records.summary: Background: DHL is the combination of health literacy and digital competency skills needed to allow patients/care teams to engage with care through digital health tools and online environments. records.title: Finding the right route to the lung: precision gene editing therapy for cystic fibrosis
records.summary: Effective treatments for all individuals with cystic fibrosis (CF) continue to present significant challenges. CRISPR-based technologies, especially base editing, offer a promising avenue towards finding a cure. However, to minimize off-target effects, the components required for base editing must be expressed transiently in the target cells. Delivering these components as ribonucleoprotein (RNPs) complexes via engineered virus-like particles (eVLPs) presents a potentially safer alternative, given their limited lifespan within cells. By utilizing envelope glycoprotein pseudotyping, eVLPs can be customized to specifically target lung epithelial cells, addressing a crucial gap in CF treatment. Objectives: Our go...

records.title: Trends in lung transplantation for cystic fibrosis following introduction of elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor: a United States Registry analysis
records.summary: Objectives: To characterize trends in cystic fibrosis (CF) lung transplantation (LT) following approval of elexacaftor-tezacaftor-ivacaftor (Trikafta). Methods: We identified CF patients ≥18 years undergoing LT from 01/2016-09/2024 in the United Network for Organ Sharing database. Patients were stratified into pre- and post-Trikafta eras (2016-2019 vs. 2020-2024). We compared transplant volumes, transplant centers, recipient characteristics, and post-transplant survival between eras. Temporal trends in transplant volume were evaluated using the Mann-Kendall test. Survival was assessed using Kaplan-Meier analysis and multivariable Cox regression. records.summary: Background: In the context of elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor (ETI) treatment, the anecdotally reported frequency of airway clearance (AC) and chronic inhaled mucoactive therapy (CIMT) has declined with many people with CF (pwCF) stopping altogether, some on their own accord. This study aims to quantify AC and CIMT de-escalation after ETI initiation. Methods: This quality improvement project included all pwCF at our pediatric CF centre who started ETI between December 2019 and September 2025. records.summary: Introduction: The oral glucose tolerance test (OGTT) remains the recommended screening and monitoring test starting the age of 10 years for abnormal glucose tolerance in people with cystic fibrosis (CF). records.title: Expanded use of remote objective monitoring during telehealth visits for people with CF experiencing acute symptoms: the EUROPA study project
records.summary: Background: Acute pulmonary exacerbations (APEs) are often managed by phone without spirometry. This QI project studies a protocolized evaluation and treatment of pwCF with acute respiratory symptoms thru telehealth and remote objective monitoring. Methods: This multi-site QI initiative expands urgent telehealth evaluation. Inclusion criteria were pwCF age 6 years and up. Subjects enrolled received spirometers, scales, and pulse oximeters. A protocol for APE treatment was based on FEV1 and CRISS score at the time of exacerbation. records.summary: Objectives: Health benefits of yoga are widely recognised for the general population and some medical conditions, but evidence of clinical efficacy is limited for CF. Methods: Participants were randomised to usual care or yoga. Spirometry, one-minute sit-to-stand, the Cystic Fibrosis Questionnaire-Revised (CFQ-R) and other questionnaires were collected via video-call pre- and post-intervention (0 and 12 weeks), and after 12-weeks of follow-up (week 24). records.title: Waitlist and lung transplant outcomes by sex for adults with cystic fibrosis: the United States experience
records.summary: Objective: Cystic fibrosis (CF) is a key indication for lung transplantation (LT). Despite medical advances, many adults develop end-stage lung disease and require LT; women with CF experience earlier infections and worse life outcomes. The impact of sex on access to LT and post-LT outcomes is unclear. We aimed to quantify differences in waitlist and post-LT outcomes by sex. Methods: We utilized the United Network for Organ Sharing (UNOS) database, including adults listed for single-organ LT from January 2005 to November 2023. records.summary: Objective: To trial Intrapulmonary Ventilation (IPV); a new airway clearance technique (ACT) device at the All Wales Adult Cystic Fibrosis Centre (AWACF) and gain insight into patient subjective experience using Sentec’s IPV1® device. records.title: Cystic fibrosis is associated with the greatest long-term survival after lung transplantation: 30 years of the Zurich experience​
records.summary: Objectives: Lung transplantation (LTx) is an established therapy for patients with advanced cystic fibrosis (CF), yet robust long-term, single-centre outcome data remain limited. records.title: Resurrecting ancestral actin-resistant DNase for cystic fibrosis lung disease therapy
records.summary: Objectives: Pulmonary symptoms in cystic fibrosis (CF) dramatically impair patient health and quality of life. The FDA-approved mucolytic recombinant human DNase1 (dornase alfa) shows reduced efficacy in many patients due to actin-rich mucus. Next-generation DNase-based therapeutics must combine resistance to actin inhibition, robust catalytic activity, and scalable manufacturing. Simple cells such as E. coli offer ideal production platforms but have so far failed to express soluble, active human DNase1L2, a DNase1 paralog naturally resistant to actin inhibition. records.title: The fastest growing demographic in CF: the life and times of older adults age ≥40 with CF in Canada.
records.summary: Objectives: Once thought of as a pediatric disease, the proportion of adults living with cystic fibrosis (CF) in Canada soared from <10% in the 1970’s to over 65% today. As care improves, people with CF are living longer and healthier lives, but little is known about these older adults. The objectives of this study were to: 1) quantify the population of older adults over time, and 2) describe the cohort of older adults. Methods: This study included all individuals age ≥40 years reported in the Canadian CF Registry between 2015-2024. Objective 2 was restricted to 2024. records.summary: Objectives: There is significant heterogeneity in outcomes of people with cystic fibrosis (pwCF). records.summary: Objectives: Forskolin-induced swelling (FIS) in patient-derived intestinal organoids (PDIOs) is a promising biomarker to predict individual drug response in pwCF. records.title: Pregnancy outcomes in foetal cystic fibrosis
records.summary: Objectives: Cystic fibrosis (CF) is a genetic, multiorgan disorder associated with meconium ileus, reduced birth weight and shorter gestational age. Methods: Data from the Medical Birth Registry of Norway (1967–2021) were linked to the Norwegian CF Registry. records.title: Impact of cystic fibrosis transmembrane conductance regulator modulator therapy on symptom and treatment burden, physical activity and participation: A South African mixed-methods study
records.summary: Objectives: Patient-reported outcomes regarding daily functioning and participation are under-explored relative to clinical outcomes of Cystic Fibrosis Transmembrane Conductance Regulator modulator (CFTRm) therapy, particularly in resource-limited settings. records.title: Early abnormal glucose tolerance is associated with a lower insulinogenic index in children with cystic fibrosis aged 6–11 years
records.summary: Background: Abnormal glucose tolerance (AGT) can develop early in children with cystic fibrosis (CF). records.title: Optimising health: digital programmes driving health and connection in cystic fibrosis
records.summary: Objectives: Optimising Health (OH) is a quality improvement, multidisciplinary team comprising social workers, dietitians, a physiotherapist, and parent partners at a paediatric CF centre (n=325) serving a multistate area in the western USA. Four digital health programmes were developed by the OH team to foster community, improve health literacy, and promote the well-being of children with cystic fibrosis (CwCF) and their families. records.title: Additive effect of combinational treatment with Splisense ASO SPL84 and elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor
records.summary: Background: SPL84 is an inhaled antisense oligonucleotide (ASO), currently being evaluated in an ongoing global Phase 2 study in pwCF with the 3849+10kb C->T (3849) mutation. Interim results indicate that SPL84 is safe, well tolerated, and shows positive preliminary efficacy. SPL84 directly and specifically corrects the underlying splicing defect, restoring wild-type CFTR mRNA and translation of a fully functional CFTR channel. In studies with 3849 patient-derived HBE and HNE cells, SPL84 treatment restored CFTR function to normal levels. records.title: Altered airway microbiome and inflammatory profile in cystic fibrosis-related diabetes
records.summary: Objectives: Cystic fibrosis (CF) is a genetic disorder characterised by progressive lung function decline and chronic airway inflammation. Cystic fibrosis-related diabetes (CFRD) is the most common extrapulmonary complication of CF and is associated with increased morbidity and mortality. Although a strong association between CFRD and impaired lung function has been established, the underlying mechanisms remain poorly understood. records.summary: Background: Home spirometry has become an integral component of CF care following the widespread adoption of telehealth and hybrid care models. records.title: Unsupervised multidimensional phenotyping reveals environmental influences on cystic fibrosis disease trajectories
records.summary: Objectives: Cystic fibrosis (CF) is characterized by marked heterogeneity in disease severity and progression. records.summary: Objectives: Airway clearance treatments (ACT) are essential for maintaining lung health in many people with cystic fibrosis. records.title: From therapeutic breakthrough to professional reconfigurations: The impact of elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor on CF care teams in France
records.summary: Since the introduction of Kaftrio® in France in 2020, the clinical condition of the majority of patients with cystic fibrosis who are eligible for this treatment has improved substantially. This improvement has led to a drastic reduction, or even the discontinuation, of certain forms of care, such as respiratory physiotherapy and courses of antibiotics. How has hospital-based care evolved following the arrival of this new therapy? To address this question, we conducted a sociological study involving healthcare professionals and patients. records.title: Pregnancy management in cystic fibrosis: A survey of UK physiotherapy practice
records.summary: Objective: Cystic fibrosis (CF) pregnancy rates have risen following initiation of highly effective modulator therapy. Current physiotherapy standards of care (SOC) have not been updated to reflect this. Methods: Lead physiotherapists at all 23 UK CF centres were invited to complete a bespoke online questionnaire. Results: Twelve (52%) centres responded. records.summary: Introduction: Cystic fibrosis (CF) lung disease is hallmarked by chronic infection and inflammation. records.title: No country for bystanders: a split and inducible precise adenine base editor for the correction of W1282X
records.summary: W1282X, the 5th most common Cystic Fibrosis causing variant, generates a premature terminating codon (PTC), causing lack of full-length functional CFTR protein production and nonsense-mediated mRNA decay. Adenine base editing (ABE) can in theory correct any PTC mutation with minimal levels of unwanted indels. ABE uses a fusion of a Cas9 nickase and a synthetic TadA enzyme modified to convert Adenine (A) to Guanine (G) in a varying editing window in the spacer region. records.summary: Objectives: As people with cystic fibrosis (CF) live longer, it has become apparent they have an increased risk of developing cancer, at a younger age, especially bowel cancer. records.summary: Background: Regular screening for bronchopulmonary colonisation is standard of care for people with cystic fibrosis (pwCF). records.summary: Background: Pulmonary disease is the main cause of morbidity and mortality in cystic fibrosis (CF), and recovery of lung function relies on efficient repair of airway damage, particularly in advanced disease. records.title: Proteases, GAGs and fibronectin a harmful trio in predisposing the cystic fibrosis airway epithelium infections
records.summary: Structural changes in the CF lungs result from a protease/anti-protease imbalance favouring recurrent respiratory infections by opportunistic bacteria such as Pseudomonas aeruginosa (Pa). Interestingly, it is not fibronectin in its integral form that is responsible for Pa adhesion, but the fragments generated by proteolysis. records.summary: Background: Calprotectin (S100A8/A9) is a major biomarker of CF lung disease, being associated with increased inflammation during exacerbation, predicting future exacerbation in people with CF and improvements with CFTR modulators. records.summary: Objectives: Women with CF have suboptimal use of contraception and a significant rate of unplanned pregnancy. records.title: Clinical-stage NBD1 stabilizers can increase F508del-CFTR protein half-life to wild-type levels when used alone or in combination with other CFTR modulators
records.summary: Objectives: F508del, the most prevalent CF-causing mutation, leads to a loss of F508 within CFTR’s first nucleotide-binding domain (NBD1). This destabilizes NBD1, impairing CFTR folding and weakening NBD1’s interface with CFTR transmembrane domains. In preclinical studies, combining suppressor mutations that stabilize NBD1 with others that improve CFTR assembly has been shown to correct F508del to wild-type (WT) levels. Without NBD1 stabilization, correction of F508del was significantly reduced. Approved modulators have significantly improved outcomes for people with CF (pwCF), but do not restore normal CFTR function in two-thirds of patients. No approved modulator directly stabilizes NBD1. With the goal of fu...
records.primary_theme: Precision CFTR biology and rare variants
secondary_themes: Emerging genetic, RNA, and cell-model therapies
records.why_it_matters: NBD1 stabilizers may address an important limitation of current F508del correction by improving mature CFTR protein stability.
records.confidence_reason: Structured preclinical abstract reports mutation rationale, compounds, pulse-chase assay, and half-life results.

records.title: Bronchiectasis regression after one year treatment with elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor
records.summary: Objectives: Elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor (ETI) has transformed cystic fibrosis (CF) care, with significant improvements observed across multiple clinical outcomes. However, reports of its potential effect on structural lung disease, including bronchiectasis (Bx) and bronchial wall thickening (BWT), remain sparse. We assess ETI’s impact on Bx and BWT among people with CF (pwCF) in Denmark, hypothesising that ETI reduces Bx and BWT severity as evaluated by high-resolution computed tomography (HRCT). Methods: One baseline and one follow-up HRCT scan were included for each pwCF. All scans were performed during clinician-assessed clinical stability and evaluated for structural lung abnormalities using automatic...
records.session_title: Workshop 16 - Further optimising measures of cystic fibrosis pulmonary disease
records.why_it_matters: Automated HRCT analysis suggests ETI can reduce bronchial widening and wall thickening, although larger bronchiectasis may persist.
records.confidence_reason: Structured observational abstract reports scan numbers, age mix, timing, imaging metrics, statistical changes, and conclusion.

records.title: Pseudomonas aeruginosa adaptation under elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor therapy and clinical implications
records.summary: Objectives: Elexacaftor/Tezacaftor/Ivacaftor (ETI) provides clinical benefits for people with Cystic Fibrosis (pwCF), although treatment response varies, possibly due to lung microbiological features. We investigated Pa adaptation to ETI by integrating genomic, transcriptomic, phenotypic, and clinical data to identify adaptive traits and explain outcome variability. Methods: Fifty-four clonal Pa isolates were collected from 11 pwCF before and up to 28 months after ETI initiation. Phenotypic traits associated with acute and chronic infection were analyzed. Whole-genome and RNA sequencing of 35 isolates tracked within-host evolution. Six isolates were exposed in vitro to ETI to assess possible direct impacts. Cl...
records.session_title: Workshop 17 - Airway microbiology in the CFTR modulator era: pathogen dynamics, adaptation and the changing microbiome
records.why_it_matters: Pseudomonas can persist under ETI through strain-specific adaptation, supporting ongoing pathogen monitoring despite clinical improvement.
records.confidence_reason: Structured abstract integrates clinical isolates, genomics, transcriptomics, phenotypes, in vitro exposure, and clinical outcomes.

records.title: Positive outcome for mother and baby in a patient with cystic fibrosis colonised with Burkholderia cenocepacia in the era of CFTR modulators
records.summary: Introduction: Burkholderia cenocepacia (Bcc) in people with cystic fibrosis (pwCF), can increase mortality. CFTR modulators have changed the course of CF, with women living in better health increasingly pursuing pregnancy. Literature on pregnancy in pwCF and Bcc is limited. A case series, published by Tanser et al. (2000), reported 4 deaths in pregnancy; 3 colonised with Bcc. A case report by Ciaverell et al. (2025), describes a successful pregnancy in pwCF with Bcc on CFTR modulators complicated by pre-term labour. Case: A 34-year-old pwCF first colonised with Bcc in 2017 elected to have a child by IVF and surrogacy in 2020, prior to CFTR modulators, due to uncertain pregnancy outcomes in pwCF and Bcc. In Nov...

records.title: A randomised 28-day, double-blind cross-over study to evaluate the safety, efficacy & pharmacokinetics of ETD001, an inhaled ENaC blocker, in people with cystic fibrosis
records.summary: Objectives: ETD001 is a blocker of the epithelial sodium channel (ENaC) that is predicted to show a long duration of activity in the airways following inhalation (1). This study is the first to administer ETD001 to people with cystic fibrosis (pwCF), and was designed to be run in two parts: Parts A and B. In both parts only pwCF ≥18 years with ppFEV1 ≥ 40% and ≤ 90% of normal and who were not taking CFTR modulator therapy were included. Methods and results: Part A enabled a first evaluation of the safety, tolerability and PK of repeat doses of ETD001 over 7 days in pwCF. ETD001 (4.5 mg) was delivered as an inhaled aerosol generated by a Pari eFlow nebuliser, over 2-3 minutes, twice daily (BiD). Part A enrolled...

records.title: ​Respiratory abnormalities in subjects referred for congenital absence of the vas deferens and carrying two CFTR mutations
records.summary: Objectives: The majority of patients with congenital absence of the vas deferens (CAVD) have two mutations in the CFTR gene and are diagnosed with a CFTR-RD. CAVD has been associated with mild respiratory disease assessed by spirometry and physical evaluation. The objective of this study is to characterize respiratory abnormalities on CT scans in subjects with CAVD carrying two CFTR mutations. Method: We conducted a single-center retrospective study of 122 subjects referred to CAVD assessment for which two CFTR mutations were identified from 2002 to 2025 (next-generation sequencing). They underwent a systematic evaluation of CFTR-related organ dysfunction (medical history, sweat test, chest CT scans, spirometr...

secondary_themes: Precision CFTR biology and rare variants

records.title: Microbial and host functional signatures in three distinct pulmotypes revealed by metaproteomics during elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor therapy in patients with cystic fibrosis
records.summary: Background: Elexacaftor/Tezacaftor/Ivacaftor (ETI) therapy has shown clinical benefit for numerous patients with cystic fibrosis (pwCF). While its impact on the airway microbiome is increasingly investigated, the influence of the microbiome on the therapy effectiveness and its potential prognostic value for clinical outcome, remain poorly understood. Methods: Sputum samples from 54 pwCF were collected at different time points. Samples underwent metaproteomic analysis to characterize the dynamic of the microbiome profile. Metaproteomic data were integrated with clinical parameters, including lung function (ppFEV1) microbiology and antibiotic use. Results: ETI therapy was associated with significant increase in...

records.title: Addressing disengagement and health decline: what are we missing?!
records.summary: Background: In our large adult cystic fibrosis (CF) centre, we adhere to national guidelines on the management of CF. However, "standard CF care" does not suit all patients. We have a cohort of patients that deteriorate rapidly despite receiving standard CF MDT care. Typical patient characteristics include poor adherence, unstable lung function, low or falling BMI, frequent exacerbations and reliance on frequent rescue intravenous (IV) antibiotics, frequent non attendance at appointments, psychological and/or social difficulties and difficulty communicating honestly with the MDT. We have created a bespoke outpatient service to engage such patients and support adherence called BOOST (Better Outpatient Outcome S...

records.title: SPL5AC, MUC5AC lowering ASO for cystic fibrosis and other muco-obstructive diseases
records.summary: Background: Airway mucus is a key defense barrier and is primarily composed of the mucins MUC5AC and MUC5B. In cystic fibrosis (CF), impaired CFTR function causes airway dehydration and increased mucin concentration. In addition, goblet cells and glandular hyperplasia in CF and other muco-obstructive diseases (asthma, COPD, NCFB) drives mucin overproduction. An elevated MUC5AC-to-MUC5B ratio in patients leads to thick, obstructive mucus and chronic infection and inflammation, with higher MUC5AC levels and mucus plugging correlating with increased disease severity. Splisense is developing an antisense oligonucleotide (ASO) to specifically reduce MUC5AC, aiming to improve mucus clearance and relieve airway obstr...
records.primary_theme: Emerging genetic, RNA, and cell-model therapies
records.why_it_matters: A MUC5AC-lowering ASO aims to reduce mucus plugging and inflammation across CF and other muco-obstructive diseases.
records.confidence_reason: Structured preclinical abstract reports in vitro and mouse-model testing, RNA and protein effects, mucus outcomes, and development plans.

records.title: Using free-breathing 3D magnetic resonance imaging to assess proximal airway dynamics in health and cystic fibrosis
records.summary: Objectives: To quantify static and dynamic airway morphology using 1H-MRI in healthy volunteers (HV) and people with Cystic Fibrosis (CF). Method: 3D Ultrashort Echo-Time (UTE) MRI of the lungs was acquired during tidal breathing and reconstructed at both end-inspiration (EIVT) and end-expiration (FRC). Synopsys’ Simpleware™ and a custom python script were used to segment and quantify the size and shape of visible airways. The ellipse axis ratio (EAR) quantified airway shape (EAR=1 represents a circle and 0 a line). The lumen cross-sectional area (CSA) quantified size. To account for differences in patient size, the CSA of each airway was normalised by the CSA of the trachea and the dynamic change in CSA was f...
records.session_title: Workshop 16 - Further optimising measures of cystic fibrosis pulmonary disease
records.why_it_matters: Free-breathing 3D MRI may quantify dynamic proximal airway abnormalities without relying only on static CT-like measures.
records.confidence_reason: Structured imaging abstract reports MRI acquisition, segmentation measures, participant characteristics, and CF versus healthy findings.

records.summary: Objective: Investigate the association of baseline structural lung disease with changes to Pseudomonas aeruginosa (Pa) isolation frequency following elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor (ETI) in people with chronic Pa infection. records.title: Challenging adult cystic fibrosis cases with chronic kidney disease
records.summary: Background: Although hepatic side effects of modulator drugs used in cystic fibrosis (CF) treatment are known, renal side effects have not been clearly described. Dosage adjustment of modulator drugs based on glomerular filtration rate(GFR) has not been defined. Here we share the clinical outcomes of our patients with low GFRs who are on modulators. Discussion: Patient 1: A 35-year-old male, diagnosed CF at 6 months. His mutations were F508del and 2789+5G>A that are eligible for Trikafta that he is on since May 2024. In 2007 during follow-ups, proteinuria was detected and a kidney biopsy revealed focal segmental glomerulosclerosis. Until 2023, he was not a on dialysis routinely, but occasionally presented with...

records.title: Gamma secretase inhibition restores histologic and functional parameters of primary airway cultures of cystic fibrosis donors
records.summary: Cystic fibrosis (CF) airway pathology is marked by the presence of hypertrophic goblet cells as faithfully modeled in air-liquid interface (ALI) primary cultures from CF donors, exhibiting functional deficits in mucociliary clearance (reduced air surface liquid and particle transport velocity). The Notch pathway has been implicated in goblet cell hyperplasia and can be targeted with gamma-secretase inhibitors (GSIs), which abrogate its activation. We evaluated the activity of multiple GSIs for correction of those phenotypic defects. Recombinant HEK293 cell cultures expressing reporter constructs for amyloid precursor protein (APP) or Notch were treated with 5 GSIs: DAPT, avagacestat, semagacestat, MK-0572, and...

secondary_themes: Emerging genetic, RNA, and cell-model therapies

records.title: Real world study of sweat chloride and lung clearance index evolution in children with cystic fibrosis treated by elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor with two versus one responsive CFTR variant
records.summary: Objectives: Clinical and functional response to elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor (ETI) is heterogeneous in children with cystic fibrosis (CF). We analyzed if the number of ETI-responsive CFTR variant could predict sweat chloride concentration (SCC) and lung clearance index (LCI) evolution in children treated with ETI, using real-world data. Methods: SCC and LCI evolution were collected in children with CF in our local pediatric center before and during their first year of treatment by ETI. Data included children without F508del treated by ETI off-label thanks to the French compassionate program. CFTR variant responsiveness to ETI was determined using the program’s classification. Results: Among 201 children in...

records.title: Longitudinal effects of elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor therapy on the airway metagenome in children with cystic fibrosis aged 6-11 years
records.summary: Objectives: Elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor (ETI) has significantly improved quality of life for people with cystic fibrosis (pwCF). As shown in culture- and sequencing-based studies of pwCF 12 years and older, ETI decreases the abundance of typical CF pathogens in the airways. For three years, ETI has also been available for children with CF aged 6-11 years. To investigate the impact of early ETI treatment on the airway microbiome our study employs shotgun metagenomics for high resolution identification of bacteria, fungi and DNA viruses. Methods: In this prospective post-approval study, cough swabs from n=15 cwCF aged 6-11 years at baseline, 3 months and 24 months after initiation of ETI therapy were collec...

records.title: Pancreatic pain resolved by elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor, psychologic complications resolved by vanzacaftor/tezacaftor/deutivacaftor - 12year-old patient genotype N1303K/3849+10kbC>T - A case report
records.summary: Background: Psychologic side effects in people with CF (pwCF) receiving elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor (ETI) are rare, albeit they have been reported. Recently, the new triple therapy with vanzacaftor/tezacaftor/deutivacaftor (VTD) was approved for pwCF with at least one non-class-1-mutation. It is highly relevant to see whether similar VTD-related neuropsychological effects may emerge. Discussion: We report on a 12-year-old male pwCF with primary exocrine pancreatic sufficiency carrying both class-2 and class-5 CFTR mutations. themes.name: Service delivery, access, and multidisciplinary care
Care burden, adherence, and psychosocial impact
Precision CFTR biology and rare variants
Emerging genetic, RNA, and cell-model therapies

findings.title: The modulator era is shifting ECFS attention from efficacy alone to optimization, burden, and long-term monitoring.
findings.rationale: Record-level synthesis groups modulator-linked studies with nutrition, infection, adherence, and service implications, suggesting the strategic question is now how to manage durable post-modulator care.

findings.title: Infection and antimicrobial strategy remain a major constraint despite therapeutic progress.
findings.rationale: Records inferred as infection-focused cluster around Pseudomonas, resistance, microbiology, and exacerbation risk, indicating persistent pathogen biology remains central to CF outcomes.

findings.title: Precision CFTR biology is increasingly tied to access decisions for rare or difficult genotypes.
findings.rationale: Rare variant, theratyping, organoid, and functional-assay records are inferred as strategically important because they can change eligibility and confidence in treatment response.

findings.title: Gene/RNA therapy, organoids, and digital methods form a smaller but high-signal emerging-science layer.
findings.rationale: The emerging-modality signal captures records where the strategic value is translational optionality rather than current standard-of-care volume.

findings.title: Screening, biomarkers, and quantitative monitoring are moving CF management toward earlier stratification.
findings.rationale: Diagnostic and biomarker records connect newborn screening, sweat chloride, genotype, imaging, and lung-function measures to earlier or more individualized decisions.

findings.title: Treatment burden, adherence, and psychosocial impact remain practical determinants of benefit.
findings.rationale: Care-burden inference links patient-reported outcomes, caregiver burden, adherence, and mental health to the real-world effectiveness of modern CF care.

findings.title: Registries and real-world cohorts are becoming a strategic evidence layer for post-modulator decisions.
findings.rationale: Real-world evidence records are strategically useful for durability, safety, access, and subgroup questions that individual trials cannot fully answer.

findings.title: Pediatric and lifespan-care questions shape endpoints, services, and monitoring strategies.
findings.rationale: Population inference identifies pediatric, adult, older-adult, pregnancy, and advanced-disease records that signal CF care is increasingly organized around lifespan-specific needs.
